description: Implement SymH type -- generalized symmetrized expression container that can represent arbitrary Young-tableau symmetry patterns. Fields: indices (the slots), symmetry_pattern (Young diagram or group generators), sign_convention. Must support: construction from Young diagram, from explicit symmetrizer/antisymmetrizer lists, equality testing, representation dimension. Integrate with existing Young tableaux code in src/algebra/young.jl. Add to src/invariants/symh.jl. Reference: xAct/SymManipulator. Test: construct SymH for Riemann symmetry (pair + cyclic + antisymmetric), verify it reproduces known symmetry dimension. ~100 LOC.
title: SymManipulator: Implement SymH type

description: Implement canonical form for SymH expressions using group theory. Given a tensor expression with SymH symmetry, reduce to canonical form by: (1) applying the symmetry group to generate all equivalent forms, (2) selecting the canonical representative (e.g., lexicographically smallest), (3) computing the coefficient (from the Young symmetrizer normalization). Must use the existing xperm infrastructure for permutation canonicalization. Add to src/invariants/symh.jl. Reference: xAct/SymManipulator. Test: symmetrize R_{abcd} with full SymH and verify it matches (anti)symmetrization via Young projector. ~100 LOC.
title: SymManipulator: Implement SymH canonicalization

description: Implement product, sum, and contraction operations for SymH expressions. Product: tensor product of two SymH expressions with combined symmetry (Littlewood-Richardson rule for Young diagrams). Sum: add SymH expressions with compatible index structure. Contraction: contract indices between SymH expressions, projecting the result onto the appropriate symmetry. Must handle: (a) product of two Riemann-symmetry tensors, (b) trace (contraction of symmetric pair), (c) wedge product as special case. Add to src/invariants/symh.jl. Reference: xAct/SymManipulator. Test: product of two SymH Riemann tensors decomposes correctly via Littlewood-Richardson. ~100 LOC.
title: SymManipulator: Implement SymH arithmetic

description: Extend TensorGR.jl's PSALTer-style particle spectrum analysis from rank-2 symmetric (graviton) to arbitrary tensor fields: vectors A_μ, antisymmetric rank-2 B_{μν}, rank-3 fields, and multi-field actions. Implement Moore-Penrose propagator, unitarity conditions, and source constraints. Ground truth: Barker, Marzo, Sherrill (2024), arXiv:2406.09500. Code lives in src/action/.
title: PSALTer Extension: Arbitrary Tensor Field Spin Projectors

description:
Design how to extend spin_project for vector fields A_μ, antisymmetric B_{μν}, rank-3 fields. Key insight: each field type has different spin content. Vector: spin-1 ⊕ spin-0. Antisymmetric rank-2: spin-1 ⊕ spin-0. Rank-3: depends on symmetry. Document projector algebra for each case. ~100 LOC design doc in notes.

Ground truth: Barker, Marzo, Sherrill (2024), arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 2-3.

Acceptance: Design document specifying projector definitions for vector, antisymmetric-2, and rank-3 fields. API surface for multi-field kernel extraction.
design:
# Multi-Field PSALTer Design for TensorGR.jl

## 1. Mathematical Foundation

### 1.1 Spin Content by Tensor Type

In d-dimensional flat space, any tensor field decomposes under SO(d-1) into
irreducible spin sectors. The spin projectors are built from the same two
fundamental 2-index operators already in TensorGR:

    theta_{mu nu} = eta_{mu nu} - k_mu k_nu / k^2   (transverse)
    omega_{mu nu} = k_mu k_nu / k^2                  (longitudinal)

satisfying theta + omega = eta, theta^2 = theta, omega^2 = omega, theta*omega = 0.

**Symmetric rank-2 h_{(mu nu)}** (EXISTING, 10 DOF in d=4):
  - spin-2: 5 DOF  (TT part)
  - spin-1: 4 DOF  (vector-longitudinal mixing)
  - spin-0-s: 1 DOF (transverse trace, scalar)
  - spin-0-w: 0 DOF (pure longitudinal trace)
  Already implemented: spin2_projector, spin1_projector, spin0s_projector, spin0w_projector.

**Vector A_mu** (4 DOF in d=4):
  - spin-1: (d-1) DOF, projector = theta_{mu nu}
  - spin-0: 1 DOF, projector = omega_{mu nu}
  Completeness: theta + omega = eta_{mu nu} = identity on vectors.

**Antisymmetric rank-2 B_{[mu nu]}** (6 DOF in d=4):
  - spin-1: (d-1)(d-2)/2 DOF = 3 in d=4
  - spin-0: d-1 DOF = 3 in d=4
  Projectors (2-index on each side, total 4 free indices):
    P^(1)_{mu nu, rho sigma} = (1/2)(theta_{mu rho} theta_{nu sigma}
                                    - theta_{mu sigma} theta_{nu rho})
    P^(0)_{mu nu, rho sigma} = (1/2)(theta_{mu rho} omega_{nu sigma}
                                    - theta_{mu sigma} omega_{nu rho}
                                    + omega_{mu rho} theta_{nu sigma}
                                    - omega_{mu sigma} theta_{nu rho})
                              + omega_{mu rho} omega_{nu sigma}
                              - omega_{mu sigma} omega_{nu rho}
  Note: No spin-2 for antisymmetric tensors (the TT symmetric part is absent).

**Symmetric rank-3 C_{(mu nu rho)}** (d=4: 20 DOF):
  Uses theta^(3) and omega^(3) chains. The decomposition yields spin-3,
  spin-2, spin-1, spin-0 sectors. Formulas involve (d-1) and (d-2)
  dimensional prefactors.

**General rank-n with Young symmetry**: The spin content depends on the
Young tableau. Each row of length l contributes spin <= l, and the
projectors are products of symmetrized/antisymmetrized theta/omega chains.
This is the most general case and requires the Young tableaux machinery
already in src/algebra/young.jl.

Reference: Barker, Marzo, Sherrill (2024), arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 2-3.

### 1.2 Projector Algebra (all tensor types)

For any tensor type T with spin sectors {J_1, ..., J_N}:

(A) Idempotence:   P^(J_i) * P^(J_i) = P^(J_i)
(B) Orthogonality: P^(J_i) * P^(J_j) = 0  for i != j
(C) Completeness:  sum_i P^(J_i) = I_T  (identity on the tensor space)

where I_T is the symmetry-compatible identity:
  - Symmetric rank-2: I_{(ab)(cd)} = (1/2)(eta_{ac} eta_{bd} + eta_{ad} eta_{bc})
  - Antisymmetric rank-2: I_{[ab][cd]} = (1/2)(eta_{ac} eta_{bd} - eta_{ad} eta_{bc})
  - Vector: I_{a,b} = eta_{ab}

Transfer operators T^(sw), T^(ws) (existing for symmetric rank-2 spin-0
mixing) generalize to any pair of spin-0 sectors that can mix. For vectors
and antisymmetric tensors, there are no transfer operators (each spin-0
sector is unique).

### 1.3 Multi-Field Systems

When multiple fields appear in a coupled Lagrangian (e.g., h_{mu nu} + A_mu
or h_{mu nu} + B_{[mu nu]}), the kinetic kernel becomes a block matrix:

    K = | K_{hh}  K_{hA} |
        | K_{Ah}  K_{AA} |

Each block K_{XY} has indices from field X on the left and field Y on the
right. Spin projection acts block-by-block:

    K^J = | Tr(K_{hh} P^J_h)    Tr(K_{hA} M^J_{hA}) |
          | Tr(K_{Ah} M^J_{Ah})  Tr(K_{AA} P^J_A)   |

where P^J_X is the spin-J projector for field X, and M^J_{XY} is a
"mixing operator" (or "transition map") that maps from the spin-J sector
of field Y to the spin-J sector of field X. The mixing operators are
NON-TRIVIAL only when X and Y share spin content (e.g., h and A both have
spin-1 and spin-0). They are constructed from theta/omega chains with
appropriate index structure to bridge the rank difference.

Key mixing operators (h_{(mu nu)} <-> A_mu):
  - spin-1: M^(1)_{(mu nu), rho} = (1/sqrt(2))(theta_{mu rho} k_nu + theta_{nu rho} k_mu) / k
  - spin-0: M^(0)_{(mu nu), rho} = (1/sqrt(d-1)) theta_{mu nu} k_rho / k
            (and the omega variant for 0-w)

Key mixing operators (h_{(mu nu)} <-> B_{[mu nu]}):
  - spin-1: M^(1)_{(mu nu), [rho sigma]} involves theta/omega products with
    appropriate symmetrization/antisymmetrization.

The propagator is then obtained by inverting the full block matrix K^J in
each spin sector separately:
    G^J = (K^J)^{-1}

Unitarity conditions (no-ghost, no-tachyon) are checked per spin sector:
  - No ghost: residue of G^J at each pole has correct sign
  - No tachyon: all poles are at k^2 >= 0 (timelike mass)


## 2. Type Design

### 2.1 FieldType (New)

A descriptor for the tensor structure of a field, determining its spin content:

    struct FieldType
        name::Symbol           # e.g., :h, :A, :B
        rank::Int              # total number of indices
        symmetry::Symbol       # :symmetric, :antisymmetric, :mixed, :none
        spin_content::Vector{Symbol}  # e.g., [:spin2, :spin1, :spin0s, :spin0w]
    end

Predefined constructors:
    symmetric_rank2_field(:h)     -> FieldType(:h, 2, :symmetric, [:spin2, :spin1, :spin0s, :spin0w])
    vector_field(:A)              -> FieldType(:A, 1, :none, [:spin1, :spin0])
    antisymmetric_rank2_field(:B) -> FieldType(:B, 2, :antisymmetric, [:spin1, :spin0])
    scalar_field(:phi)            -> FieldType(:phi, 0, :none, [:spin0])

For rank-3 and higher, spin_content is computed from the Young tableau
using existing machinery in src/algebra/young.jl.

### 2.2 SpinProjectorSet (New)

Bundles all projectors for a given field type:

    struct SpinProjectorSet
        field_type::FieldType
        projectors::Dict{Symbol, Function}   # :spin2 => (indices...) -> TensorExpr
        transfer_ops::Dict{Tuple{Symbol,Symbol}, Function}  # (:spin0s,:spin0w) => fn
        identity::Function                   # symmetry-compatible identity
        dim::Int
    end

Factory functions:
    spin_projectors(ft::FieldType; dim=4, metric=:g, k_name=:k, k_sq=:k2)
        -> SpinProjectorSet

This returns prebuilt projector functions. For symmetric rank-2 fields,
this wraps the existing spin2_projector etc. For vectors and antisymmetric
tensors, new implementations (see Sec 3).

### 2.3 MultiFieldKernel (New)

Extends KineticKernel to multiple fields:

    struct MultiFieldKernel
        fields::Vector{FieldType}
        blocks::Dict{Tuple{Symbol,Symbol}, KineticKernel}
        # blocks[(:h,:h)] = diagonal kernel for h
        # blocks[(:h,:A)] = off-diagonal kernel for h-A mixing
    end

Constructed from a multi-field Lagrangian:

    extract_multifield_kernel(expr, [ft_h, ft_A]; registry=reg)

This decomposes each bilinear term into (field_X_left, field_Y_right, coefficient)
and groups them into the block structure.

### 2.4 SpinSectorMatrix (New)

The spin-projected kernel in a given sector:

    struct SpinSectorMatrix
        spin::Symbol
        fields::Vector{Symbol}    # ordered field labels
        matrix::Matrix{Any}       # N_fields x N_fields scalar entries
    end

    spin_project_multi(K::MultiFieldKernel, spin::Symbol;
                       projector_sets::Dict{Symbol, SpinProjectorSet},
                       mixing_ops=nothing, ...) -> SpinSectorMatrix

### 2.5 ParticleSpectrum (New)

The full spectrum analysis result:

    struct ParticleSpectrum
        sectors::Dict{Symbol, SpinSectorMatrix}  # :spin2 => 1x1, :spin1 => 2x2, etc.
        propagators::Dict{Symbol, Matrix{Any}}    # inverted sector matrices
        poles::Dict{Symbol, Vector{Any}}          # mass poles per sector
        ghost_check::Dict{Symbol, Bool}           # residue sign checks
        tachyon_check::Dict{Symbol, Bool}         # pole location checks
    end

    particle_spectrum(K::MultiFieldKernel; dim=4, ...) -> ParticleSpectrum


## 3. API Design

### 3.1 Vector Field Projectors (TGR-4zw.2)

New functions in src/action/spin_projectors.jl:

    # Vector spin-1 projector: P^(1)_{mu,nu} = theta_{mu nu}
    vector_spin1_projector(mu, nu; kwargs...) -> TensorExpr

    # Vector spin-0 projector: P^(0)_{mu,nu} = omega_{mu nu}
    vector_spin0_projector(mu, nu; kwargs...) -> TensorExpr

    # Completeness: P^(1) + P^(0) = eta
    # Idempotence: P^(J) * P^(J) = P^(J)
    # These are trivially satisfied since theta and omega already satisfy them.

### 3.2 Antisymmetric Rank-2 Projectors (TGR-4zw.3)

New functions in src/action/spin_projectors.jl:

    # Antisymmetric spin-1: P^(1)_{[mu nu],[rho sigma]}
    antisym_spin1_projector(mu, nu, rho, sigma; kwargs...) -> TensorExpr

    # Antisymmetric spin-0: P^(0)_{[mu nu],[rho sigma]}
    antisym_spin0_projector(mu, nu, rho, sigma; kwargs...) -> TensorExpr

### 3.3 Multi-Field Kernel Extraction (TGR-4zw.5)

    extract_multifield_kernel(expr, field_types::Vector{FieldType};
                              registry=current_registry()) -> MultiFieldKernel

    # Generalized spin projection for multi-field systems
    spin_project_multi(K::MultiFieldKernel, spin::Symbol; ...) -> SpinSectorMatrix

### 3.4 Spectrum Analysis (TGR-4zw.6, 4zw.7, 4zw.8)

    # Moore-Penrose propagator (handles gauge-degenerate sectors)
    moore_penrose_propagator(S::SpinSectorMatrix) -> Matrix{Any}

    # Unitarity conditions
    no_ghost_condition(spectrum::ParticleSpectrum, spin::Symbol) -> Bool
    no_tachyon_condition(spectrum::ParticleSpectrum, spin::Symbol) -> Bool

    # Source constraints from gauge invariance
    source_constraints(K::MultiFieldKernel; gauge_symmetries=...) -> Vector{TensorExpr}


## 4. Integration with Existing Code

### 4.1 KineticKernel Generalization

The existing KineticKernel stores bilinear terms as
(coeff, left::Vector{TIndex}, right::Vector{TIndex}).
This structure already supports arbitrary tensor ranks since left/right
are variable-length index lists.

The key change is that spin_project must dispatch on the number of indices:
  - length(left) == 2: use existing rank-2 projectors
  - length(left) == 1: use new vector projectors
  - length(left) == 2 with antisymmetry: use antisymmetric projectors

This dispatch is handled by the FieldType metadata.

### 4.2 Backward Compatibility

All existing API is preserved. The new types and functions are purely additive:
  - spin_project(K::KineticKernel, spin) continues to work for single-field rank-2
  - New multi-field functions are separate entry points
  - SpinProjectorSet for symmetric rank-2 wraps existing projector functions

### 4.3 QuadraticForm Integration

The existing QuadraticForm (scalar matrix of momentum-dependent entries) is the
output of spin projection in each sector. SpinSectorMatrix is essentially a
QuadraticForm with spin-sector metadata. The existing sym_det, sym_inv, propagator
functions work directly on the .matrix field.

### 4.4 extract_kernel_direct Extension

The existing _unwrap_field_chain and _extract_bilinear_direct work for any
tensor rank (they track derivative indices and field indices generically).
The only extension needed is tracking which FieldType each bilinear factor
belongs to, so the correct projector is selected.

### 4.5 SVT Connection

The SVT decomposition (src/svt/) performs a conceptually similar decomposition
in position space using spatial projectors. The PSALTer projectors operate in
momentum space on Lorentz-covariant fields. The two approaches give the same
physical spectrum but through different routes:
  - SVT: gauge-fix first, then decompose into scalar/vector/tensor sectors
  - PSALTer: project first, then analyze each spin sector

The existing svt_quadratic_forms_6deriv demonstrates the SVT approach.
The PSALTer approach via spin_project_multi provides an independent cross-check.


## 5. Implementation Plan (maps to child issues)

### Phase 1: Single-field projectors
  TGR-4zw.2: Vector projectors (vector_spin1_projector, vector_spin0_projector)
             ~30 LOC in spin_projectors.jl, ~50 LOC tests
  TGR-4zw.3: Antisymmetric rank-2 projectors
             ~60 LOC in spin_projectors.jl, ~80 LOC tests
  TGR-4zw.4: Rank-3 projectors (symmetric and hook)
             ~120 LOC, depends on Young tableaux

### Phase 2: Multi-field infrastructure
  TGR-4zw.5: FieldType, MultiFieldKernel, extract_multifield_kernel,
             spin_project_multi, mixing operators
             ~200 LOC new file src/action/multifield_kernel.jl

### Phase 3: Propagator and spectrum
  TGR-4zw.6: Moore-Penrose propagator (extends sym_inv for degenerate matrices)
             ~80 LOC in quadratic_action.jl
  TGR-4zw.7: Unitarity conditions (no_ghost_condition, no_tachyon_condition)
             ~100 LOC new file src/action/unitarity.jl
  TGR-4zw.8: Source constraints from gauge invariance
             ~80 LOC new file src/action/source_constraints.jl

### Phase 4: Validation
  TGR-4zw.9:  Maxwell theory A_mu -> massless spin-1, no spin-0
  TGR-4zw.10: Proca theory A_mu -> massive spin-1, decoupled spin-0
  TGR-4zw.11: Fierz-Pauli unitarity (existing FP tests extended)

Total estimated: ~750 LOC source, ~500 LOC tests.


## 6. Ground Truth / Validation Targets

From Barker, Marzo, Sherrill (2024), arXiv:2406.09500:

### Maxwell (Sec 4.1):
  L = -(1/4) F_{mu nu} F^{mu nu} = -(1/2) partial_mu A_nu (partial^mu A^nu - partial^nu A^mu)
  Kernel: K^(1) = k^2 (transverse, massless spin-1)
          K^(0) = 0   (gauge mode, no propagation)
  Propagator: G^(1) = theta/k^2, gauge mode is pure gauge.

### Proca (Sec 4.2):
  L = -(1/4) F^2 + (1/2) m^2 A^2
  Kernel: K^(1) = k^2 - m^2, K^(0) = -m^2
  Propagator: (theta/(k^2-m^2)) + (omega/m^2), massive spin-1 with 3 DOF.
  No-ghost: residue(K^(1)) > 0. No-tachyon: m^2 > 0.

### Fierz-Pauli (Sec 4.3, existing):
  Spin-2: f_2 = (5/2)k^2, Spin-0s: f_0 = -k^2.
  Already verified in test_kernel_extraction.jl.

### Einstein-Proca (Sec 5):
  Coupled h_{mu nu} + A_mu. The spin-1 sector becomes a 2x2 matrix mixing
  the vector part of h with the transverse A. This is the key multi-field
  validation target.
title: Research: Design multi-field PSALTer for arbitrary tensor spin projectors

description:
Validate PSALTer pipeline for Proca theory L = -¼F_{μν}F^{μν} + ½m²A_μA^μ. Expected results:
- Kernel: k² θ_{μν} + m² η_{μν} = (k²+m²)θ_{μν} + m² ω_{μν}
- Propagator: θ_{μν}/(k²+m²) + ω_{μν}/m²
- Spectrum: 3 massive spin-1 modes (mass m), no spin-0 propagation issue
- No ghosts if m² > 0, no tachyons if m² > 0

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 8.2. STRING MATCH on spectrum.

Acceptance: End-to-end test. Verify 3 polarizations (d-1=3 in d=4), mass m, unitarity.
title: PSALTer Validation: Proca theory spectrum

description:
Extend existing Fierz-Pauli spin-2 validation to explicitly verify unitarity conditions using the new framework. Already have spin-2 = 5 modes and spin-0 = 1 mode from bench_12. Now verify:
- Moore-Penrose propagator matches known result
- No-ghost condition for spin-2 sector
- Spin-0 ghost identification (Boulware-Deser ghost at m=0 limit)
- Source constraint: ∂_μ T^{μν} = 0, T^μ_μ = 0 (transverse-traceless)

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 8.3. STRING MATCH on unitarity.

Acceptance: Full unitarity report matching PSALTer Table 1 for Fierz-Pauli.
title: PSALTer Validation: Fierz-Pauli unitarity conditions

description:
Implement spin projectors for vector field A_μ. Two projectors:
- P^(1)_{μν} = θ_{μν} = η_{μν} - k_μ k_ν / k² (transverse, spin-1)
- P^(0)_{μν} = ω_{μν} = k_μ k_ν / k² (longitudinal, spin-0)

Completeness: P^(1) + P^(0) = η. Orthogonality: P^(i) P^(j) = δ^{ij} P^(i). Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 3.1.

Acceptance: vector_spin1_projector(k) and vector_spin0_projector(k) functions. Verify completeness and orthogonality. Verify Proca theory gives spin-1 mass m.
title: PSALTer: Implement vector field spin projectors (spin-1, spin-0)

description:
Implement spin projectors for antisymmetric rank-2 field B_{μν} = -B_{νμ}. In d=4, spin content is 1 ⊕ 0. Build projectors from θ_{μν} and ω_{μν} (vector projectors) contracted with antisymmetric structure. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 3.2.

Acceptance: antisym2_spin1_projector and antisym2_spin0_projector functions. Verify completeness and orthogonality on antisymmetric subspace. Verify Kalb-Ramond B-field spectrum.
title: PSALTer: Implement antisymmetric rank-2 field spin projectors

description:
Implement spin projectors for general rank-3 tensor fields with various symmetry types (totally symmetric, hook symmetry, totally antisymmetric). Each symmetry class has different spin decomposition. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 3.3.

Acceptance: rank3_projectors(symmetry_type) returning complete set of orthogonal projectors. Verify completeness for each symmetry class.
title: PSALTer: Implement rank-3 field spin projectors

description:
Extend extract_kernel for actions with multiple fields. For an action S[h_{μν}, A_μ, φ, ...], the kernel is a matrix K^{IJ}_{...} where I,J index field species. Cross-terms (e.g., h_{μν}φ mixing) appear as off-diagonal blocks. Generalize extract_kernel_direct to handle multi-field bilinears. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 4.

Acceptance: extract_kernel_multi(expr, [field1, field2, ...]) returning matrix of kernels. Verify diagonal blocks match single-field extraction. Verify cross-terms for scalar-graviton mixing.
title: PSALTer: Implement multi-field kernel extraction

description:
Implement Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of the kinetic matrix to compute the propagator, handling gauge zero modes. For gauge theories, the kinetic matrix is singular (det=0 in gauge directions). The Moore-Penrose inverse gives the physical propagator on the gauge-invariant subspace. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 5.

Acceptance: moore_penrose_propagator(kernel_matrix) function. Verify: for Maxwell, returns transverse propagator 1/k². For linearized GR, returns TT propagator.
title: PSALTer: Implement Moore-Penrose propagator

description:
Implement unitarity analysis from propagator poles. Two conditions per spin-J sector:
- No-ghost: residue of propagator pole is positive (correct sign kinetic term)
- No-tachyon: pole location m² > 0 (or m² = 0 for massless)

Extract pole structure from Moore-Penrose propagator. Report per-sector results. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 6.

Acceptance: unitarity_conditions(propagator) returning Dict{SpinSector, (ghost_free::Bool, tachyon_free::Bool, mass_squared)}. Verify for Fierz-Pauli: spin-2 healthy, spin-0 ghost at m=0.
title: PSALTer: Implement unitarity conditions (no-ghost, no-tachyon)

description:
Implement extraction of source constraints from gauge invariance. Gauge symmetry of the action implies constraints on source currents: transversality (∂_μ J^μ = 0), tracelessness (T^μ_μ = 0), etc. These follow from the null space of the kinetic matrix. Add to src/action/.

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 7.

Acceptance: source_constraints(kernel_matrix) returning list of constraint equations on source tensors. Verify: Maxwell gives ∂_μ J^μ = 0. Linearized GR gives ∂_μ T^{μν} = 0.
title: PSALTer: Implement source constraints from gauge invariance

description:
Validate PSALTer pipeline for Maxwell theory L = -¼F_{μν}F^{μν} with F_{μν} = ∂_μ A_ν - ∂_ν A_μ. Expected results:
- Kernel: k² θ_{μν} (transverse projector only)
- Propagator: θ_{μν}/k² (massless transverse)
- Spectrum: 1 massless spin-1 mode, no spin-0 propagating mode
- No ghosts, no tachyons
- Source constraint: ∂_μ J^μ = 0

Ground truth: PSALTer arXiv:2406.09500, Sec 8.1. STRING MATCH on spectrum.

Acceptance: End-to-end test from Lagrangian to spectrum. All results match ground truth.
title: PSALTer Validation: Maxwell theory spectrum

description: degeneracy_conditions() uses g_tensor_name() which returns Symbols, not numeric values. When Horndeski is embedded as DHOST via horndeski_as_dhost(), the conditions should evaluate to zero but symbolic arithmetic cannot reduce them. Needs CAS integration (Symbolics.jl) or a numeric evaluation path. 9 tests are @test_broken in test_dhost_degeneracy.jl.
title: Fix DHOST degeneracy conditions for symbolic Horndeski-as-DHOST path

description: Register the Weyl spinor Psi_{ABCD}: totally symmetric in all 4 undotted spinor indices, representing the self-dual part of the Weyl tensor. 5 independent complex components (Psi_0...Psi_4). Register with TensorRegistry: name=:Psi, 4 undotted down-spinor indices, total symmetry group S_4. Also register conjugate Psi_bar_{A'B'C'D'} (4 dotted indices, totally symmetric). ~60 LOC in src/spinors/curvature_spinors.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 4.6.41. Test: (1) Psi_{ABCD} = Psi_{BACD} after simplify (total symmetry), (2) Psi_{ABCD} has 5 independent components (verify via Young tableau count), (3) Psi_{ABCD} epsilon^{AD} = 0 (trace-free in spinor sense after full symmetrization).
title: Spinors: Register Weyl spinor Psi_{ABCD}

description: Implement the GHP commutator relations: [thorn, thorn'], [thorn, edth], [thorn, edth'], [thorn', edth], [thorn', edth'], [edth, edth']. These express commutators of GHP operators in terms of curvature (Weyl scalars, Ricci scalars) and spin coefficients of definite GHP type. Each commutator acting on a type {p,q} quantity gives specific curvature terms. ~80 LOC in src/spinors/ghp_equations.jl. Reference: GHP (1973) Eq 3.2 (6 commutator equations). Test: (1) STRING-MATCH each commutator against GHP paper, (2) verify GHP type consistency of each commutator equation, (3) in vacuum, verify commutators reduce correctly.
title: GHP: Implement GHP commutator relations

description: Implement the unifying EFT of dark energy parametrization that encompasses all single-field scalar-tensor theories on FRW backgrounds. Parameters: alpha_M (running Planck mass), alpha_K (kineticity), alpha_B (braiding), alpha_T (tensor speed excess), alpha_H (beyond-Horndeski). Express alpha functions in terms of Horndeski/GLPV/DHOST Lagrangian functions. Extends existing Bellini-Sawicki (alpha_M, alpha_K, alpha_B, alpha_T) by adding alpha_H. Function: eftofde_alphas(lagrangian; registry) -> Dict of alpha functions. ~80 LOC in src/scalar_tensor/eftofde.jl. Reference: Gleyzes et al, JCAP 1308 (2013) 025, arXiv:1304.4840, Sec 3-4; Bellini & Sawicki, JCAP 1407 (2014) 050, arXiv:1404.3713. Test: GR limit (f=M_Pl^2/2, all others zero) gives alpha_M = alpha_K = alpha_B = alpha_T = alpha_H = 0, matching Bellini & Sawicki Eq 4.1.
title: EFTofDE: Implement effective field theory of dark energy alpha parametrization

close_reason: Implemented with MP Secs IV-V, 163 tests
description:
Implement the Regge-Wheeler-Zerilli decomposition of the metric perturbation h_{ab} on a Schwarzschild background into even-parity (Zerilli) and odd-parity (Regge-Wheeler) sectors, expressed in tensor spherical harmonics.

The metric perturbation decomposes as (Martel & Poisson, PRD 71, 104003, Eqs 5.1-5.2):

  h^{even}_{ab} = diag(H_0 f, H_2/f) Y_{lm} + [0, h_1; h_1, 0] Y^A_{lm} + [r^2 K Y_{lm} Omega_{AB} + r^2 G Z^{AB}_{lm}]
  h^{odd}_{ab}  = [0, h_0; -h_0, 0] X^A_{lm} + h_2 X^{AB}_{lm}

where f = 1 - 2M/r, and {H_0, H_2, h_1, K, G} are even-parity radial functions, {h_0, h_2} are odd-parity radial functions.

Infrastructure needed:
- `SchwarzschildPerturbation` struct holding M, radial coefficient symbols, parity, (l,m)
- `schwarzschild_decompose(h, l, m, parity)` -> builds the TensorExpr from tensor harmonics
- Must work with existing `EvenTensorHarmonicY`, `EvenTensorHarmonicZ`, `OddTensorHarmonic`, `EvenVectorHarmonic`, `OddVectorHarmonic` (all in src/harmonics/)
- Register the 7 radial coefficient tensors (H_0, H_2, h_1, K, G, h_0, h_2) with appropriate Schwarzschild manifold properties

File: src/perturbation/schwarzschild_decompose.jl (~120 LOC)
Reference: Martel & Poisson, PRD 71, 104003 (2005), Sec V; Regge & Wheeler, PR 108, 1063 (1957); Zerilli, PRD 2, 2141 (1970).
Test: Even-parity decomposition at l=0 reduces to only H_0, H_2, K (G and h_1 vanish). Odd-parity decomposition at l=0,1 vanishes identically (tensor harmonics X^{AB} require l >= 2).
title: BH-Pert2: Implement Regge-Wheeler-Zerilli metric decomposition on Schwarzschild background

description: Tensorial extension of Invar for expressions with free (uncontracted) indices, equivalent to xAct/TInvar. Extends cyclic, Bianchi, and dimensionally-dependent reductions to tensorial Riemann monomials. Reference: xAct/TInvar documentation; Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, arXiv:0706.2209.
title: Invar Epic 4: TInvar (Tensorial Invariant Canonicalization)

description: Research and design the tensorial extension of Invar for expressions with free (uncontracted) indices. Key challenge: cyclic and Bianchi identities generate relations between tensorial expressions, not just scalars. Design decisions: (1) how to represent a tensorial Riemann monomial canonically (generalization of RInv with free index slots), (2) how to enumerate independent tensorial monomials at given degree and index structure, (3) how the free-index canonicalization interacts with existing xperm. Output: design document. Reference: xAct/TInvar documentation; the tensorial case is harder because the symmetry group acts on both contracted and free indices. ~100 LOC for prototype.
title: TInvar: Research tensorial invariant canonicalization design

description: Implement canonicalization of tensorial Riemann monomials -- expressions like R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ef} (with free indices a,b,e,f). Must use xperm canonical_perm with the free indices treated as distinguishable labels. The canonical form must be independent of the original dummy index names but preserve free index structure. Algorithm: (1) partition indices into free and dummy, (2) canonicalize dummy contractions via xperm, (3) normalize free index positions. Add to src/invariants/tinvar.jl. Ground truth: xAct/TInvar documentation. Test: R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ef} and R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ef} with different dummy names give same canonical form. ~100 LOC.
title: TInvar: Implement tensorial Riemann monomial canonicalization

description: Implement cyclic identity reduction for tensorial Riemann expressions (R_{a[bcd]}=0 with free indices). When the cyclic identity is applied to a Riemann factor in a tensorial monomial, it produces relations between tensorial monomials with the same free index structure. Algorithm: for each Riemann factor, apply R_{abcd}+R_{acdb}+R_{adbc}=0, re-canonicalize, collect relations. Add to src/invariants/tinvar.jl. Ground truth: xAct/TInvar. Test: R_{abcd}+R_{acdb}+R_{adbc}=0 holds as tensorial identity. ~100 LOC.
title: TInvar: Implement tensorial cyclic reduction

description: Implement second Bianchi identity reduction for tensorial Riemann expressions with covariant derivatives. The identity nabla_{[a}R_{bc]de}=0 with free indices generates relations between tensorial differential Riemann monomials. Must handle: nabla acting on Riemann with various index structures, contracted and free derivative indices. Add to src/invariants/tinvar.jl. Ground truth: xAct/TInvar. Test: nabla_a R_{bcde} + nabla_b R_{cade} + nabla_c R_{abde} = 0. ~100 LOC.
title: TInvar: Implement tensorial Bianchi reduction

description: Implement dimensionally-dependent identity (DDI) reduction for tensorial Riemann expressions. In dimension d, the (d+1)-antisymmetrized Kronecker delta contracted with tensorial Riemann monomials gives zero. For the tensorial case, the free indices participate in the antisymmetrization, giving relations that do not exist in the scalar case. Algorithm: construct delta^{[a1...a_{d+1}]}_{[b1...b_{d+1}]} with some slots filled by free indices, contract with Riemann monomials, expand. Add to src/invariants/tinvar.jl. Ground truth: Lovelock (1971); Harvey (1995). Test: in d=4, tensorial DDI applied to Riemann with 2 free indices gives a relation. ~100 LOC.
title: TInvar: Implement tensorial DDI reduction

description: Implement tinvar_simplify(expr; registry=reg, dim=nothing) -- top-level function for simplifying tensorial Riemann expressions with free indices. Applies tensorial canonicalization, cyclic reduction, Bianchi reduction, and DDI reduction in sequence. Returns the expression in terms of independent tensorial monomials. Add to src/invariants/tinvar.jl. Ground truth: xAct/TInvar. Test: simplify R_{a}^{bcd}R_{bcd}^{e} to known canonical form in terms of Ricci and Riemann. ~100 LOC.
title: TInvar: Implement TInvarSimplify top-level function

description: Implement the SL(2,C) spin metric epsilon_{AB} and its inverse epsilon^{AB}. Properties: antisymmetric (epsilon_{AB} = -epsilon_{BA}), epsilon_{01} = 1, epsilon^{AB} epsilon_{BC} = delta^A_C. Register with TensorRegistry following register_tensor! pattern. Add symmetry generators for antisymmetry. ~80 LOC in src/spinors/spin_metric.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 2.5.24. Test: (1) epsilon_{AB} = -epsilon_{BA} after simplify, (2) epsilon^{AC} epsilon_{CB} simplifies to delta^A_B, (3) string match antisymmetry identity.
title: Spinors: Implement spin metric epsilon_{AB}

description: Extend the Butler-Portugal canonicalization (xperm.c FFI) to handle spinor index symmetries. Dotted and undotted spinor indices must canonicalize independently (they live in different index groups). Key changes: (1) _build_permutation in canonicalize.jl must separate spinor index slots by conjugation type, (2) dummy spinor pairs must be identified correctly (A contracted with A, not A with A'), (3) free spinor indices sorted: undotted before dotted. ~100 LOC modifying src/algebra/canonicalize.jl + src/xperm/wrapper.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Sec 2.5 (index conventions). Test: (1) Psi_{ABCD} = Psi_{(ABCD)} after canonicalize, (2) X_{AB} Y^{BA} canonicalizes to -X_{AB} Y^{AB}, (3) mixed tensor-spinor indices handled.
title: Spinors: Extend xperm canonicalization for spinor indices

close_reason: Added spin_project_mss and mss_form_factors to kernel_extraction.jl. Uses Vandermonde interpolation to separate kinetic form factor from Lichnerowicz mass term.
description: In src/action/spin_projectors.jl, add dS-adapted versions of the spin projectors. Add optional cosmological_constant kwarg (default=nothing→flat) to spin2_projector, spin1_projector, spin0s_projector, spin0w_projector. The key change: replace k² in the ω projector denominator with k²+c_J·Λ per the coefficients from the research issue. In src/action/kernel_extraction.jl, update spin_project() and _kernel_build_projector() to accept and pass through a cosmological_constant kwarg. At Λ=0 or Λ=nothing, must exactly reproduce current flat behavior (backward compatible).
title: Implement dS-adapted Barnes-Rivers spin projectors

description: Add DifferentialEquations.jl to [weakdeps] and [extensions] in Project.toml, following the pattern used for Symbolics.jl. Create ext/TensorGRDiffEqExt.jl stub module that imports TensorGR and DifferentialEquations. Add to test/Project.toml for testing. Verify the extension loads with 'using TensorGR, DifferentialEquations'. This provides the ODE solver infrastructure for both geodesic integration and TOV solver. ~30 lines.
title: Add DifferentialEquations.jl as weak dependency

description: Implement gauge field infrastructure for TensorGR.jl, following FieldsX (Fröb, arXiv:2008.12422). Requires fermion infrastructure from Epic TGR-2jh (for Grassmann grading). Covers: internal Lie algebra bundles, Yang-Mills field strength and action, BRST differential with ghost fields, ghost number grading. New directory: src/gauge/. Ground truth: Fröb (2020); Henneaux & Teitelboim 'Quantization of Gauge Systems' (1992) Ch 8. ~700 LOC total across 7 sub-issues.
title: Gauge Fields & BRST (FieldsX)

description: Design gauge field representation for TensorGR.jl. Core objects: Lie algebra valued 1-form A^I_a, field strength F^I_{ab} = partial_a A^I_b - partial_b A^I_a + f^I_{JK} A^J_a A^K_b. Need: (1) internal indices I,J,K on Lie algebra bundle (extend existing VBundle), (2) structure constants f^I_{JK} with antisymmetry and Jacobi identity, (3) gauge covariant derivative D_a X^I = partial_a X^I + f^I_{JK} A^J_a X^K. Design decisions: how structure constants are stored (tensor property or separate registry entry), how gauge vs spacetime indices interact in simplify pipeline. Deliverable: design document. Ground truth: standard gauge theory textbooks. ~0 LOC (design only).
title: Research: Design gauge field infrastructure for TensorGR

description: Implement Lie algebra vector bundle for gauge groups (SU(N), SO(N), etc.). Extend existing VBundle system with structure constants f^I_{JK}. API: define_gauge_bundle!(reg, :SU2; manifold=:M4, dim=3, structure_constants=f) where f is a rank-3 tensor with antisymmetry f^I_{JK} = -f^I_{KJ} and Jacobi identity f^I_{JL}f^L_{KM} + cyclic = 0. Register structure constants as a special tensor with total antisymmetry in the Lie algebra case. Must support: Killing form kappa_{IJ} = f^K_{IL}f^L_{JK}, Casimir operators. New file: src/gauge/lie_algebra.jl. Ground truth: standard gauge theory. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement internal Lie algebra bundle with structure constants

description: Implement Yang-Mills field strength F^I_{ab} = partial_a A^I_b - partial_b A^I_a + f^I_{JK} A^J_a A^K_b. Properties: antisymmetric in ab, transforms in adjoint of gauge group. Bianchi identity: D_{[a}F^I_{bc]} = 0 where D_a is gauge covariant derivative. Implement: (1) yang_mills_field_strength(A_name, gauge_bundle; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr, (2) gauge_covd(expr, A_name) for gauge covariant derivative, (3) verify Bianchi automatically. New file: src/gauge/yang_mills.jl. Ground truth: Peskin & Schroeder Ch 15; Weinberg QFT Vol 2 Ch 15. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Yang-Mills field strength with Bianchi identity

description: Implement Yang-Mills action S = -(1/4) integral F^I_{ab} F^{Iab} sqrt(g) d^4x. Derive field equations D_a F^{Ia}_b = 0 (sourceless Yang-Mills equations). Implement: (1) yang_mills_action(A_name; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr for the Lagrangian density, (2) yang_mills_equations(A_name; registry=reg) -> field equation expression, (3) verify: variation of action gives correct field equations (cross-check with variational_derivative). New file extends src/gauge/yang_mills.jl. Ground truth: Peskin & Schroeder Ch 15; Weinberg QFT Vol 2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Yang-Mills action and field equations

description: Implement BRST transformation s acting on gauge fields and ghosts. BRST rules: s(A^I_a) = D_a c^I (gauge ghost), s(c^I) = -(1/2)f^I_{JK} c^J c^K (ghost self-interaction), s(c_bar^I) = B^I (anti-ghost to Nakanishi-Lautrup), s(B^I) = 0. Ghost field c^I has ghost number +1 and is Grassmann-odd. Anti-ghost c_bar^I has ghost number -1. Nilpotency: s^2 = 0 on all fields. Implement: brst(expr, gauge_name; registry=reg) that applies BRST transformation. Ghost fields must use GradedTensor from fermion infrastructure. New file: src/gauge/brst.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 8; Fröb (2020) Sec 4. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement BRST differential on gauge and ghost fields

description: Implement ghost number tracking for BRST complex. Each field has an integer ghost number: physical fields (A, psi) = 0, ghost c = +1, anti-ghost c_bar = -1, Nakanishi-Lautrup B = 0. BRST increases ghost number by 1: gh(s(X)) = gh(X) + 1. Ghost number is additive in products: gh(XY) = gh(X) + gh(Y). Implement: (1) ghost_number(expr) -> Int that computes total ghost number, (2) filter_by_ghost_number(expr, n) to extract terms at given ghost number, (3) verify BRST-exact states have gh >= 1. Extends src/gauge/brst.jl. Ground truth: Fröb (2020) Sec 4; Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 8. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement ghost number grading

description: Verify BRST nilpotency s^2 = 0 on all field types. Tests: (1) s^2(A^I_a) = s(D_a c^I) = D_a(-(1/2)f^I_{JK}c^Jc^K) + f^I_{JK}(D_a c^J)c^K = 0 (uses Jacobi identity), (2) s^2(c^I) = s(-(1/2)f^I_{JK}c^Jc^K) = 0 (uses Jacobi + Grassmann antisymmetry), (3) s^2(c_bar^I) = s(B^I) = 0 (trivial), (4) s^2(psi) = 0 for matter field in representation R. Also verify: ghost number of s^2(X) is gh(X)+2, and the result vanishes identically. STRING MATCH s^2 = 0. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Theorem 8.1. New test file: test/test_brst.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: BRST nilpotency s^2 = 0 on all fields

description: Implement the second-order source term S^{(2)}_{ab} that arises from products of first-order metric perturbations h^{(1)} x h^{(1)} in the Einstein equations expanded to second order. On Schwarzschild background: G^{(2)}_{ab}[h^{(1)}, h^{(1)}] contains terms quadratic in h^{(1)} and its derivatives. Decompose into tensor spherical harmonics: S^{(2)}_{lm} = sum_{l_1,m_1,l_2,m_2} C^{lm}_{l_1 m_1, l_2 m_2} * f(r) where f(r) involves products of first-order radial functions. Must handle both even (Zerilli) and odd (RW) parity sources. ~150 LOC in src/perturbation/bh_second_order.jl. Reference: Brizuela, Martin-Garcia & Tiglio, PRD 80, 024021 (2009), arXiv:0903.1134, Eq 3.1, Sec 3. Test: source term vanishes when first-order perturbation is zero, i.e., S^{(2)}[0,0] = 0.
title: BH-Pert2: Implement second-order source terms from products of first-order perturbations

close_reason: Same-position contraction fallback added to _eval_spin_scalar. The residual dS gauge violation is a theoretical issue (flat projectors on dS kernel), not a code bug.
description: When projecting kernels extracted from MSS (de Sitter) expressions, spin_project produces terms with free indices (e.g. k^o k^o, g^{oo}) instead of a fully scalar result. Root cause: the projector's internal dummy indices (from theta/omega) collide with coefficient indices after ensure_no_dummy_clash. At Λ=0 (flat limit) this doesn't manifest because Λ-dependent terms vanish. At finite Λ, the collision creates uncontractable free index pairs. Diagnosis: inspecting spin_project(K_EH_dS, :spin2) shows 3 of 7 terms have 2 free Up indices named 'o'. Fix needed: ensure all dummy indices in the projector are unique w.r.t. the full coefficient expression.
notes: The residual g^{oo} issue is handled by _eval_spin_scalar fallback contraction. The REAL blocker for dS crosscheck is that flat Barnes-Rivers projectors give wrong results on dS kernels — the projectors need MSS adaptation (background curvature modifies the eigenvalue spectrum). This is a theoretical extension (dS-adapted spin projectors), not a bug in the existing code.
title: spin_project has index collision on dS kernels — free indices leak into scalar result

description: Six-level simplification algorithm for Riemann invariants, equivalent to xAct/Invar's RiemannSimplify. Levels: (1) permutation symmetries, (2) cyclic/first Bianchi, (3) second Bianchi, (4) derivative commutation, (5) dimensionally-dependent identities, (6) dual invariant products. Reference: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, arXiv:0706.2209, Section 4.
title: Invar Epic 2: Six-Level Simplification Algorithm

description: Level 1 of Invar's 6-level simplification: permutation symmetries. Ensure existing canonicalize() correctly handles all Riemann monomial cases: single Riemann with pair symmetry R_{abcd}=R_{cdab}, antisymmetry R_{abcd}=-R_{bacd}, and products of Riemann tensors. The existing xperm-based canonicalization should handle this, but verify coverage for: (a) products of n Riemann tensors, (b) mixed Riemann/Ricci/Weyl products, (c) products involving metric tensors. Add targeted tests in test/invariants/. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 4, Level 1. Extend src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 1 -- Permutation symmetries for Riemann monomials

description: Level 2 of Invar's 6-level simplification: cyclic symmetry R_{a[bcd]}=0 (first Bianchi identity). In terms of contractions, this generates relations between different invariants. For a product of n Riemann tensors, the cyclic identity on any factor produces a linear relation among invariants. Algorithm: for each Riemann factor in a monomial, apply R_{abcd}+R_{acdb}+R_{adbc}=0 and re-canonicalize using Level 1. Collect all relations to reduce to independent invariants. Add to src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 4.1. Test: at degree 2, cyclic identity reduces 6 naive invariants to 3 independent ones (R^2, Ric^2, Kretschner). ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 2 -- Cyclic symmetry (first Bianchi identity)

description: Level 3 of Invar's 6-level simplification: second Bianchi identity nabla_{[a}R_{bc]de}=0. Applies only to differential invariants (those with covariant derivatives). Algorithm: for each derivative-Riemann pair, apply the differential Bianchi identity to generate relations. Must integrate with existing commute_covds infrastructure. Add to src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 4.2. Test: nabla_a R^{abcd} = nabla^b R^{cd} - nabla^c R^{bd} (contracted Bianchi). ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 3 -- Second Bianchi identity for differential invariants

description: Level 4 of Invar's 6-level simplification: covariant derivative commutation [nabla_a, nabla_b]T_{...} = Riemann terms. Canonicalize derivative orderings on Riemann monomials. When two derivatives act on a Riemann tensor, commute them to canonical order, generating additional Riemann terms. Must integrate with existing sort_covds.jl infrastructure. Add to src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 4.3. Test: nabla_a nabla_b R_{cdef} - nabla_b nabla_a R_{cdef} produces sum of Riemann*Riemann terms. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 4 -- Derivative commutation for differential invariants

description: Level 5 of Invar's 6-level simplification: dimensionally-dependent identities (DDIs). In dimension d, the generalized Kronecker delta delta^{[a1...a_{d+1}]}_{[b1...b_{d+1}]}=0 generates identities between Riemann invariants that do not hold in general dimension. In d=4: delta^{[a1...a5]}_{[b1...b5]}=0 contracted with Riemann products gives non-trivial relations. Algorithm: (1) construct the (d+1)-antisymmetrized Kronecker delta, (2) contract with all possible Riemann monomial index sets, (3) expand and simplify to get relations. This is the most powerful level and where most invariant count reduction happens. Add to src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. Ground truth: Lovelock, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 12 (1971) 385; Harvey, J. Math. Phys. 36 (1995) 356; Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia (2007) Section 4.4. Test: in d=4, the Gauss-Bonnet combination R^2 - 4Ric^2 + Kretschner is a total derivative (DDI at degree 2). ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 5 -- Dimensionally-dependent identities (DDIs)

description: Level 6 of Invar's 6-level simplification: relations between products of dual invariants and non-dual invariants. Products of epsilon tensors can be expanded as sums of metric products (epsilon^{abcd}epsilon_{efgh} = 24 delta^{[a}_e delta^b_f delta^c_g delta^{d]}_h), converting dual invariants to non-dual ones. In d=4, this yields e.g. *R*R = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 2R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2/2 type relations (up to signs depending on conventions). Algorithm: for any product of DualRInvs, expand epsilon products to metrics and re-canonicalize. Add to src/invariants/simplify_levels.jl. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 4.5. Test: (*R*R)^2 reduces to algebraic invariants via epsilon expansion. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Level 6 -- Dual invariant product relations

description: Implement riemann_simplify(expr; registry=reg, dim=nothing, maxlevel=6) -- top-level function applying all 6 simplification levels in sequence. Algorithm: (1) convert to RInv canonical form, (2) apply levels 1-6 in order, each producing relations, (3) use Gaussian elimination on the relation matrix to find independent invariants, (4) express input as linear combination of independent invariants, (5) convert back to TensorExpr. If dim is specified, apply DDIs (level 5); otherwise skip. The function should be idempotent: applying it twice gives the same result. Add to src/invariants/riemann_simplify.jl. Ground truth: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 5. Test: simplify R_{abcd}R^{acbd} to (Kretschner - 2Ric^2 + R^2/2)/2 or equivalent canonical form. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Implement RiemannSimplify top-level function

close_reason: VBundle boundary enforcement in xperm, 25 tests
description:
## Summary

Extend the Butler-Portugal canonicalization engine (canonicalize.jl + wrapper.jl)
to correctly handle spinor index groups. The key changes are:

1. **Index group separation**: xperm must treat :SL2C, :SL2C_dot, and :Tangent
   indices as INDEPENDENT groups. Currently _canonicalize_product lumps all indices
   into a single permutation domain. For spinor expressions, indices from different
   vbundles must not be permuted into each other.

2. **Antisymmetric metric for dummies**: When spinor dummy pairs are passed to
   xperm_canonical_perm, metricQ must be -1 (antisymmetric), not +1 (symmetric).
   This affects the canonical form: X_{AB} Y^{BA} = -X_{AB} Y^{AB} under
   antisymmetric metric, vs +X_{AB} Y^{AB} under symmetric metric.

3. **Mixed tensor-spinor products**: For expressions like T_{a}^{AA'}, the
   permutation domain includes slots from all three vbundles. Symmetry generators
   must only swap slots within the same vbundle.

## Functions to modify

```julia
# src/algebra/canonicalize.jl

# _canonicalize_product(p::TProduct): Group slots by vbundle before building
# the permutation. Each vbundle group gets its own set of dummy pairs and
# free indices. The joint permutation domain includes all groups but generators
# are restricted to intra-group swaps.
#
# Key change in name_slots grouping:
#   Current:  key = (idx.name, idx.vbundle)  -- already vbundle-aware for pairing
#   Needed:   Additionally pass metricQ per vbundle group to xperm
#
# For the all-free mode (current approach), this mostly works already because
# indices are grouped by (name, vbundle). The main change is ensuring that
# symmetry generators do not cross vbundle boundaries.

# src/xperm/wrapper.jl

# xperm_canonical_perm: May need multiple calls (one per vbundle group) or
# a single call with vbundle-aware metricQ. The C API canonical_perm supports
# only one metricQ value. Options:
#   (a) Single call with metricQ=0 (no metric dummies), handle metric
#       contraction separately -- PREFERRED, matches current all-free mode
#   (b) Multiple calls per vbundle group -- complex, ordering issues
#
# CHOSEN: Option (a). Continue using all-free mode. Spinor metric contraction
# (epsilon contraction) is handled by contract_metrics, not by xperm.
```

## Design constraint

The current all-free xperm mode already avoids metricQ issues: all indices are
treated as free, and dummy normalization happens in collect_terms. This approach
extends naturally to spinors. The main work is ensuring that:
- Symmetry generators (from symmetry_generators()) respect vbundle boundaries
- Factor sort key accounts for spinor index vbundles
- _ImplodedObject handles mixed-vbundle tensors

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Section 3.3: spinor symmetries
- Psi_{ABCD} has FullySymmetric(1,2,3,4) on its :SL2C indices
- Phi_{ABA'B'} has Symmetric(1,2) on :SL2C pair + Symmetric(3,4) on :SL2C_dot pair
- R_{abcd} in spinor form: Psi_{ABCD} eps_{A'B'} eps_{C'D'} + ... has symmetries
  that mix tensor and spinor indices via the soldering form, NOT directly

## Tests

1. Psi_{ABCD} = Psi_{BACD} after canonicalize (symmetry within :SL2C)
2. X_{AB} Y^{BA} canonicalizes to -X_{AB} Y^{AB} (antisymmetry of epsilon contraction)
3. Phi_{ABA'B'} = Phi_{BAA'B'} (undotted pair symmetry)
4. Phi_{ABA'B'} = Phi_{ABB'A'} (dotted pair symmetry)
5. Mixed: T_{a}^{A} sigma^{BB'}_{b} -- tensor indices :Tangent not swapped with spinor :SL2C
6. Zero detection: epsilon_{AB} psi^A psi^B = 0 (antisymmetry + identical factors)
title: Spinors: Extend xperm canonicalization for spinor index symmetries

close_reason: Fixed by clean-room extract_kernel_direct: bypasses both simplify and to_fourier for bilinear forms
description: The simplify pipeline (canonicalize + collect_terms) merges ΓΓ and ∂Γ terms during term collection. After merging, bilinear terms end up wrapped inside derivative nodes as ∂(h₁×stuff×h₂), which cannot be decomposed into left-h × right-h × coefficient. This is the core reason the Fourier→extract_kernel pipeline gives wrong spin projections. Workaround: extract kernel from RAW (unsimplified) perturbation engine output, or pre-simplify individual factors before forming bilinear products.
title: simplify merges terms into ∂(bilinear) that breaks kernel extraction

description: Implement automatic enforcement of divergence-free constraints: when a tensor T is registered as divergence-free (nabla_a T^{ab...} = 0), any covariant divergence of T should automatically simplify to zero. Extend TensorProperties with is_divfree::Bool and divfree_index::Int (which index is divergence-free). Modify the simplify pipeline to detect nabla_a T^{a...} patterns after CovD expansion and set them to zero. Must work with both partial derivatives and covariant derivatives. Function: set_divfree!(reg, :T; covd=:D, index=1). ~80 LOC modifying src/registry.jl + src/algebra/simplify.jl. Reference: xAct-contrib/DivFree package documentation; Einstein tensor nabla_a G^{ab} = 0, stress-energy conservation nabla_a T^{ab} = 0. Test: Given divergence-free T^{ab}, simplify(TDeriv(down(:a), T^{ab}, :D)) returns TScalar(0), matching conservation equation.
title: Constraints: Implement automatic divergence-free enforcement in simplify pipeline

description: Implement the diagonalization of the linearized Hassan-Rosen bimetric system into mass eigenstates: a massless mode h^{(0)}_{ab} and a massive mode h^{(m)}_{ab}. The mass eigenstates are linear combinations: h^{(0)} = alpha delta_g + beta delta_f, h^{(m)} = gamma delta_g + delta delta_f with coefficients depending on the background ratio c and the beta_n parameters. The massless mode satisfies the linearized Einstein equation, while the massive mode satisfies the Fierz-Pauli equation with mass m^2 = (beta_1 + 2c beta_2 + c^2 beta_3)(1 + c^2)/(c M_g^2). Function: bimetric_mass_eigenstates(delta_g, delta_f; betas, c) -> (h0, hm, m2). ~80 LOC in src/perturbation/bimetric.jl. Reference: Hassan & Rosen, JHEP 1202, 126 (2012), arXiv:1109.3515, Eq 3.5; Torsello et al, arXiv:1904.10464, Sec 4.2. Test: STRING MATCH — mass eigenstate decomposition is orthogonal: h^{(0)} and h^{(m)} decouple in the quadratic action (cross term vanishes), matching Hassan & Rosen Eq 3.5.
title: Bimetric: Implement mass eigenstates diagonalization (massless + massive spin-2 modes)

description: Verify that Schwarzschild and Kerr spacetimes are correctly classified as Petrov Type D using the petrov_type function. For both spacetimes: Psi_0 = Psi_1 = Psi_3 = Psi_4 = 0, only Psi_2 nonzero. This means two double principal null directions (PNDs) = Type D. Also verify: (1) Minkowski is Type O (all Psi = 0), (2) a generic spacetime with all Psi nonzero is Type I, (3) pp-wave (only Psi_4 nonzero) is Type N. ~60 LOC in test/test_petrov.jl. Reference: Chandrasekhar 'Mathematical Theory of Black Holes' Ch 1; Stephani et al Ch 4. Test: petrov_type returns :D for Schwarzschild and Kerr, :O for Minkowski, :N for pp-wave.
title: Validation: Petrov Type D verification for Schwarzschild/Kerr

close_reason: Implemented with Penrose-Rindler Sec 2.5, eps contraction + see-saw verified
description:
## Summary

Register the SL(2,C) spin metric epsilon_{AB} and its inverse epsilon^{AB},
plus the dotted conjugates epsilon_{A'B'} and epsilon^{A'B'}. These are the
fundamental index-raising/lowering objects for spinor calculus, analogous to
g_{ab} for tensor indices but ANTISYMMETRIC.

Key difference from tensor metric: epsilon_{AB} = -epsilon_{BA}, so raising/
lowering picks up signs (the "see-saw" identity). The contraction engine in
contraction.jl must handle this antisymmetric metric correctly.

## Tensors to register

```julia
# In src/spinors/spin_metric.jl (~100 LOC)

define_spin_metric!(reg::TensorRegistry; manifold::Symbol=:M4)
```

Registers:
1. :eps_spin -- rank (0,2) on :SL2C, symmetry [AntiSymmetric(1,2)], is_metric=true
2. :eps_spin -- inverse: same tensor, rank (2,0), handled by metric engine (g^{AB} = epsilon^{AB})
3. :eps_spin_dot -- rank (0,2) on :SL2C_dot, symmetry [AntiSymmetric(1,2)], is_metric=true
4. :delta_spin -- rank (1,1) on :SL2C, is_delta=true (epsilon^{AC} epsilon_{CB} = delta^A_B)
5. :delta_spin_dot -- rank (1,1) on :SL2C_dot, is_delta=true

Store in registry metric_cache: metric_cache[:SL2C] = :eps_spin,
delta_cache[:SL2C] = :delta_spin (keyed by vbundle symbol, not manifold).
This requires extending metric_cache/delta_cache to also index by vbundle.

## Contraction engine changes

The existing _try_metric_contraction in contraction.jl already checks
idx.vbundle match, but uses metricQ=1 (symmetric) for dummy pair handling.
For spinor metrics, metricQ must be -1 (antisymmetric). The contraction
engine needs to check whether the metric tensor is antisymmetric and pass
the correct sign.

Key identity: epsilon^{AC} epsilon_{CB} = delta^A_B (NOT -delta^A_B).
This works because the contraction produces eps^{AC} eps_{CB} where the
matched indices are (C_up, C_down) and the free index A transfers normally.

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 2.5.2-2.5.8:
  epsilon_{AB} = -epsilon_{BA}
  epsilon_{01} = 1, epsilon_{10} = -1
  epsilon^{AB} epsilon_{BC} = delta^A_C
  phi^A = epsilon^{AB} phi_B (raising convention: FIRST index of epsilon)
  phi_A = phi^B epsilon_{BA} (lowering convention: SECOND index of epsilon)

- Wald Ch 13, Eq 13.1.14-13.1.16

## Tests

1. epsilon_{AB} = -epsilon_{BA} after canonicalize
2. epsilon^{AC} epsilon_{CB} simplifies to delta^A_B
3. Self-trace: epsilon^A_A = epsilon^{AB} delta_{BA} = 2 (dim of SL2C bundle)
4. Sign: epsilon^{AB} epsilon_{AB} = 2 (not -2; contraction of antisym with antisym)
5. Dotted metric: same tests with :SL2C_dot indices
6. No cross-contraction: epsilon^{AB} (undotted) does not contract with phi_{A'} (dotted)
title: Spinors: Implement spinor metric epsilon_{AB} with antisymmetric contraction

The perturbation engine pipeline uses: K_EH = extract_kernel_v2(h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab}), K_R² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹R)²), K_Ric² = extract_kernel_v2((δ¹Ric)²). The -2 sign is NOT a bug in the extraction; it comes from the relationship between the action and the kinetic operator. close_reason: Implemented with Penrose-Rindler Sec 2.5, see-saw sign verified
description:
## Summary

Implement the spinor "see-saw" identity for raising/lowering spinor indices
via the antisymmetric spin metric epsilon_{AB}. Unlike the symmetric tensor
metric g_{ab}, the antisymmetric epsilon requires careful sign tracking:

  phi^A = epsilon^{AB} phi_B       (raise: use FIRST index of epsilon^{AB})
  phi_A = phi^B epsilon_{BA}       (lower: use SECOND index of epsilon_{BA})
  
The see-saw identity: psi^A chi_A = -psi_A chi^A (sign flip on contraction).
This is the signature feature of 2-component spinor calculus.

## Function

```julia
# In src/spinors/contraction.jl (~100 LOC)

contract_spin_metrics(expr::TensorExpr; registry=current_registry()) -> TensorExpr
```

Extends the existing contract_metrics() pipeline. When a spin metric epsilon^{AB}
or epsilon_{AB} appears in a product and shares a dummy spinor index with another
factor, contract it by raising/lowering the spinor index on the partner tensor.

The sign convention matters:
- epsilon^{AB} T_{B...} -> T^{A}_{...}  (no extra sign: contracted on second index of epsilon)
- epsilon^{AB} T_{A...} -> -T^{B}_{...} (extra minus: contracted on first index, antisymmetry gives sign)
- Equivalently: epsilon^{BA} T_{A...} -> T^{B}_{...} (swap first, pick up -1, then contract second)

## Integration with simplify pipeline

Option 1: Modify contract_metrics() to also handle spin metrics (check is_metric
+ antisymmetric flag). This is clean since the existing engine already handles
metric contraction.

Option 2: Separate function called after contract_metrics in the simplify pipeline.

CHOSEN: Option 1 -- extend contract_metrics. The existing _try_metric_contraction
already works for any metric tensor. The only change needed is tracking the
antisymmetry sign when the contraction is on the "wrong" index of an antisymmetric
metric (first index instead of second, or vice versa).

Specifically: when metric g has AntiSymmetric(1,2), and the contracted index is
at metric slot 1 (not slot 2), introduce an extra -1 sign.

## Ground truth

- Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 2.5.25-2.5.27:
  psi^A = epsilon^{AB} psi_B
  psi_A = psi^B epsilon_{BA}  (note: second index of epsilon)
  KEY: psi^A chi_A = epsilon^{AB} psi_B chi_A = -psi_B chi^B = -psi_A chi^A

- Wald Ch 13, Eq 13.1.14-13.1.16

## Tests

1. epsilon^{AB} phi_B simplifies to phi^A (contraction on second index)
2. epsilon^{AB} phi_A simplifies to -phi^B (contraction on first index, antisymmetry sign)
3. See-saw: simplify(psi^A chi_A + psi_A chi^A) = 0  (they cancel due to sign)
4. Double raise: epsilon^{AB} epsilon^{CD} T_{BD} simplifies correctly
5. Dotted see-saw: same tests with :SL2C_dot indices
6. Mixed: epsilon^{AB} T_{aB} (tensor + spinor indices) contracts spinor only
title: Spinors: Implement see-saw contraction rule for antisymmetric spin metric

description: Extend TensorGR.jl exterior calculus to algebra-valued differential forms. Connection 1-form A = A^I_a T_I dx^a, curvature 2-form F = dA + A wedge A, gauge-covariant exterior derivative D = d + [A, .], Chern-Simons form. Builds on existing src/exterior/ infrastructure. New files in src/exterior/ or src/gauge/. Ground truth: Nakahara 'Geometry, Topology and Physics' Ch 11. ~400 LOC total across 4 sub-issues.
title: Algebra-Valued Forms (AVF)

description: Extend existing exterior calculus (src/exterior/forms.jl) to support forms valued in a Lie algebra. A Lie-algebra-valued p-form is omega = omega^I_{a1...ap} T_I dx^{a1} wedge ... wedge dx^{ap} where T_I are Lie algebra generators. Implement AlgValuedForm type wrapping (form_degree, lie_algebra_bundle, tensor_expr). Must support: wedge product with Lie bracket [omega wedge eta]^I = f^I_{JK} omega^J wedge eta^K, exterior derivative d acting on form indices only. Connection 1-form: A = A^I_a T_I dx^a. New file: src/exterior/algebra_forms.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Ch 11, Sec 11.1. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement algebra-valued differential forms

description: Implement gauge-covariant exterior derivative D = d + A wedge (.) acting on algebra-valued forms. For a Lie-algebra-valued p-form omega: D omega = d omega + [A, omega] = d omega^I T_I + f^I_{JK} A^J wedge omega^K T_I. Properties: D^2 omega = [F, omega] (curvature as obstruction to D^2=0). Must handle: (1) D on scalar-valued forms (just d), (2) D on adjoint-valued forms (with structure constants), (3) Leibniz rule for wedge products of mixed-valued forms. Extends src/exterior/algebra_forms.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Sec 11.1; Eguchi, Gilkey & Hanson, Phys. Rep. 66, 213 (1980). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement gauge-covariant exterior derivative

description: Implement field strength as curvature 2-form: F = dA + A wedge A (equivalently F^I = dA^I + (1/2)f^I_{JK} A^J wedge A^K). This is the structure equation of a principal bundle connection. Verify Bianchi identity: DF = dF + [A, F] = 0 (automatic consequence of d^2=0 and Jacobi). Implement: (1) curvature_2form(A_connection) -> algebra-valued 2-form, (2) verify_bianchi(F, A) that checks DF = 0. Must connect with existing connection_form/curvature_form in src/exterior/connection_forms.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Sec 11.2; Kobayashi & Nomizu Vol 1 Ch 2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement gauge field strength as curvature 2-form
