description: Implement Chern-Simons 3-form: CS = Tr(A wedge dA + (2/3)A wedge A wedge A) where Tr is the Killing form on the Lie algebra. Key identity: dCS = Tr(F wedge F) (the Pontryagin/instanton density). This connects to existing topological invariant infrastructure in src/gr/topological.jl. Implement: (1) chern_simons_3form(A_connection) -> 3-form expression, (2) verify d(CS) = Tr(F wedge F), (3) gauge transformation: under A -> g^{-1}Ag + g^{-1}dg, CS changes by exact + winding number term. New file: src/exterior/chern_simons.jl. Ground truth: Nakahara Sec 11.5; Chern & Simons, Ann. Math. 99, 48 (1974). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Chern-Simons form with dCS = Tr(F wedge F)

description: Implement the full set of 18 Newman-Penrose field equations (the Ricci identities projected onto the null tetrad). These are first-order ODEs for the 12 spin coefficients involving the Weyl scalars, Ricci scalars, and directional derivatives D=l^a nabla_a, Delta=n^a nabla_a, delta=m^a nabla_a, deltabar=mbar^a nabla_a. All 18 equations from NP (1962) Eqs 4.2a through 4.2r. Each equation must be stored as a RewriteRule or identity that can be applied. Function: np_field_equations(; registry) -> Vector of equations. ~100 LOC in src/spinors/np_equations.jl. Reference: NP (1962) Eqs 4.2a-4.2r. Test: STRING-MATCH each of the 18 equations against the NP paper. Verify equation count = 18.
title: NP: Implement 18 NP field equations (Ricci identities)

description:
DESIGN DECISION: The Tetrad struct and its registration API.

Struct design:
  struct Tetrad
    name::Symbol
    chart::Symbol            # underlying coordinate chart
    manifold::Symbol
    basis_type::Symbol       # :orthonormal or :null
    frame_vectors::Matrix    # e^a_mu (frame x coord), rows = frame indices, cols = coord indices
    inverse_frame::Matrix    # theta^mu_a = e_a^mu (inverse), auto-computed from frame_vectors
    frame_metric::Matrix     # eta_{IJ} = e^mu_I e^nu_J g_{mu nu}
    lorentz_vbundle::Symbol  # VBundle for frame indices (default :Lorentz)
  end

Registration API:
  define_tetrad!(reg, :tet; chart=:Schw, frame_vectors=e, basis_type=:orthonormal)

This function should:
1. Verify chart exists and get its manifold + metric
2. Compute inverse_frame = inv(frame_vectors)
3. Verify completeness: e^a_mu * theta^mu_b = delta^a_b
4. Compute frame_metric: eta_{IJ} = sum_mu,nu e^mu_I g_{mu nu} e^nu_J
5. For :orthonormal, verify eta = diag(signature)
6. For :null, verify NP normalization (l.n = -1, m.mbar = +1, rest = 0)
7. Register a :Lorentz VBundle if not already present
8. Register the tetrad tensor e^a_I as a TensorProperties entry
9. Store Tetrad in registry options

Interaction with existing null_tetrad_from_metric in src/xideal/weyl_scalars.jl:
  - That function returns (l, n, m, mbar) as vectors -- wrap into Tetrad via convenience:
    null_tetrad(g::CTensor) -> Tetrad  (calls null_tetrad_from_metric, wraps result)

Interaction with existing basis_change in src/components/ctensor.jl:
  - basis_change(ct, jacobian) already handles Up/Down transformation
  - to_frame(ct, tetrad) should call basis_change with frame_vectors as jacobian

References: xCoba DefBasis[]; Chandrasekhar Ch 1 Sec 1-3; Stephani et al. Ch 3.
title: Design: Tetrad struct and define_tetrad! API

description: Implement remaining xTras functionality: SymmetrizeCovDs (force CovDs into symmetrized form), EulerDensity for arbitrary d (generalize current d=4), InvarLagrangian (construct most general Lagrangian at given derivative order). Ground truth: Nutma Secs 5-6, Lovelock (1971). 3 sub-issues, ~100 LOC each.
title: Additional xTras Gaps (SymmetrizeCovDs, EulerDensity, InvarLagrangian)

description: Force covariant derivatives into symmetrized form: nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} = (1/2)(nabla_a nabla_b + nabla_b nabla_a). When acting on a scalar, nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} phi = nabla_a nabla_b phi (already symmetric). When acting on tensors, use commutator relation [nabla_a, nabla_b]V^c = R^c_{dab}V^d to write nabla_a nabla_b = nabla_{(a} nabla_{b)} + (1/2)R... This is the inverse operation of commute_covds: instead of sorting to canonical order, express in manifestly symmetric form. API: symmetrize_covds(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 5. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.
title: Implement SymmetrizeCovDs

description: Generalize current d=4 Euler density (in src/gr/topological.jl) to E_{2n} in d=2n for arbitrary even dimension. The Euler density in d=2n is: E_{2n} = (1/(2^n)) delta^{a1 b1 ... an bn}_{c1 d1 ... cn dn} R^{c1 d1}_{a1 b1} ... R^{cn dn}_{an bn}. This uses the generalized Kronecker delta from Epic TGR-xlu. Cases: d=2: E_2 = R (Ricci scalar), d=4: E_4 = R^2 - 4RabRab + RabcdRabcd (Gauss-Bonnet), d=6: cubic Lovelock, etc. API: euler_density(manifold; registry=reg) or euler_density(dim, indices...; registry=reg). Ground truth: Nutma Sec 6; Lovelock (1971). ~100 LOC in src/gr/topological.jl.
title: Implement EulerDensity for arbitrary dimension

description: Construct the most general diffeomorphism-invariant Lagrangian at a given derivative order using independent curvature invariants. At each order, enumerate all independent scalar invariants built from metric, Riemann, and covariant derivatives, modulo Bianchi identities and DDIs. Orders: 0 derivatives: cosmological constant Lambda. 2 derivatives: R (unique). 4 derivatives: R^2, R_{ab}R^{ab}, R_{abcd}R^{abcd} (3 in d>=5, 2 independent in d=4 by Gauss-Bonnet). 6 derivatives: 8 invariants in d>=7 (fewer in low d). Uses AllContractions (Epic TGR-ihp) and DDIs (Epic TGR-xlu). API: invariant_lagrangian(order; dim, registry=reg) -> TSum with undetermined coefficients. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 2; Fulling et al (1992). ~100 LOC in src/algebra/all_contractions.jl.
title: Implement InvarLagrangian

close_reason: Fixed by clean-room extract_kernel_direct: bypasses both simplify and to_fourier for bilinear forms
description: In a quadratic action ∫dx h₁(x) K h₂(x), the two fields carry opposite momenta (+k and -k). The to_fourier function replaces ALL ∂→k regardless of which field the derivative acts on. This is correct only when each derivative acts on a different field (symmetric: n_L=n_R). For asymmetric terms like h₁(∂²h₂), uniform-k gives k² but the correct result is -k² (the (-ik)² = -k² from the right field momentum). The phase correction is (-1)^{n/2+n_R} where n=n_L+n_R.
title: to_fourier uniform-k gives wrong signs for bilinear forms with asymmetric derivatives

close_reason: Fixed: expand_products inside _distribute_derivs_sums. R□R/Ric□Ric kernels work. Commit aa7d665.
description: extract_kernel_direct flattens nested TDeriv(TProduct(...)) structures via _unwrap_field_chain/_unwrap_deriv_product/_extract_bilinear_direct. When inner dummy indices share names with outer indices, the flattening produces indices appearing 3+ times, creating invalid index structures. Fix: alpha-rename inner dummies to fresh names during scope flattening. This is the foundational fix that enables unconjugated (stronger) canonicalization. See HANDOFF.md for full context.
title: Fix kernel extraction index scoping for unconjugated canonicalization

description: Implement irreducible decomposition of arbitrary spinors into totally symmetric parts using Young projectors for SL(2,C). Any spinor phi_{ABC...} decomposes into totally symmetric pieces plus trace terms (epsilon contractions). For rank-2: phi_{AB} = phi_{(AB)} + (1/2) epsilon_{AB} phi^C_C. For rank-3: phi_{ABC} = phi_{(ABC)} + epsilon_{A(B} chi_{C)} where chi_C involves traces. Function: irreducible_decompose(expr; registry). ~100 LOC in src/spinors/irreducible.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Section 3.3. Test: (1) rank-2: phi_{AB} = phi_{(AB)} + (1/2) epsilon_{AB} trace, (2) totally symmetric input returns unchanged, (3) antisymmetric part of 2-spinor is pure trace: phi_{[AB]} = (1/2) epsilon_{AB} phi^C_C.
title: Spinors: Implement irreducible spinor decomposition

description: Ensure spinor expressions work seamlessly with the existing TensorGR simplify(), canonicalize(), and contract_metrics() pipeline. Key integration points: (1) simplify() must handle mixed tensor-spinor expressions (indices of both kinds), (2) contract_metrics() must contract spin metrics epsilon_{AB} as well as spacetime metrics g_{ab}, (3) canonicalize() must use extended xperm for spinor symmetries (depends on issue TGR-60o), (4) collect_terms() must identify equivalent spinor terms, (5) rules engine must support spinor pattern variables (spin_down(:A_) as pattern). ~100 LOC modifying src/algebra/simplify.jl, src/algebra/contraction.jl, src/rules.jl. Reference: internal consistency. Test: (1) simplify(Psi_{ABCD} epsilon^{AD}) correctly contracts, (2) mixed expression g^{ab} T_{a AA'} sigma^{BB'}_b simplifies, (3) spinor rule application works, (4) full round-trip: define spinor tensor, build expression, simplify, verify canonical form.
title: Integration: Spinor expressions in TensorGR simplify pipeline

description: Verify the Goldberg-Sachs theorem: In a vacuum spacetime, Psi_0 = Psi_1 = 0 if and only if kappa = sigma = 0 (the repeated principal null direction is geodesic and shear-free). Test both directions: (1) Forward: given Schwarzschild in Kinnersley tetrad (aligned with PND), verify kappa = sigma = 0 and Psi_0 = Psi_1 = 0 simultaneously, (2) Converse: if we set kappa = sigma = 0 in the NP equations and assume vacuum, verify the NP field equations force Psi_0 = Psi_1 = 0, (3) For Kerr, same verification. ~60 LOC in test/test_goldberg_sachs.jl. Reference: Goldberg & Sachs, Acta Phys. Polon. Suppl. 22, 13 (1962); also Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 Sec 7.3. Test: verify kappa=sigma=0 iff Psi_0=Psi_1=0 for Schwarzschild and Kerr.
title: Validation: Goldberg-Sachs theorem test

description: Implement scalar spherical harmonics Y_{lm}(theta, phi) for TensorGR.jl. This provides the foundation for tensor harmonic decomposition on S^2, needed for black hole perturbation theory. New directory: src/harmonics/. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson, Phys. Rev. D 71, 104003 (2005), arXiv:gr-qc/0502028, Sec II.A-II.B. Issues: ScalarHarmonic type, algebra (Clebsch-Gordan), angular Laplacian eigenvalue, scalar field decomposition. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: Harmonics Epic 1: Scalar Spherical Harmonics Infrastructure

description: Implement ScalarHarmonic type representing Y_{lm}(theta, phi) with quantum numbers (l, m). Properties: l >= 0, |m| <= l, eigenvalue of Laplacian on S^2. New file: src/harmonics/scalar_harmonics.jl. Type should store (l::Int, m::Int) and integrate with TensorExpr AST (probably as TScalar wrapper or new leaf node). Must support conjugation Y*_{lm} = (-1)^m Y_{l,-m}. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson, Phys. Rev. D 71, 104003 (2005), Sec II.A. Test: construct Y_{2,1}, verify quantum number validation rejects l<0 or |m|>l. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement ScalarHarmonic type Y_{lm}

description: Implement product algebra for scalar harmonics: Y_{l1,m1} * Y_{l2,m2} = sum over (l3,m3) of Clebsch-Gordan coefficients times Y_{l3,m3}. Selection rules: |l1-l2| <= l3 <= l1+l2, m3 = m1+m2. Implement Clebsch-Gordan coefficient computation (or Wigner 3j symbols). File: src/harmonics/clebsch_gordan.jl. Ground truth: standard angular momentum coupling theory. Test: Y_{1,0} * Y_{1,0} decomposes into Y_{0,0} and Y_{2,0} with correct coefficients. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement scalar harmonic algebra (Clebsch-Gordan)

description: Implement the angular Laplacian acting on scalar harmonics: Delta_Omega Y_{lm} = -l(l+1) Y_{lm}. This is a simplification rule that replaces Laplacian(Y_{lm}) with the eigenvalue times Y_{lm}. Should integrate with the rules engine (RewriteRule). The Laplacian on S^2 is Delta_Omega = (1/sin theta) d/d_theta (sin theta d/d_theta) + (1/sin^2 theta) d^2/d_phi^2. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eq 2.1. File: src/harmonics/laplacian.jl. Test: simplify(laplacian_S2(Y_{3,1})) == -12 * Y_{3,1}. ~80 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement angular Laplacian eigenvalue relation

description: Implement decomposition of a scalar field on S^2 x R^2: f(t,r,theta,phi) = sum_{l,m} f_{lm}(t,r) Y_{lm}(theta,phi). Provide decompose_scalar(expr, harmonics_config) that rewrites a scalar TensorExpr into a sum over (l,m) modes with radial coefficient functions f_{lm} as abstract tensors (functions of t,r only). Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Sec II.B. File: src/harmonics/decompose_scalar.jl. Test: decompose a scalar Phi and verify it produces sum over modes with correct structure. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement scalar field harmonic decomposition

description:
Implement worldline matter-graviton coupling vertices for point particles in the NRGR framework.

## Background

In the EFT of post-Newtonian gravity (Goldberger & Rothstein, hep-th/0409156, Sec 3; Porto, 1104.2712, Sec 2.2), compact objects are modeled as point particles with a worldline action:

    S_pp = -m int ds sqrt(-g_{ab}(x(s)) v^a v^b)

Expanding g_{ab} = eta_{ab} + kappa h_{ab}:
- Linear coupling:   (kappa/2) int dt T^{ab}(x) h_{ab}(x)
- Quadratic:         (kappa^2/8) int dt [...] h_{ab} h_{cd}
- where T^{ab} = m v^a v^b delta^(d)(x - z(t)) is the point-particle stress-energy tensor.

For spinning objects, additional spin-gravity couplings arise from the spin supplementary condition (SSC):

    S_spin = -(1/2) int ds S^{ab} Omega_{ab}

where S^{ab} is the spin tensor and Omega_{ab} is the angular velocity of the body-fixed frame. At leading order in the multipole expansion, this yields spin-orbit and spin-spin couplings via the Riemann tensor.

## Integration with existing infrastructure

Must build on:
- `Worldline`, `define_worldline!`, `pn_order`, `truncate_pn` from `src/worldline/worldline.jl` — existing worldline type with velocity normalization rules
- `perturb`, `expand_perturbation` from `src/perturbation/` — metric expansion
- `MetricPerturbation`, `define_metric_perturbation!` from `src/perturbation/metric_perturbation.jl`
- `to_fourier` from `src/svt/fourier.jl` — Fourier transform to momentum space
- `lie_derivative` from `src/gr/lie.jl` — for gauge transformations of matter couplings

## Function signatures

```julia
# Point-particle stress-energy tensor (momentum space)
pp_stress_energy(wl::Worldline, a::TIndex, b::TIndex;
                 k::Symbol=:k) -> TensorExpr
# Returns: m * v^a * v^b * exp(i k.x)  (Fourier convention)

# Linear matter-graviton vertex: kappa * T^{ab} h_{ab}
matter_vertex_linear(wl::Worldline, h_indices::Tuple{TIndex,TIndex};
                     k::Symbol=:k, kappa::Symbol=:kappa) -> TensorExpr

# Quadratic matter-graviton vertex: kappa^2 T^{abcd} h_{ab} h_{cd}
matter_vertex_quadratic(wl::Worldline, h1_indices, h2_indices;
                        ...) -> TensorExpr

# Spin-orbit coupling vertex (LO)
spin_orbit_vertex(wl::Worldline, spin::Symbol;
                  k::Symbol=:k, order::Int=1) -> TensorExpr

# Generic n-th order matter-graviton coupling
matter_vertex_n(wl::Worldline, n::Int;
                h_index_groups::Vector{Tuple{TIndex,TIndex}},
                ...) -> TensorExpr

# Two-body potential from single graviton exchange (tree level)
tree_potential(wl1::Worldline, wl2::Worldline;
               metric::Symbol=:eta, kappa::Symbol=:kappa) -> TensorExpr
```

## Ground truth (verification tests)

1. **Newton potential**: Linear coupling vertex for particle 1 contracted with graviton propagator D^{abcd}(k) and linear coupling vertex for particle 2, Fourier-transformed to position space, gives:
   V_0 = -G m_1 m_2 / r
   This is the tree-level diagram in NRGR. Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Eq 30.

2. **Velocity corrections**: At 1PN, the v^2 corrections to the potential arise from:
   (a) v^2 * T^{00} coupling (kinetic energy of source)
   (b) T^{0i} h_{0i} coupling (gravitomagnetic)
   (c) T^{ij} h_{ij} coupling (stress contribution)
   Must reproduce the Einstein-Infeld-Hoffmann Lagrangian at 1PN. Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Eq 31.

3. **Spin-orbit at LO**: Leading spin-orbit coupling gives the Lense-Thirring potential V_SO ~ G S.L / (m r^3). Reference: Porto, 1104.2712, Eq 2.15; Levi & Steinhoff 1510.06752 Eq 2.6.

4. **Gauge invariance**: Under linearized diffeomorphism h_{ab} -> h_{ab} + partial_a xi_b + partial_b xi_a, the matter action is invariant up to total derivatives (stress-energy conservation).

## Implementation notes

- The existing Worldline struct already tracks velocity, position, and normalization rules
- Each vertex order n carries a factor kappa^n / (2^n n!)
- In NRGR, temporal components h_{00} = 2 Phi (Newtonian potential), h_{0i} = A_i (gravitomagnetic)
- Interface with foliation decomposition for spatial/temporal split
- ~100 LOC in src/feynman/matter_vertices.jl
title: EFTofPNG: Implement matter-graviton coupling vertices for point particles

description: Implement Degenerate Higher-Order Scalar-Tensor (DHOST) class I Lagrangian: L = f(phi,X) R + sum_{i=1}^{5} a_i(phi,X) L_i^{(2)} where the 5 quadratic building blocks are: L_1 = (nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^a nabla^b phi), L_2 = (Box phi)^2, L_3 = (Box phi)(nabla^a phi)(nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^b phi), L_4 = (nabla^a phi)(nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^b nabla_c phi)(nabla^c phi), L_5 = [(nabla^a phi)(nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^b phi)]^2. Each L_i as TensorExpr with a scalar field phi and metric g. ~120 LOC in src/scalar_tensor/dhost.jl. Reference: Langlois & Noui, JCAP 1602 (2016) 034, arXiv:1510.06930, Eq 2.1-2.5. Test: L_1 and L_2 correctly simplify to known expressions when phi has constant gradient (nabla_a phi = delta^0_a), matching Langlois & Noui Eq 2.3.
title: DHOST: Implement class I Lagrangian with 5 quadratic combinations of nabla nabla phi

description: Implement the SU(2) spatial spin metric epsilon_{AB} (real, antisymmetric, 2x2) and the 3D soldering form sigma^i_{AB} (symmetric in AB) connecting spatial vector indices i,j,k to spatial spinor indices A,B. Properties: epsilon_{AB} epsilon^{BC} = delta_A^C, sigma^i_{AB} sigma_i^{CD} = epsilon^{(C}_{(A} epsilon^{D)}_{B)}. The soldering form is the 3D analog of the Infeld-van der Waerden symbols. Must register these as tensors with appropriate symmetries in TensorRegistry. ~80 LOC in src/spinors/space_spinors.jl. Reference: Ashtekar, Lectures on Non-Perturbative Canonical Gravity (1991), Ch 2, Eq 2.1.3-2.1.8; Sen, J. Math. Phys. 22, 1781 (1981), Eq 2.1-2.5. Test: STRING MATCH — sigma^i_{AB} sigma_i^{CD} contracted and simplified gives the symmetrized product of epsilon tensors, matching Ashtekar Eq 2.1.5.
title: SpaceSpinors: Implement spatial spin metric epsilon_{AB} and soldering form sigma^i_{AB}
