close_reason:
Research complete. Created 7 issues decomposing the second-order BH perturbation mode coupling infrastructure:

**New issues (dependency order):**
1. TGR-5i0: RWZ metric decomposition on Schwarzschild (even/odd parity sectors, radial coefficients)
2. TGR-bw9: Angular integration engine for tensor harmonic products (vector/tensor Gaunt coefficients)
3. TGR-wbm: Mode coupling coefficient tables C^{lm}_{l1m1,l2m2} (precomputed, selection rules)
4. TGR-xb3: First-order RW/Zerilli master equations + gauge-invariant variables (unblocks bench_05 @test_skip)
5. TGR-u19: Radial source term assembly from mode-coupled second-order Einstein equations
6. TGR-22h: Second-order gauge-invariant master equations with coupled source terms
7. TGR-2y0: End-to-end validation (l=2 quadrupole coupling, Brizuela et al Table I)

**Design rationale:** The pipeline flows: metric decomposition (TGR-5i0) + angular integrals (TGR-bw9) -> coupling tables (TGR-wbm) -> source assembly (TGR-u19) + first-order master eqs (TGR-xb3) -> second-order master eqs (TGR-22h) -> validation (TGR-2y0). Existing issues TGR-4p8, TGR-68g, TGR-2yl, TGR-31k, TGR-2gv are wired to the new chain.

**Key infrastructure reuse:** ScalarHarmonic, EvenVectorHarmonic, OddVectorHarmonic, EvenTensorHarmonicY/Z, OddTensorHarmonic (src/harmonics/), wigner3j + clebsch_gordan (clebsch_gordan.jl), harmonic_product (Gaunt coefficients), expand_perturbation at order 2 (expand.jl), vacuum_background! (backgrounds.jl), gauge_transformation (gauge.jl).

**Ground truth references:** Brizuela, Martin-Garcia & Tiglio, PRD 80, 024021 (2009); Martel & Poisson, PRD 71, 104003 (2005); Gleiser, Nicasio, Price & Pullin, Phys. Rep. 325, 41 (2000); Zerilli, PRD 2, 2141 (1970); Regge & Wheeler, PR 108, 1063 (1957).
description: Design how to handle second-order black hole perturbation theory, where source terms S^{(2)}_{lm} couple (l_1,m_1) x (l_2,m_2) first-order modes. Key design decisions: (1) representation of tensor spherical harmonics Y^{lm}_{ab} (even/odd parity), (2) mode coupling coefficients from angular integrals of products of harmonics (Clebsch-Gordan / Wigner 3j symbols), (3) how to store the coupled source terms as symbolic expressions with mode labels, (4) interface with existing perturbation infrastructure (expand_perturbation at order 2). The first-order theory gives the Regge-Wheeler (odd) and Zerilli (even) master equations; at second order these gain source terms quadratic in first-order modes. ~50 LOC design doc. Reference: Brizuela, Martin-Garcia & Tiglio, PRD 80, 024021 (2009), arXiv:0903.1134, Sec 2-3; Campanelli & Lousto, PRD 59, 124022 (1999), Sec 2. Test: design document identifies all required angular integral types and coupling coefficient structures.
title: Research: Design second-order BH perturbation mode coupling infrastructure

description: Implement automatic enforcement of trace-free constraints: when a tensor T is registered as trace-free (T^a_a = 0), any expression containing a trace of T should automatically simplify to zero. Extend TensorProperties with is_tracefree::Bool field. Modify the simplify pipeline (specifically contract_metrics or a new trace_enforce step) to detect and eliminate traces of trace-free tensors. Also handle partial traces: if T^{ab}_{cd} is trace-free on (a,c), then T^{ab}_{ad} = 0. Function: set_tracefree!(reg, :T; pairs=[(1,3)]) to specify which index pairs are trace-free. ~80 LOC modifying src/registry.jl + src/algebra/contraction.jl. Reference: xAct-contrib/TraceFree package documentation; Wald, General Relativity (1984), Sec 3.2. Test: Given trace-free Weyl C_{abcd}, simplify(g^{ac} C_{abcd}) returns TScalar(0), matching the Weyl trace-free identity.
title: Constraints: Implement automatic trace-free enforcement in simplify pipeline

close_reason: Flat BR projectors give correct form factors on MSS; the -5Λ offset is the Lichnerowicz mass term. spin_project_mss subtracts it via Vandermonde polynomial extraction.
description: On MSS (R_μν=Λg_μν, d=4), the Lichnerowicz operator on spin-J fields has eigenvalues Δ_L=-k²+c_J·Λ. Fetch arXiv:2406.09500 (Barker, Marzo, Rigouzzo 2024) and/or Bueno-Cano references. Extract c_2 (TT tensor), c_1 (vector), c_0s (scalar), c_0w (trace) in d=4. Also extract the modified transverse projector formula θ̃_{μν} if it differs from flat θ_{μν}=g_{μν}-k_μk_ν/k². Document exact values as ground-truth constants with paper equation numbers. Cross-check: current flat projectors give spin-2(EH)=2.5-5Λ; correct is 2.5. So the spin-2 correction must cancel the -5Λ.
title: Extract dS Lichnerowicz eigenvalue shifts c_J from PSALTer paper

description: Implement parametrized post-Newtonian (PPN) formalism in TensorGR.jl. PPN metric ansatz with 10 parameters (γ, β, ξ, α₁, α₂, α₃, ζ₁, ζ₂, ζ₃, ζ₄), velocity-order expansion, PPN potentials, and order-by-order field equation solving. Validate against GR and Brans-Dicke. Ground truth: Will, 'Theory and Experiment in Gravitational Physics' (2018); Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014). New directory: src/ppn/.
title: Parametrized Post-Newtonian Formalism (xPPN)

description:
Design the parametrized post-Newtonian framework. Key decisions:
- How to implement velocity-order expansion (v/c powers) within existing perturbation engine
- 10 PPN parameters: γ, β, ξ, α₁, α₂, α₃, ζ₁, ζ₂, ζ₃, ζ₄
- Integration with existing pn_order/truncate_pn in src/worldline/
- PPN potentials as abstract tensors vs component expressions
- API: define_ppn_metric!, ppn_params, expand_ppn, ppn_eos
- New directory: src/ppn/

Ground truth: Will, 'Theory and Experiment in Gravitational Physics' (2018), Ch 4; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014).

Acceptance: Design document specifying type hierarchy, API surface, integration points with existing code.
title: Research: Design PPN framework for TensorGR.jl

description:
Implement the standard PPN gauge conditions that fix the coordinate freedom in the PPN expansion. The PPN formalism uses a specific gauge (Will's 'standard PPN gauge') which must be enforced during the order-by-order solving process.

Key gauge conditions (Will 2018, Sec 4.1):
- At O(2): g_{ij}^{(2)} is spatially isotropic: g_{ij}^{(2)} = 2 gamma U delta_{ij}
- At O(3): div condition on g_{0i}^{(3)} fixes the split between V_i and W_i
- At O(4): no additional gauge freedom remains once O(2) and O(3) are fixed

Implementation:
- ppn_gauge_conditions(order) returns vector of constraint equations
- Integrates with existing gauge_transformation in src/perturbation/gauge.jl
- Gauge freedom at each order is a residual coordinate transformation x^a -> x^a + xi^a_{(n)}
- At O(2), spatial isotropy eliminates the anisotropic part of g_{ij}
- The harmonic-like condition partial_j g^{0j}_{(3)} = 0 splits the vector potentials

Design constraints:
- Must NOT break existing gauge.jl infrastructure (gauge_transformation function)
- Should use the existing lie_derivative machinery for Lie derivative of background metric
- Gauge conditions stored as RewriteRules that can be applied during ppn_solve
- The gauge is NOT harmonic gauge -- it is the 'standard post-Newtonian gauge' per Will
- Must handle the subtlety that PPN gauge is defined order-by-order, not globally

Add to src/ppn/gauge.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Sec 4.1, Box 4.1; Weinberg (1972) Sec 9.1.

Acceptance:
- ppn_gauge_conditions(2) returns spatial isotropy constraint
- ppn_gauge_conditions(3) returns vector potential divergence constraint  
- Gauge conditions are compatible with existing RewriteRule engine
title: xPPN: Implement standard PPN gauge conditions and coordinate fixing

description:
Implement an abstract Poisson equation solver for defining PPN potentials via their integral representations. All PPN potentials satisfy Poisson-type equations: nabla^2 U = -4 pi rho, nabla^2 Phi_W = source, etc. The solver does NOT numerically integrate -- it represents the inversion symbolically.

Key design:
- PoissonEquation struct: lhs_potential, source_expr, boundary_conditions
- define_ppn_potential!(reg, :U; source=:(-4pi*rho), ppn_order=2) registers a potential
- poisson_invert(source_expr) -> potential expression (symbolic, not numeric)
- The inversion is stored as a RewriteRule: nabla^2 U -> -4pi rho (and vice versa)
- For iterated Poisson integrals (super-potentials): nabla^2 U_{ij} = -4pi rho (x_i x_j / r^2 - 1/3 delta_{ij})

Potentials and their sources (Will 2018, Eqs 4.4-4.12):
- U: nabla^2 U = -4pi rho
- U_{ij}: nabla^2 U_{ij} defined via integral with directional factor
- Phi_W (Whitehead): nabla^2 from double integral
- Phi_1 through Phi_4: nabla^2 Phi_n = -4pi rho * (source_n)
- A (preferred-frame): nabla^2 A = -4pi rho * (...)
- V_i, W_i: nabla^2 from velocity-weighted integrals

Design constraints:
- This is purely symbolic -- no numeric PDE solving
- Uses TDeriv for the Laplacian: nabla^2 = delta^{ij} partial_i partial_j (spatial)
- Must distinguish spatial Laplacian from spacetime d'Alembertian (existing box())
- Potentials are time-independent at leading order (quasi-static approximation)
- Must integrate with the simplify pipeline: when simplify encounters nabla^2 U, it should be able to substitute -4pi rho via the registered rule

Add to src/ppn/potentials.jl (split from TGR-bgl.5 implementation).

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.4-4.12; MTW Box 39.3.

Acceptance:
- define_ppn_potential! registers potential with Poisson source as RewriteRule
- spatial_laplacian(U) builds delta^{ij} partial_i partial_j U
- simplify(spatial_laplacian(U)) -> -4pi rho via the Poisson rule
- All 10+ PPN potentials can be defined via this interface
title: xPPN: Implement abstract Poisson equation solver for PPN potentials

description:
Implement the standard PPN observables: closed-form expressions for solar system tests as functions of PPN parameters. These are the primary output of the PPN framework -- they connect abstract PPN parameters to measurable quantities.

Observables to implement (Will 2018, Ch 7-8):
1. Perihelion precession: delta_omega = (2+2gamma-beta) * 6pi M / a(1-e^2) per orbit
2. Light deflection: delta_theta = (1+gamma)/2 * 4M/d (impact parameter d)
3. Shapiro time delay: delta_t = (1+gamma) * 2M * ln(4 r_1 r_2 / d^2) 
4. Gravitational redshift: delta_nu/nu = (1+alpha) * Delta U / c^2
5. Nordtvedt effect: eta_N = 4beta - gamma - 3 - 10xi/3 - alpha_1 + 2alpha_2/3 - 2zeta_1/3 - zeta_2/3
6. Geodetic precession: Omega_gp = (gamma + 1/2) * (M/r) per orbit (de Sitter/LT)
7. Preferred-frame effects: orbital polarization ~ alpha_1, alpha_2, alpha_3

API:
- ppn_perihelion(params; M, a, e) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_deflection(params; M, d) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_shapiro_delay(params; M, r1, r2, d) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_nordtvedt_eta(params) -> scalar expression in PPN parameters
- ppn_geodetic_precession(params; M, r) -> symbolic expression
- ppn_preferred_frame_bounds(params) -> Dict of alpha constraints

Design constraints:
- Expressions are closed-form functions of PPNParams, NOT from solving field equations
- These are SEPARATE from the field equation solving -- they parametrize observables
- The Nordtvedt parameter eta = 4beta - gamma - 3 - ... is a derived quantity
- Must return symbolic expressions (TScalar with symbolic content)
- Solar system bounds stored as metadata: gamma-1 < 2.3e-5 (Cassini), beta-1 < 8e-5 (LLR), etc.

Add to src/ppn/observables.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 7.1, Ch 7-8; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014) Tables 3-4.

Acceptance:
- ppn_nordtvedt_eta(PPNParams(:GR)) == 0
- ppn_perihelion expression contains the combination (2+2gamma-beta)
- ppn_deflection expression contains the combination (1+gamma)/2
- All observables reduce to GR predictions when PPNParams() is substituted
title: xPPN: Implement PPN observable expressions (perihelion, deflection, Shapiro, Nordtvedt)

description:
Implement the bridge between abstract tensor expressions and the 3+1 component representation required by the PPN formalism. The PPN metric is defined component-by-component (g_{00}, g_{0i}, g_{ij}) with explicit v/c order assignments, while the existing codebase primarily works with abstract indexed tensors. This issue provides the conversion layer.

Key design:
- ppn_foliation!(reg; manifold=:M4, temporal=0, spatial=[1,2,3]) defines the 3+1 split
  and registers spatial delta, spatial metric, lapse, shift as appropriate for PPN
- Uses existing FoliationProperties from src/foliation/foliation.jl
- ppn_decompose(g_ab) -> (g_00::TensorExpr, g_0i::TensorExpr, g_ij::TensorExpr) splits the metric
- ppn_compose(g_00, g_0i, g_ij) -> g_ab reconstructs the full metric
- Christoffel components: Gamma^a_{bc} auto-computed from g_{00}, g_{0i}, g_{ij} at each v/c order
  using existing metric_christoffel infrastructure from src/components/metric_compute.jl
- Ricci components: Ric_{ab} at each order, using the PPN-truncated Christoffels

Integration points:
1. src/foliation/foliation.jl -- FoliationProperties, split_spacetime, split_all_spacetime
2. src/components/metric_compute.jl -- metric_christoffel, metric_riemann, metric_ricci
3. src/components/ctensor.jl -- CTensor arithmetic for component expressions
4. src/perturbation/backgrounds.jl -- vacuum_background! / maximally_symmetric_background!

Design constraints:
- The PPN expansion is NOT a standard perturbation (no small parameter epsilon). It is an
  asymptotic expansion in v/c where different metric components have different leading orders.
  This means MetricPerturbation from metric_perturbation.jl is NOT directly usable.
- Must handle the order mismatch: g_{00} = -1 + O(2) + O(4), g_{0i} = O(3), g_{ij} = delta_{ij} + O(2)
- Time derivatives introduce additional v/c factors: partial_0 f ~ (v/c) partial_i f for bound systems
- The spatial Laplacian delta^{ij} partial_i partial_j is NOT the spacetime d'Alembertian

Add to src/ppn/decompose.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 4.1; MTW Ch 39.

Acceptance:
- ppn_decompose splits g_{ab} into (g_00, g_0i, g_ij) components
- Christoffel components computed order-by-order agree with Will (2018) Eqs 4.13-4.19
- Integration with existing foliation infrastructure is seamless (uses FoliationProperties)
title: xPPN: Implement PPN-to-component bridge connecting abstract tensors to 3+1 coordinate expressions

description:
Validate the PPN parameter constraint hierarchy from conservation of stress-energy (nabla_b T^{ab} = 0). Will (2018) Ch 4.4 derives that:
- Fully conservative theories: zeta_1 = zeta_2 = zeta_3 = zeta_4 = 0 (total momentum conserved)
- Semi-conservative theories: additionally alpha_3 = 0 (center-of-mass conserved)
- The Whitehead term xi enters only in the preferred-location sector

Tests:
1. Verify is_fully_conservative(PPNParams(:GR)) == true
2. Verify is_semi_conservative(PPNParams(:BransDicke, omega=:w)) == true
3. Verify the 5 conservation law identities (Will 2018, Eqs 4.67-4.71) hold symbolically:
   - Euler equation at O(3): test that zeta_1-2xi, 1+alpha_2-zeta_1+2xi combinations appear correctly
   - Energy equation at O(4): test that zeta-parameter combinations vanish for conservative theories
4. Verify Nordtvedt eta = 0 for fully conservative + Lorentz-invariant theories (alpha_1=alpha_2=0)
5. Check that 10 PPN parameters reduce to 2 free parameters (gamma, beta) for fully conservative,
   Lorentz-invariant theories (as expected for most viable theories)

This validates the theoretical consistency of the PPN framework, independent of any specific theory.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Sec 4.4, Table 4.2; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014) Sec 3.3.

Acceptance:
- All 5 conservation law identities verified symbolically
- is_fully_conservative, is_semi_conservative predicates tested
- Constraint hierarchy: 10 params -> 6 (conservative) -> 5 (semi-conservative) -> 2 (GR-like) verified
title: xPPN Validation: Conservation laws and the 10-parameter constraint hierarchy

description:
Validate PPN framework against general scalar-tensor gravity (Bergmann-Wagoner / Horndeski subclass). This extends the Brans-Dicke test (TGR-bgl.8) to the full scalar-tensor sector. Expected PPN parameters for a general scalar-tensor theory with coupling function omega(phi):

- gamma = (1 + omega)/(2 + omega)
- beta = 1 + (d omega/d phi) / ((2+omega)^2 (3+2*omega))
- All other parameters = 0 (fully conservative, no preferred frame)

This is the most important validation beyond GR and Brans-Dicke because:
1. It tests the scalar field perturbation expansion (phi = phi_0 + delta_phi)
2. It tests the nonlinear beta parameter (requires O(4) in g_{00})
3. It connects to the existing scalar-tensor infrastructure (TGR-ada / DHOST epic)
4. beta != 1 means this theory has a genuine PPN difference from GR at O(4)

Additional tests:
- Verify gamma -> 1 as omega -> infinity (GR limit)
- Verify beta -> 1 as d(omega)/d(phi) -> 0
- Verify PPNParams(:BransDicke, omega=:w) matches the omega=constant subcase
- Verify ppn_nordtvedt_eta = (1+omega)^{-1} * d(omega)/d(phi) / (3+2*omega) for general case

Ground truth: Will (2018) Sec 5.3, Eqs 5.32-5.33; Damour & Esposito-Farese, CQG 9, 2093 (1992).

Acceptance:
- ppn_solve on scalar-tensor field equations returns symbolic gamma = (1+omega)/(2+omega)
- ppn_solve returns symbolic beta with correct d(omega)/d(phi) dependence
- GR limit (omega -> infinity) gives gamma = beta = 1 after simplification
- Brans-Dicke subcase (d(omega)/d(phi) = 0) gives beta = 1
title: xPPN Validation: Scalar-tensor theory PPN parameters (Horndeski subclass)

description:
Implement the standard PPN metric ansatz:
- g₀₀ = -1 + 2U - 2βU² + 2ξΦ_W + (2γ+2+α₃+ζ₁-2ξ)Φ₁ + 2(3γ-2β+1+ζ₂+ξ)Φ₂ + 2(1+ζ₃)Φ₃ + 2(3γ+3ζ₄-2ξ)Φ₄ - (ζ₁-2ξ)𝒜
- g₀ᵢ = -½(4γ+3+α₁-α₂+ζ₁-2ξ)Vᵢ - ½(1+α₂-ζ₁+2ξ)Wᵢ
- gᵢⱼ = (1+2γU)δᵢⱼ

Build as abstract tensor expressions with PPN parameters as symbolic scalars. Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.1-4.3.

Acceptance: ppn_metric(params) returns (g00, g0i, gij) as TensorExpr. Parameters appear symbolically.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN metric ansatz

description:
Implement PPNParams struct holding all 10 PPN parameters with default GR values:
- γ = 1 (space curvature per unit mass)
- β = 1 (nonlinearity in gravity)
- ξ = 0 (preferred-location effects)
- α₁ = α₂ = α₃ = 0 (preferred-frame effects)
- ζ₁ = ζ₂ = ζ₃ = ζ₄ = 0 (conservation law violation)

Include named constructors for common theories: PPNParams(:GR), PPNParams(:BransDicke, omega=ω). Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 4.1.

Acceptance: PPNParams() gives GR defaults. is_fully_conservative(p) checks ζ's = 0. is_preferred_frame_free(p) checks α's = 0.
title: xPPN: Implement PPNParams type with GR defaults

description:
Implement velocity-order (v/c) expansion for tensors. Extend existing pn_order/truncate_pn from src/worldline/. Key:
- Track v/c order of each quantity: U ~ O(2), v^i ~ O(1), p ~ O(2), etc.
- Expand metric, Christoffel, Ricci to required order
- Truncate at specified order: truncate_ppn(expr, order)
- g₀₀ needs through O(4), g₀ᵢ through O(3), gᵢⱼ through O(2)

Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 4 (order counting); existing pn_order/truncate_pn API.

Acceptance: ppn_order(expr) returns v/c order. truncate_ppn(expr, n) drops terms above order n. Consistent with existing worldline PN infrastructure.
title: xPPN: Implement velocity-order expansion

description:
Register PPN potential tensors with appropriate properties:
- U: Newtonian potential (scalar, O(2))
- Uᵢⱼ: super-potential (symmetric rank-2 spatial, O(2))
- Φ_W: Whitehead potential (scalar, O(4))
- Φ₁, Φ₂, Φ₃, Φ₄: post-Newtonian potentials (scalar, O(4))
- 𝒜: preferred-frame potential (scalar, O(4))
- Vᵢ, Wᵢ: vector potentials (spatial vector, O(3))

Each defined with integral representations (Poisson-type integrals over matter source). Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.4-4.12.

Acceptance: All potentials registered with correct v/c order, symmetry. Integral representations stored as metadata.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN potentials (U, Uᵢⱼ, Φ_W, Φ₁-Φ₄, 𝒜, Vᵢ, Wᵢ)

description:
Implement order-by-order solving of field equations in the PPN framework. Given a gravitational theory (action or field equations), solve for the metric order-by-order in v/c:
1. At O(2): solve for g₀₀^(2) and gᵢⱼ^(2) in terms of U
2. At O(3): solve for g₀ᵢ^(3) in terms of Vᵢ, Wᵢ
3. At O(4): solve for g₀₀^(4) in terms of Φ's
4. Read off PPN parameters from coefficients

Use existing solve_tensors infrastructure. Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 5.

Acceptance: ppn_solve(field_eqs, matter_source; order=4) returns PPNParams. Correct for GR field equations.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN field equations (order-by-order solving)

description:
Validate PPN framework against Einstein's general relativity. Starting from EH action S = ∫R√(-g)d⁴x (or equivalently G_{μν} = 8πT_{μν}), solve for PPN parameters. Expected:
- γ = 1
- β = 1
- ξ = α₁ = α₂ = α₃ = 0
- ζ₁ = ζ₂ = ζ₃ = ζ₄ = 0

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 4.1. STRING MATCH on all 10 parameters.

Acceptance: ppn_solve on GR field equations returns PPNParams matching all 10 GR values exactly.
title: xPPN Validation: GR PPN parameters (γ=β=1, rest=0)

description:
Validate PPN framework against Brans-Dicke theory. Action: S = ∫[φR - (ω/φ)(∂φ)² + L_m]√(-g)d⁴x. Solve for PPN parameters. Expected:
- γ = (1+ω)/(2+ω)
- β = 1
- All others = 0 (fully conservative, no preferred frame)

Note: γ → 1 as ω → ∞ (GR limit). Current solar system bound: ω > 40000 (Cassini).

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eq 5.23; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014). STRING MATCH on γ and β.

Acceptance: ppn_solve on Brans-Dicke field equations returns γ = (1+ω)/(2+ω), β = 1 symbolically.
title: xPPN Validation: Brans-Dicke PPN parameters

description:
Implement the PPN matter source: a perfect fluid stress-energy tensor T^{ab} with density rho, specific internal energy Pi, pressure p, and coordinate velocity v^i. Required because all PPN potentials are integrals over the matter source.

Key types and functions:
- PPNMatterSource struct: holds symbols for rho, Pi, p, v^i and their velocity orders
- define_ppn_matter!(reg; density=:rho, pressure=:p, internal_energy=:Pi, velocity=:v)
- Registers rho as scalar O(0), p ~ O(rho*v^2) = O(2), Pi ~ O(v^2) = O(2)
- Registers v^i as spatial vector O(1)
- Builds T^{00} = rho(1 + Pi + v^2 + 2U), T^{0i} = rho v^i, T^{ij} = rho v^i v^j + p delta^{ij}
- Computes T = g_{ab} T^{ab} (trace)

Design constraints:
- Must integrate with existing register_tensor! infrastructure
- v/c order must be tracked as metadata on each tensor (new :ppn_order option key)
- Density rho is the conserved rest-mass density (rho* = rho sqrt(-g) u^0)
- The coordinate velocity v^i = dx^i/dt is NOT the four-velocity u^a. Relation: u^a = u^0(1, v^i)
- All v/c order counting follows Will (2018) Ch 4.2: T^{00} through O(4), T^{0i} through O(3), T^{ij} through O(2)

Add to src/ppn/matter.jl.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.47-4.55; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014) Sec 3.2.

Acceptance:
- define_ppn_matter! registers all matter tensors with correct v/c order metadata
- T^{ab} expression generated with correct velocity-order structure
- Integrates with existing registry, e.g. has_tensor(reg, :rho) == true after setup
title: xPPN: Implement perfect fluid stress-energy tensor and matter source infrastructure

description: Validation: verify that the massive spin-2 mode in bimetric gravity on a de Sitter background satisfies the Higuchi bound m^2 >= 2 Lambda / 3. Below this bound, the helicity-0 mode of the massive graviton becomes a ghost (negative kinetic energy). Procedure: (1) set up bimetric theory on proportional dS backgrounds with cosmological constant Lambda, (2) compute the mass eigenstate mass m^2 from the beta parameters, (3) compute the kinetic matrix for all helicity components, (4) verify that the helicity-0 kinetic term flips sign when m^2 < 2 Lambda/3. Ground truth: Higuchi, Nucl. Phys. B 282, 397 (1987); Hassan, Schmidt-May & von Strauss, PLB 715, 335 (2012), arXiv:1203.5283. Test: STRING MATCH — at the Higuchi bound m^2 = 2 Lambda/3, the helicity-0 kinetic coefficient equals exactly zero, confirming the partially massless point. Coefficient is proportional to (m^2 - 2 Lambda/3).
title: Bimetric Validation: Verify Higuchi bound m^2 >= 2 Lambda/3 for massive spin-2 on de Sitter

description: Implement scalar-tensor gravity parametrization in new src/scalar_tensor/ directory. Horndeski theory with 4 free functions G_2..G_5(phi,X), Lagrangian construction, field equations, beyond-Horndeski (GLPV) extensions, Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization, quadratic action on FRW. Validation: f(R) as Horndeski subcase. Ground truth: Horndeski, Int. J. Theor. Phys. 10, 363 (1974); Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), arXiv:1901.04778. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: xIST/COPPER Epic 6: Scalar-Tensor Theory

close_reason: Design document complete. Produced comprehensive type hierarchy (ScalarTensorFunction, HorndeskiTheory, BelliniSawickiAlphas, FRWBackground), file layout (src/scalar_tensor/ with 6 files), integration plan with existing box.jl/curvature.jl/perturbation/quadratic_action infrastructure, and detailed tensor expressions for all 4 Horndeski Lagrangians L_2..L_5 with Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778 equation references. Updated all 6 child issues (TGR-ble.2..7) with precise tensor formulas, API specifications, test criteria, and dependency chain.
description:
DESIGN DOCUMENT: Horndeski/scalar-tensor theory parametrization for TensorGR.jl

## 1. Physics Overview

Horndeski theory (1974) is the most general scalar-tensor theory with second-order equations of motion in 4D. The action is S = integral d^4x sqrt(-g) (L_2 + L_3 + L_4 + L_5) where the four Lagrangians depend on a scalar field phi, its kinetic term X = -(1/2) g^{ab} nabla_a(phi) nabla_b(phi), and the metric g_{ab}:

  L_2 = G_2(phi, X)
  L_3 = -G_3(phi, X) Box(phi)
  L_4 = G_4(phi, X) R + G_{4,X}(phi, X) [(Box phi)^2 - (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2]
  L_5 = G_5(phi, X) G_{ab} nabla^a nabla^b phi
        - (1/6) G_{5,X}(phi, X) [(Box phi)^3 - 3 Box(phi) (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2
                                   + 2 (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^3]

Here G_{i,X} = dG_i/dX, G_{i,phi} = dG_i/dphi, and Box(phi) = g^{ab} nabla_a nabla_b phi.

Ground truth: Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), arXiv:1901.04778, Eqs 2.1-2.4.

Special cases:
  - GR: G_2 = -2*Lambda, G_3 = 0, G_4 = M_Pl^2/2, G_5 = 0
  - Brans-Dicke: G_2 = (omega_BD/phi)*X, G_3 = 0, G_4 = phi/2, G_5 = 0
  - Quintessence: G_2 = X - V(phi), G_3 = G_5 = 0, G_4 = M_Pl^2/2
  - f(R): G_2 = f - Rf', G_3 = 0, G_4 = f'/2, G_5 = 0, X = 0 (Kobayashi Sec 2.3)
  - Cubic galileon: G_2 = -c_2 X, G_3 = -c_3 X / M^3, G_4 = M_Pl^2/2, G_5 = 0

## 2. Type Design

### 2.1 Scalar Field (new type vs existing Tensor)

Decision: Use Tensor(:phi, TIndex[]) for the scalar field phi. This is a rank-0 tensor, consistent with the existing AST. The kinetic term X is built from existing box.jl primitives:

  X = -(1//2) * grad_squared(Tensor(:phi, TIndex[]), :g)

This uses existing grad_squared(field, metric) which produces g^{ab} d_a(phi) d_b(phi). No new types needed for the scalar field itself.

Registration:
  register_tensor!(reg, TensorProperties(name=:phi, manifold=:M4, rank=(0,0),
      symmetries=SymmetrySpec[], options=Dict(:is_scalar_field => true)))

### 2.2 Abstract Functions G_i(phi, X) and Derivatives

New type: ScalarTensorFunction

  struct ScalarTensorFunction
      name::Symbol          # :G2, :G3, :G4, :G5
      phi_derivs::Int       # number of phi-derivatives (0 = G_i, 1 = G_{i,phi}, etc.)
      X_derivs::Int         # number of X-derivatives (0 = G_i, 1 = G_{i,X}, etc.)
  end

Instances: G2 = ScalarTensorFunction(:G2, 0, 0), G4X = ScalarTensorFunction(:G4, 0, 1), G5XX = ScalarTensorFunction(:G5, 0, 2).

Each ScalarTensorFunction is registered as a rank-0 tensor in the registry:

  G2_name = :G2           # for ScalarTensorFunction(:G2, 0, 0)
  G4X_name = :G4_X        # for ScalarTensorFunction(:G4, 0, 1)
  G5XX_name = :G5_XX      # for ScalarTensorFunction(:G5, 0, 2)

These are wrapped as Tensor(G4X_name, TIndex[]) in expressions, i.e., they participate in TProduct/TSum as opaque scalars. This avoids modifying the core TensorExpr type hierarchy.

Derivative helper: differentiate_G(stf, :phi) and differentiate_G(stf, :X) return a new ScalarTensorFunction with incremented derivative count, plus the corresponding registered tensor name.

### 2.3 HorndeskiTheory Container

  struct HorndeskiTheory
      manifold::Symbol       # e.g. :M4
      metric::Symbol         # e.g. :g
      scalar_field::Symbol   # e.g. :phi
      G_functions::NTuple{4, ScalarTensorFunction}  # (G2, G3, G4, G5)
      covd::Symbol           # covariant derivative name
  end

Constructor: define_horndeski!(reg; manifold, metric, scalar_field, covd) registers all necessary tensors (phi, G2, G3, G4, G5, plus their needed derivatives G3_phi, G4_X, G4_phi, G5_X, G5_phi, G5_XX) and returns HorndeskiTheory.

### 2.4 Lagrangian Construction

Each L_i is a function returning a TensorExpr:

  horndeski_L2(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr  # G_2(phi, X) as Tensor(:G2, [])
  horndeski_L3(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr  # -G_3 * box(phi)
  horndeski_L4(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr  # G_4*R + G_{4X}*[...]
  horndeski_L5(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr  # G_5*G_{ab}*nabla^a nabla^b phi - ...

These use existing infrastructure:
  - box(field, metric) from src/gr/box.jl for Box(phi) = g^{ab} nabla_a nabla_b phi
  - grad_squared(field, metric) for (nabla phi)^2
  - covd_chain(field, idxs) for nabla_a nabla_b phi
  - Tensor(:RicScalar, []) for R
  - Tensor(:Ein, [idx_a, idx_b]) for G_{ab}

Key tensor structures needed in L_4 and L_5:
  (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2 = g^{ac} g^{bd} (nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla_c nabla_d phi)
  (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^3 = g^{ad} g^{be} g^{cf} (nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla_d nabla_e phi)(nabla_c nabla_f phi) -- 3-fold contraction -- NOT trace(M^3), but nabla_a nabla^b phi nabla_b nabla^c phi nabla_c nabla^a phi

### 2.5 Perturbation Interface

Scalar field perturbation: phi = phi_0 + epsilon * delta_phi, using existing define_tensor_perturbation!(reg, :phi, :dphi). Background: phi_0 is constant (or time-dependent for cosmological backgrounds).

Metric perturbation: g_{ab} = g_0_{ab} + epsilon * h_{ab}, using existing define_metric_perturbation!.

The G_i functions on the background are just scalar constants (evaluated at background phi_0, X_0). Their perturbations follow from the chain rule:
  delta(G_i) = G_{i,phi} * delta_phi + G_{i,X} * delta_X
where delta_X = -g^{ab} nabla_a(phi_0) nabla_b(delta_phi) + (1/2) h^{ab} nabla_a(phi_0) nabla_b(phi_0) on a non-trivial background.

### 2.6 Bellini-Sawicki Alpha Parametrization

On an FRW background g_{ab} = diag(-1, a^2, a^2, a^2) with time-dependent phi_0(t), the four alpha functions are (Kobayashi Sec 5.1, Eqs 5.6-5.9; Bellini & Sawicki JCAP 1407 (2014) 050):

  M_*^2 = 2(G_4 - 2X*G_{4X} + X*G_{5phi} - H*X_dot*G_{5X})
  alpha_M = (1/H) d(ln M_*^2)/dt
  alpha_K = (2X/H^2 M_*^2) (G_{2X} + 2X*G_{2XX} + 12H*phi_dot*(G_{3X} + X*G_{3XX})
            - 12H^2*(G_{4X} + 8X*G_{4XX} + 4X^2*G_{4XXX}) + ...)
  alpha_B = (phi_dot/(H M_*^2)) (G_{3phi} + X*(G_{3X} + 2G_{3phiX}) + ...)
  alpha_T = (2X/M_*^2) (2G_{4X} - 2G_{5phi} - (ddot_phi - H*dot_phi)*G_{5X})

These are time-dependent scalars on the FRW background.

Type:
  struct BelliniSawickiAlphas
      alpha_M::Any   # scalar expression (Expr tree or Symbolics.Num)
      alpha_K::Any
      alpha_B::Any
      alpha_T::Any
      M_star_sq::Any
  end

### 2.7 File Layout

  src/scalar_tensor/
    types.jl                  # ScalarTensorFunction, HorndeskiTheory, BelliniSawickiAlphas
    horndeski.jl              # L_2 through L_5 construction, define_horndeski!
    horndeski_eom.jl          # metric + scalar field equations
    beyond_horndeski.jl       # GLPV L_4^bH, L_5^bH with F_4, F_5
    alpha_params.jl           # Bellini-Sawicki alpha computation
    quadratic_action_st.jl    # Quadratic action on FRW in alpha-parametrization

  test/scalar_tensor/
    test_horndeski.jl         # Lagrangian construction, special cases
    test_horndeski_eom.jl     # Field equations, GR limit
    test_beyond_horndeski.jl  # GLPV, recovery of Horndeski
    test_alpha_params.jl      # Alpha computation, GR limit, f(R) limit
    test_fR_validation.jl     # f(R) as Horndeski subcase
    test_quadratic_action.jl  # Quadratic action, stability conditions

## 3. Integration Points with Existing Code

- src/gr/box.jl: box(), grad_squared(), covd_chain() -- used directly for kinetic structures
- src/gr/curvature.jl: Tensor(:RicScalar, []), Tensor(:Ein, [a,b]) -- used in L_4, L_5
- src/perturbation/metric_perturbation.jl: define_metric_perturbation! -- for g -> g + h
- src/perturbation/metric_perturbation.jl: define_tensor_perturbation! -- for phi -> phi_0 + dphi
- src/perturbation/expand.jl: expand_perturbation -- for quadratic action computation
- src/perturbation/variation.jl: variational_derivative, euler_lagrange -- for EOM derivation
- src/action/quadratic_action.jl: QuadraticForm -- for stability analysis output
- src/algebra/derivatives.jl: expand_derivatives -- Leibniz rule on products
- src/gr/covd.jl: define_covd!, covd_to_christoffel -- covariant derivatives on scalar field
- src/perturbation/backgrounds.jl: maximally_symmetric_background! -- background rules pattern

## 4. Issue Chain Summary

TGR-ble.1 (this issue) -> design doc (DONE)
TGR-ble.2 -> ScalarTensorFunction type + define_horndeski! + L_2..L_5 (~120 LOC)
TGR-ble.3 -> Horndeski field equations E_{ab}=0, E_phi=0 (~120 LOC)
TGR-ble.4 -> Beyond-Horndeski GLPV extensions F_4, F_5 (~100 LOC)
TGR-ble.5 -> Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization (~100 LOC)
TGR-ble.6 -> Quadratic action on FRW background (~120 LOC)
TGR-ble.7 -> Validation: f(R) as Horndeski subcase (~80 LOC)
title: xIST: Research Horndeski theory parametrization design

description:
Implement the core types and Lagrangian construction for Horndeski scalar-tensor theory.

## New Types (src/scalar_tensor/types.jl)

1. ScalarTensorFunction: represents abstract functions G_i(phi, X) and their derivatives.

  struct ScalarTensorFunction
      name::Symbol          # :G2, :G3, :G4, :G5
      phi_derivs::Int       # number of phi-derivatives
      X_derivs::Int         # number of X-derivatives
  end

  Naming convention: G4 -> :G4, G4_X -> :G4_X, G4_XX -> :G4_XX, G4_phi -> :G4_phi, G4_phiX -> :G4_phiX
  differentiate_G(stf::ScalarTensorFunction, :X) -> new ScalarTensorFunction with X_derivs+1
  differentiate_G(stf::ScalarTensorFunction, :phi) -> new ScalarTensorFunction with phi_derivs+1
  g_tensor_name(stf) -> Symbol for registry (e.g. :G4_X)

2. HorndeskiTheory: container linking manifold, metric, scalar field, and G-functions.

  struct HorndeskiTheory
      manifold::Symbol
      metric::Symbol
      scalar_field::Symbol
      G_functions::NTuple{4, ScalarTensorFunction}
      covd::Symbol
  end

3. define_horndeski!(reg; manifold, metric, scalar_field=:phi, covd=:nabla) -> HorndeskiTheory
   Registers: scalar field phi (rank-0 tensor), all G_i and needed derivatives as rank-0 tensors,
   kinetic term X (optional convenience), covariant derivative (if not already defined).
   Required derivatives to register: G2, G3, G3_phi, G4, G4_X, G4_phi, G5, G5_X, G5_phi, G5_XX.

## Lagrangian Functions (src/scalar_tensor/horndeski.jl)

Four functions returning TensorExpr, using existing box.jl and curvature.jl primitives:

1. horndeski_L2(ht; registry) -> Tensor(:G2, TIndex[])
   L_2 = G_2(phi, X). Simply the abstract G2 scalar.
   Kobayashi Eq 2.1.

2. horndeski_L3(ht; registry) -> -Tensor(:G3, []) * box(Tensor(:phi, []), metric)
   L_3 = -G_3(phi, X) * Box(phi) where Box(phi) = g^{ab} nabla_a nabla_b phi.
   Uses box(field, metric) from src/gr/box.jl.
   Kobayashi Eq 2.2.

3. horndeski_L4(ht; registry) -> TensorExpr
   L_4 = G_4 * R + G_{4,X} * [(Box phi)^2 - (nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^a nabla^b phi)]
   where:
     (nabla_a nabla_b phi)(nabla^a nabla^b phi) = g^{ac} g^{bd} (d_a d_b phi)(d_c d_d phi)
   Uses: Tensor(:RicScalar, []) for R, box() for Box(phi), covd_chain() for d_a d_b phi.
   The squared second-derivative term needs 4 fresh indices {a,b,c,d} with metric contractions.
   Kobayashi Eq 2.3.

4. horndeski_L5(ht; registry) -> TensorExpr
   L_5 = G_5 * G_{ab} nabla^a nabla^b phi
         - (1/6) G_{5,X} * [(Box phi)^3 - 3 Box(phi) (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2
                              + 2 (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^3]
   where:
     G_{ab} nabla^a nabla^b phi = Ein^{ab} * d_a d_b phi (needs 2 fresh contracted indices)
     (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^3 = nabla_a nabla^b phi nabla_b nabla^c phi nabla_c nabla^a phi
       = g^{ad} g^{be} g^{cf} (d_a d_b phi)(d_d d_e phi)(d_c d_f phi)
     Needs 6 fresh indices for the triple contraction.
   Kobayashi Eq 2.4.

5. horndeski_lagrangian(ht; registry) -> L_2 + L_3 + L_4 + L_5
   Full Horndeski Lagrangian density.

## Kinetic Term Helper

kinetic_X(scalar_field, metric; registry) -> TensorExpr
  X = -(1//2) * grad_squared(Tensor(scalar_field, []), metric)
  = -(1//2) * g^{ab} d_a(phi) d_b(phi)

## Tests (test/scalar_tensor/test_horndeski.jl)

1. GR limit: set G2 = -2*Lambda (via rule), G3 = G5 = 0 (vanishing), G4 = kappa (constant, G4_X = 0).
   Then L_total = kappa*R - 2*Lambda (cosmological Einstein-Hilbert). Verify L_3 = L_5 = 0 and
   L_4 = kappa * R (since G4_X = 0, the second term vanishes).
2. Quintessence: G2 = X - V(phi), G4 = M_Pl^2/2, G3 = G5 = 0. Verify L = M_Pl^2/2 * R + X - V(phi).
3. Index structure: all Lagrangians are rank-0 (scalar densities), no free indices.
4. ScalarTensorFunction derivative counting: differentiate_G(:G4, :X) gives (:G4, 0, 1).

File: src/scalar_tensor/types.jl (~40 LOC), src/scalar_tensor/horndeski.jl (~80 LOC).
Total: ~120 LOC + ~60 LOC tests.
Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Eqs 2.1-2.4.
title: xIST: Implement Horndeski Lagrangian types L_2 through L_5

close_reason: Implemented with Kobayashi (2019) Eqs 2.5-2.7, GR limit verified
description:
Implement the Horndeski field equations: metric EOM E_{ab} = 0 and scalar EOM E_phi = 0.

## Physics

Horndeski's key result (1974): despite containing second derivatives of phi in the Lagrangian,
the equations of motion are SECOND-ORDER in both g_{ab} and phi. This is the defining property.

The metric equation E_{ab} = 0 is obtained by varying S = integral sqrt(-g) L d^4x w.r.t. g^{ab}:
  E_{ab} = sum_{i=2}^{5} E^{(i)}_{ab} = 0

The scalar equation E_phi = 0 is obtained by varying w.r.t. phi:
  E_phi = sum_{i=2}^{5} J^{(i)} = 0

Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Eqs 2.5-2.6 (metric), Eq 2.7 (scalar).

## Implementation (src/scalar_tensor/horndeski_eom.jl)

### Approach 1: Direct variational derivative (preferred)

Use existing variational_derivative(lagrangian, field) from src/perturbation/variation.jl:

  horndeski_metric_eom(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr
    = sum of metric_variation(L_i, metric, down(:a), down(:b)) for i in 2:5
    Returns E_{ab} as a TensorExpr with free indices (a, b).

  horndeski_scalar_eom(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr
    = sum of variational_derivative(L_i, scalar_field) for i in 2:5
    Returns E_phi as a scalar TensorExpr.

Challenge: The metric_variation function in variation.jl needs to handle:
  - Variation of R w.r.t. g^{ab}: delta(R)/delta(g^{ab}) = R_{ab} (Palatini identity)
  - Variation of G_{ab} w.r.t. g^{cd}: needs second-order variation
  - Variation of g^{ab} factors in X = -(1/2) g^{ab} d_a phi d_b phi
  - Variation of Box(phi) = g^{ab} nabla_a nabla_b phi w.r.t. g^{ab}

### Approach 2: Direct construction from known formulas

Alternatively, hardcode the known EOM expressions from Kobayashi Eqs 2.5-2.7.
This is more robust but less general. The L_2 contribution to E_{ab} is:

  E^{(2)}_{ab} = (1/2) G_{2,X} nabla_a phi nabla_b phi + (1/2) G_2 g_{ab}

The L_3 contribution:
  E^{(3)}_{ab} = (1/2) G_{3,X} Box(phi) nabla_a phi nabla_b phi
                 - (1/2) G_{3,X} nabla_a phi nabla^c phi nabla_c nabla_b phi (+ symmetrize)
                 + (1/2) G_{3,phi} nabla_a nabla_b phi (+ terms)

The L_4 and L_5 contributions are long but known explicitly.

Recommendation: Use Approach 2 (known formulas) for E_{ab} because the variational derivative
of L_4 and L_5 w.r.t. the metric is very intricate (involves Gauss-Bonnet type terms).
Use Approach 1 (variational_derivative) for E_phi since it is more straightforward.

### API

  horndeski_metric_eom(ht::HorndeskiTheory; idx_a=down(:a), idx_b=down(:b), registry) -> TensorExpr
  horndeski_scalar_eom(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> TensorExpr
  horndeski_eom(ht::HorndeskiTheory; registry) -> (E_ab, E_phi)

## Tests (test/scalar_tensor/test_horndeski_eom.jl)

1. GR limit: G4 = kappa, others = 0. Metric EOM = kappa * Ein_{ab} (Einstein tensor). ~5 lines.
2. Quintessence: G2 = X - V(phi), G4 = M_Pl^2/2. Metric EOM should include scalar stress-energy.
3. Scalar EOM for quintessence: Box(phi) - V'(phi) = 0 (Klein-Gordon equation).
4. Symmetry: E_{ab} = E_{ba} (symmetric tensor equation).
5. Second-order check: E_{ab} and E_phi contain at most second derivatives of g and phi.

File: src/scalar_tensor/horndeski_eom.jl (~120 LOC + ~60 LOC tests).
Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Eqs 2.5-2.7 and Appendix A.
title: xIST: Implement Horndeski field equations (metric + scalar EOMs)

description:
Implement beyond-Horndeski (GLPV) extensions: two additional Lagrangian pieces with free functions F_4, F_5.

## Physics

Gleyzes-Langlois-Piazza-Vernizzi (GLPV, 2015) extended Horndeski theory with two additional
Lagrangians that yield higher-order EOMs but still propagate only 3 DOF (1 scalar + 2 tensor).
The key insight is that certain higher-derivative terms are degenerate (constraints remove
the Ostrogradsky ghost).

  L_4^{bH} = F_4(phi, X) epsilon^{abce} epsilon^{dfg}_{e} nabla_a(phi) nabla_d(phi)
              nabla_b nabla_f(phi) nabla_c nabla_g(phi)

  L_5^{bH} = F_5(phi, X) epsilon^{abcd} epsilon^{efgh} nabla_a(phi) nabla_e(phi)
              nabla_b nabla_f(phi) nabla_c nabla_g(phi) nabla_d nabla_h(phi)

Using the epsilon identity epsilon^{abce} epsilon_{dfge} = -det(delta^{a,b,c}_{d,f,g}), these
can be rewritten as:

  L_4^{bH} = F_4(phi, X) [2(Box phi)^2 nabla^a phi nabla^b phi nabla_a nabla_b phi
              - (Box phi)(nabla^a phi nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2 ...]
  (expanded form from Kobayashi Eq 2.13)

  L_5^{bH} = F_5(phi, X) [...]
  (expanded form from Kobayashi Eq 2.14)

Ground truth: Gleyzes et al, PRL 114, 211101 (2015); Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 2.2, Eqs 2.13-2.14.

## Implementation (src/scalar_tensor/beyond_horndeski.jl)

### Types

  struct BeyondHorndeskiTheory
      horndeski::HorndeskiTheory    # base Horndeski part
      F4::ScalarTensorFunction      # F_4(phi, X)
      F5::ScalarTensorFunction      # F_5(phi, X)
  end

  define_beyond_horndeski!(reg, ht::HorndeskiTheory; F4=:F4, F5=:F5) -> BeyondHorndeskiTheory
  Registers F_4 and F_5 as rank-0 tensors, plus needed derivatives F4_X, F5_X.

### Lagrangian Functions

  beyond_horndeski_L4(bht; registry) -> TensorExpr
    Constructs L_4^{bH} using epsilon tensors (from exterior calculus, if available)
    or the expanded determinant form. Needs Levi-Civita tensor epsilon^{abcd}.
    IMPORTANT: Must register the Levi-Civita tensor for the manifold if not already present.
    Alternative: use the determinant expansion to avoid needing epsilon explicitly.

  beyond_horndeski_L5(bht; registry) -> TensorExpr
    Constructs L_5^{bH} similarly.

  beyond_horndeski_lagrangian(bht; registry) -> L_Horn + L_4^bH + L_5^bH
    Full beyond-Horndeski Lagrangian.

### Epsilon Tensor Handling

The Levi-Civita tensor epsilon^{abcd} is a totally antisymmetric rank-4 tensor density.
It may already be registered for the manifold (check exterior calculus module src/exterior/).
If not, register as:
  register_tensor!(reg, TensorProperties(name=:eps, manifold=manifold, rank=(4,0),
      symmetries=[TotallyAntiSymmetric(1,2,3,4)], options=Dict(:is_levi_civita => true)))

For the expanded form without epsilon, use the identity:
  epsilon^{abce} epsilon_{dfge} = -6 delta^{[a}_d delta^{b}_f delta^{c]}_g
This gives purely metric-based expressions.

## Tests (test/scalar_tensor/test_beyond_horndeski.jl)

1. F_4 = F_5 = 0 (vanishing): beyond_horndeski_lagrangian recovers horndeski_lagrangian.
2. Index structure: L_4^bH and L_5^bH are scalar densities (no free indices).
3. Degenerate higher-order gravity (DHOST): L_4^bH with specific F_4 gives known DHOST action.
4. Type hierarchy: BeyondHorndeskiTheory contains HorndeskiTheory.

File: src/scalar_tensor/beyond_horndeski.jl (~100 LOC + ~40 LOC tests).
Ground truth: Gleyzes et al, PRL 114, 211101 (2015); Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 2.2.
title: xIST: Implement beyond-Horndeski (GLPV) extensions

close_reason: Implemented with Bellini-Sawicki (2014) Eqs 3.7-3.10, GR/BD/f(R)/galileon limits verified
description:
Implement the Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization for linear cosmological perturbations in Horndeski theory.

## Physics

On a spatially flat FRW background ds^2 = -dt^2 + a(t)^2 delta_{ij} dx^i dx^j with homogeneous
scalar field phi_0(t), the linearized perturbation dynamics of Horndeski theory are fully captured
by four time-dependent functions (Bellini & Sawicki 2014; Kobayashi Sec 5.1):

  M_*^2 = 2(G_4 - 2X G_{4,X} + X G_{5,phi} - H dot{phi} X G_{5,X})     (effective Planck mass)

  alpha_M = (1/(H M_*^2)) dM_*^2/dt                                       (running of Planck mass)
  alpha_K = (2X/(H^2 M_*^2)) [G_{2,X} + 2X G_{2,XX}                      (kineticity)
             + 12H dot{phi} (G_{3,X} + X G_{3,XX})
             - 12H^2 (G_{4,X} + 8X G_{4,XX} + 4X^2 G_{4,XXX})
             + 12H^3 dot{phi} (3G_{5,X} + 7X G_{5,XX} + 2X^2 G_{5,XXX})]
  alpha_B = (dot{phi}/(H M_*^2)) [G_{3,phi} + X(G_{3,phiX} + 2G_{3,XX})  (braiding)
             + 6H dot{phi} (G_{4,phiX} + 2X G_{4,XX} - G_{4,phi})/(dot{phi})
             - 2H (G_{4,X} + 2X G_{4,XX} - G_{5,phi} - X G_{5,phiX})
             + H^2 dot{phi} (3G_{5,X} + 2X G_{5,XX})]
  alpha_T = (2X/(M_*^2)) [2G_{4,X} - 2G_{5,phi}                          (tensor speed excess)
             - (ddot{phi} - H dot{phi}) G_{5,X}]

Here X = (1/2) dot{phi}^2 on FRW (since nabla_i phi = 0), H = dot{a}/a is the Hubble parameter.

Physical meaning:
  - alpha_M: time variation of gravitational coupling (GW luminosity distance)
  - alpha_K: kinetic energy of scalar perturbations
  - alpha_B: kinetic mixing between metric and scalar perturbations
  - alpha_T: c_T^2 - 1 where c_T is gravitational wave speed (GW170817: |alpha_T| < 10^{-15})

Ground truth: Bellini & Sawicki, JCAP 1407 (2014) 050, Eqs 3.7-3.10;
              Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 5.1, Eqs 5.6-5.9.

## Implementation (src/scalar_tensor/alpha_params.jl)

### Types

  struct FRWBackground
      H::Any              # Hubble parameter (scalar expression or symbol :H)
      phi_dot::Any         # time derivative of background phi (symbol :phi_dot or value)
      phi_ddot::Any        # second time derivative (symbol :phi_ddot or value)
      X_bg::Any            # background kinetic term X_0 = (1/2) phi_dot^2
      scale_factor::Any    # a(t), typically symbol :a
  end

  struct BelliniSawickiAlphas
      alpha_M::Any         # scalar expression
      alpha_K::Any
      alpha_B::Any
      alpha_T::Any
      M_star_sq::Any       # effective Planck mass squared
      background::FRWBackground
  end

### API

  define_frw_background(; H=:H, phi_dot=:phi_dot, phi_ddot=:phi_ddot) -> FRWBackground
    Convenience constructor. Computes X_bg = (1//2) * phi_dot^2.

  compute_alphas(ht::HorndeskiTheory, bg::FRWBackground; registry) -> BelliniSawickiAlphas
    Evaluates the four alpha functions by:
    1. Evaluating all G_i and their derivatives at background (phi_0, X_0)
       -- these are just the registered scalar tensors Tensor(:G4_X, []) etc.
    2. Constructing the Expr-tree expressions for M_*^2, alpha_M, alpha_K, alpha_B, alpha_T
       using _sym_mul, _sym_add, _sym_div from src/action/quadratic_action.jl.
    3. Returning BelliniSawickiAlphas struct.

  compute_alphas_beyond(bht::BeyondHorndeskiTheory, bg::FRWBackground; registry) -> BelliniSawickiAlphas
    Extended version for beyond-Horndeski, adds alpha_H parameter:
    alpha_H = -(2X dot{phi})/(H M_*^2) (F_4 X + F_5 dot{phi} H)
    (Kobayashi Sec 5.2 for beyond-Horndeski modifications.)

### G-function Derivatives Needed

For alpha_K: G_{2,X}, G_{2,XX}, G_{3,X}, G_{3,XX}, G_{4,X}, G_{4,XX}, G_{4,XXX},
             G_{5,X}, G_{5,XX}, G_{5,XXX}
For alpha_B: G_{3,phi}, G_{3,phiX}, G_{3,XX}, G_{4,phi}, G_{4,phiX}, G_{4,X}, G_{4,XX},
             G_{5,phi}, G_{5,phiX}, G_{5,X}, G_{5,XX}
For alpha_T: G_{4,X}, G_{5,phi}, G_{5,X}
For M_*^2:  G_4, G_{4,X}, G_{5,phi}, G_{5,X}

All these must be registered by define_horndeski! (extend the registration list from TGR-ble.2).

## Tests (test/scalar_tensor/test_alpha_params.jl)

1. GR limit: all G_i derivatives = 0 except G_4 = M_Pl^2/2 -> all alphas = 0, M_*^2 = M_Pl^2.
2. Brans-Dicke: G_4 = phi/2 -> alpha_T = 0 (no tensor speed excess), alpha_M = dot{phi}/(H phi).
3. f(R) limit: alpha_T = 0 (GW speed = light speed), alpha_B = -alpha_M/2 (Kobayashi Sec 2.3).
4. Quintic galileon: G_5 = c_5 X^2/M^9 -> nonzero alpha_T (constrained by GW170817).
5. Positivity: alpha_K > 0 (ghost-free) is a separate stability check in TGR-ble.6.

File: src/scalar_tensor/alpha_params.jl (~100 LOC + ~50 LOC tests).
Ground truth: Bellini & Sawicki JCAP 1407 (2014) 050; Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 5.1.
title: xIST: Implement Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization

close_reason: Implemented with Bellini-Sawicki Eqs 4.1-4.5, stability verified
description:
Implement the quadratic action for cosmological perturbations in Horndeski theory on FRW background.

## Physics

On a spatially flat FRW background, the second-order (quadratic) action for linear perturbations
in Horndeski theory splits into decoupled tensor and scalar sectors (Kobayashi Sec 5.2):

### Tensor Sector (gravitational waves)

The tensor perturbation h_{ij}^{TT} (transverse-traceless) has quadratic action:

  S_T^{(2)} = (1/8) integral dt d^3x a^3 M_*^2 [dot{h}_{ij}^2 - (1+alpha_T)(c^2/a^2)(nabla h_{ij})^2]

This gives:
  - Graviton kinetic term: M_*^2 > 0 (no ghost) -> ghost-free condition
  - Graviton speed: c_T^2 = 1 + alpha_T -> gradient stability requires alpha_T > -1
  - GW170817 constraint: |alpha_T| < 10^{-15}

### Scalar Sector (curvature perturbation)

In unitary gauge (delta_phi = 0), the curvature perturbation zeta has:

  S_S^{(2)} = integral dt d^3x a^3 Q_S [dot{zeta}^2 - c_s^2 (nabla zeta)^2 / a^2]

where the kinetic coefficient and sound speed are:

  Q_S = (M_*^2/(c_T^2 H^2)) D                                            (Kobayashi Eq 5.22)
  c_s^2 = (M_*^2/(Q_S H)) [d(H Q_S/M_*^2)/dt] + alpha_T                 (Kobayashi Eq 5.23)

  D = alpha_K + (3/2) alpha_B^2                                           (Kobayashi Eq 5.15)

Stability conditions:
  - No scalar ghost: Q_S > 0  <=>  D > 0  <=>  alpha_K + (3/2) alpha_B^2 > 0
  - No gradient instability: c_s^2 > 0
  - No tensor ghost: M_*^2 > 0
  - No tensor gradient instability: c_T^2 > 0  <=>  alpha_T > -1

Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 5.2, Eqs 5.14-5.23;
              Bellini & Sawicki JCAP 1407 (2014) 050, Sec 3.

## Implementation (src/scalar_tensor/quadratic_action_st.jl)

### Types

  struct ScalarTensorQuadraticAction
      tensor_kinetic::Any       # (1/8) M_*^2 coefficient of dot{h}_{ij}^2
      tensor_gradient::Any      # -(1/8) M_*^2 (1+alpha_T) c^2/a^2 coefficient
      scalar_kinetic::Any       # Q_S coefficient of dot{zeta}^2
      scalar_gradient::Any      # -Q_S c_s^2 / a^2 coefficient
      alphas::BelliniSawickiAlphas
  end

  struct StabilityConditions
      no_tensor_ghost::Bool     # M_*^2 > 0
      no_tensor_gradient::Bool  # 1 + alpha_T > 0
      no_scalar_ghost::Bool     # Q_S > 0  <=>  D > 0
      no_scalar_gradient::Bool  # c_s^2 > 0
      c_T_sq::Any               # 1 + alpha_T
      c_s_sq::Any               # scalar sound speed squared
      Q_S::Any                  # scalar kinetic coefficient
  end

### API

  quadratic_action_horndeski(alphas::BelliniSawickiAlphas) -> ScalarTensorQuadraticAction
    Constructs the quadratic action from alpha parameters.
    Uses _sym_mul, _sym_add from src/action/quadratic_action.jl for Expr-tree arithmetic.

  stability_conditions(qa::ScalarTensorQuadraticAction) -> StabilityConditions
    Evaluates the four stability conditions.
    For symbolic parameters, returns the Expr-tree conditions.
    For numeric parameters, evaluates and returns Bool.

  check_stability(qa::ScalarTensorQuadraticAction, params::Dict{Symbol,<:Number}) -> StabilityConditions
    Numeric evaluation: substitute parameter values and check all four conditions.
    Uses sym_eval from src/action/quadratic_action.jl.

### Integration with Existing QuadraticForm

  to_quadratic_form(qa::ScalarTensorQuadraticAction) -> QuadraticForm
    Maps to the existing QuadraticForm type with fields = [:h_TT, :zeta].
    Matrix is 2x2 block-diagonal (tensor and scalar sectors decouple).
    Entries are momentum-dependent: M[1,1] = (1/8) M_*^2 (omega^2 - c_T^2 k^2),
                                     M[2,2] = Q_S (omega^2 - c_s^2 k^2).

## Tests (test/scalar_tensor/test_quadratic_action.jl)

1. GR limit (all alphas = 0): c_T^2 = 1, c_s^2 is undefined (no scalar DOF),
   tensor action = (M_Pl^2/8)(dot{h}^2 - (nabla h)^2). Q_S = 0 (no propagating scalar).
2. Quintessence (alpha_T = 0, alpha_B = 0, alpha_K > 0): c_T = 1 (GW speed = light),
   c_s^2 > 0 for standard kinetic term, Q_S ~ alpha_K.
3. Cubic galileon: nonzero alpha_B -> scalar-metric mixing generates effective c_s^2 != 1.
4. Stability violation: construct parameters where c_s^2 < 0, verify detection.
5. GW170817 consistency: alpha_T = 0 -> c_T = 1.

File: src/scalar_tensor/quadratic_action_st.jl (~120 LOC + ~60 LOC tests).
Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 5.2; Bellini & Sawicki Sec 3.
title: xIST: Implement quadratic action for scalar-tensor perturbations on FRW

description:
Validation task: verify that f(R) gravity maps correctly to a Horndeski subcase.

## Physics

f(R) gravity replaces the Einstein-Hilbert Lagrangian L = R with L = f(R), where f is an
arbitrary function of the Ricci scalar. This is equivalent to a scalar-tensor theory via
the conformal transformation / Legendre transform:

  phi = f'(R)     (scalar field is the derivative of f)
  X = 0           (phi has no kinetic term in the Jordan frame)

The Horndeski functions are (Kobayashi Sec 2.3):

  G_2(phi, X) = f(R(phi)) - R(phi) f'(R(phi))     [potential-like]
  G_3(phi, X) = 0                                   [no cubic galileon]
  G_4(phi, X) = f'(R(phi)) / 2 = phi / 2           [conformal coupling]
  G_5(phi, X) = 0                                   [no quintic coupling]

Key properties of the f(R) -> Horndeski mapping:
  - G_4 = phi/2, so G_{4,X} = 0: the second term in L_4 vanishes
  - G_{4,phi} = 1/2: nonzero, gives alpha_M != 0
  - L_total = (phi/2) R + f - R f' = f(R) (via Legendre transform identity)

Alpha parameters for f(R) (Kobayashi Sec 5.1):
  - alpha_T = 0 (gravitons travel at speed of light, since G_{4,X} = G_5 = 0)
  - alpha_B = -alpha_M / 2 (exact relation for f(R))
  - alpha_M = dot{F} / (H F) where F = f'(R) = phi
  - alpha_K = 0 at leading order (no kinetic term for phi)
  - M_*^2 = F = f'(R)

The scalar field equation reduces to the trace equation:
  3 Box(F) + 2f(R) - R F = T      (where F = f'(R) = phi, T = matter trace)
or equivalently:
  Box f'(R) - (1/3)(2f - f'R) = (1/3) T

Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 2.3, Eqs 2.20-2.23;
              Sotiriou & Faraoni, Rev. Mod. Phys. 82, 451 (2010), Sec III.

## Implementation (test/scalar_tensor/test_fR_validation.jl)

### Test 1: Lagrangian equivalence (~20 LOC)

  1. define_horndeski! with G_4 = phi/2, G_2 = f - Rf' (as abstract scalars), G_3 = G_5 = 0.
  2. Construct horndeski_lagrangian(ht).
  3. Verify L_3 = 0, L_5 = 0.
  4. Verify L_4 = (phi/2) * R (since G_{4,X} = 0, the Box-squared term vanishes).
  5. Verify L_2 = f - R*f' (as abstract scalar).
  6. Total L = (phi/2)*R + f - Rf' = f(R) after Legendre transform (verify structure).

### Test 2: Field equation (trace equation) (~30 LOC)

  1. Compute horndeski_scalar_eom(ht) for the f(R) Horndeski.
  2. Set up substitution rules: G_{4,phi} = 1/2, G_4 = phi/2, all other G_i derivatives = 0.
  3. Simplify the scalar EOM.
  4. Verify it matches: Box(phi) + (1/3)(R phi - 2 G_2) = 0 (vacuum, T=0).
     Since G_2 = f - Rf' and phi = f', this becomes Box(f') - (1/3)(2f - f'R) = 0.

### Test 3: Alpha parameters (~25 LOC)

  1. Compute compute_alphas(ht, frw_background).
  2. Set G_{4,X} = 0, G_5 = 0, G_{5,phi} = 0, G_{5,X} = 0.
  3. Verify alpha_T = 0 (exact: GW speed equals speed of light).
  4. Verify M_*^2 = 2 * G_4 = phi = f'(R).
  5. Verify alpha_B = -alpha_M / 2 (the defining f(R) relation).
  6. Verify alpha_K expression reduces to the known f(R) form.

### Test 4: Stability conditions (~15 LOC)

  1. For viable f(R) models (f'' > 0): check M_*^2 = f' > 0 (no tensor ghost).
  2. Check c_T^2 = 1 (no superluminal gravitons).
  3. Scalar sector: Q_S and c_s^2 in terms of f', f'', R.

## Dependencies

Requires: TGR-ble.2 (Lagrangian types), TGR-ble.3 (field equations), TGR-ble.5 (alpha params).

File: test/scalar_tensor/test_fR_validation.jl (~80 LOC).
Ground truth: Kobayashi arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 2.3; Sotiriou & Faraoni RMP 82, 451 (2010).
title: xIST: Validation — f(R) gravity as Horndeski subcase

description: Derive Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli master equations from first principles using the harmonic infrastructure from Epics 1-3. Schwarzschild 2+2 decomposition, RW gauge, RW equation (odd-parity), Zerilli equation (even-parity), master function extraction. STRING MATCH validation against known potentials. Unblocks 3 existing @test_skip benchmarks. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler, Phys. Rev. 108, 1063 (1957); Zerilli, Phys. Rev. D 2, 2141 (1970); Martel & Poisson Sec III-IV. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: Harmonics Epic 4: Regge-Wheeler / Zerilli Master Equations

description: Set up Schwarzschild background in 2+2 decomposition: ds^2 = -f(r)dt^2 + f(r)^{-1}dr^2 + r^2 d_Omega^2, where f(r) = 1 - 2M/r. Register the background metric as a 2D Lorentzian part (t,r) times S^2 with radius r. Use existing product_manifold infrastructure for M2 x S2 structure. Define tortoise coordinate r* via dr*/dr = 1/f(r). File: src/harmonics/schwarzschild.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Sec 2; Martel & Poisson Sec III. Test: verify Schwarzschild Ricci = 0 (vacuum). ~100 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement Schwarzschild background 2+2 decomposition

description: Implement the Regge-Wheeler gauge choice for odd-parity perturbations on Schwarzschild. Gauge conditions: h_{tr}^{odd} = h_{rr}^{odd} = h_{t}^{odd} = 0 (3 gauge freedoms used). This leaves a single odd-parity master variable. For even-parity, implement the RW gauge: h_{t,even}^a = 0, G_{lm} = 0 (removes 4 gauge freedoms). Implement as set_vanishing! rules applied after harmonic decomposition. File: src/harmonics/rw_gauge.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Eq 6; Martel & Poisson Sec III.C. Test: apply gauge to generic perturbation, verify correct DOF count. ~80 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement Regge-Wheeler gauge

description: Derive the Regge-Wheeler equation for odd-parity Schwarzschild perturbations: [-d^2/dt^2 + d^2/dr*^2 - V_RW(r)] psi_RW = 0, where V_RW(r) = (1 - 2M/r)[l(l+1)/r^2 - 6M/r^3]. Pipeline: (1) apply tensor harmonic decomposition to linearized Einstein equations, (2) apply RW gauge, (3) extract the single odd-parity master equation. STRING MATCH validation: the extracted potential must equal (1 - 2M/r)*(l*(l+1)/r^2 - 6*M/r^3). File: src/harmonics/regge_wheeler.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Eq 11; Martel & Poisson Eq 4.9. Test: string match V_RW against known form. ~120 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Derive Regge-Wheeler equation (odd-parity master)

description: Derive the Zerilli equation for even-parity Schwarzschild perturbations: [-d^2/dt^2 + d^2/dr*^2 - V_Z(r)] psi_Z = 0, where V_Z(r) = (1-2M/r)/(r^3 * (nr+3M)^2) * [2n^2(n+1)r^3 + 6n^2 M r^2 + 18nM^2 r + 18M^3] with n = (l-1)(l+2)/2. Pipeline: apply tensor harmonic decomposition to linearized Einstein, apply RW gauge for even sector, combine equations to single master variable psi_Z (Zerilli function). STRING MATCH validation against known V_Z. File: src/harmonics/zerilli.jl. Ground truth: Zerilli, Phys. Rev. D 2, 2141 (1970), Eq 11; Martel & Poisson Eq 4.22. ~120 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Derive Zerilli equation (even-parity master)

description: Implement extraction of Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli master functions (psi_RW, psi_Z) from the metric perturbation h_{ab} in harmonic decomposition. For odd-parity: psi_RW = (r/l(l+1)) * f(r) * (d/dr(h_1/r) - (1/r) h_0) where h_0, h_1 are odd-parity RW gauge coefficients. For even-parity: psi_Z = r/(n+1) * [K + (f/(nr+3M))(r H_1 - r^2 dK/dr*)] where K, H_1 are even-parity coefficients. Provide extract_master_functions(h_decomposed) -> (psi_RW, psi_Z). File: src/harmonics/master_functions.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eqs 4.7, 4.20. Test: extract from known perturbation. ~100 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement master function extraction from metric perturbation

description: Validation task: verify that the Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli potentials are isospectral (produce the same quasinormal mode spectrum). This is a nontrivial result: V_RW and V_Z look completely different but are related by a Darboux/supersymmetric transformation. Verify: (1) V_RW and V_Z satisfy the partner potential relation V_{+/-} = W^2 +/- dW/dr* for some superpotential W(r*), (2) for l=2, M=1, evaluate both potentials numerically at r=3M,4M,6M,10M and verify they differ but share the same spectrum. This unblocks the 3 existing @test_skip benchmarks in benchmarks/. Ground truth: Chandrasekhar, Mathematical Theory of Black Holes, Ch 4; Martel & Poisson Sec V. Test: verify superpotential relation algebraically. ~80 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Validation — isospectrality of V_RW and V_Z

description: Implement even-parity and odd-parity vector spherical harmonics on S^2. Even: Y^a_{lm} = D^a Y_{lm} (gradient type). Odd: X^a_{lm} = epsilon^a_b D^b Y_{lm}. Orthogonality relations and vector field decomposition. New files in src/harmonics/. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson, Phys. Rev. D 71, 104003 (2005), Sec II.C. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: Harmonics Epic 2: Vector Spherical Harmonics

description: Implement even-parity (polar) vector spherical harmonics: Y^a_{lm} = D^a Y_{lm}, where D^a is the covariant derivative on S^2. These are the gradient-type vector harmonics. New type EvenVectorHarmonic with (l, m) quantum numbers, l >= 1 (l=0 gives zero). Must carry a single S^2 index (angular). Properties: D_a Y^a_{lm} = -l(l+1) Y_{lm} (divergence). File: src/harmonics/vector_harmonics.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eq 2.5. Test: verify divergence relation. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement even-parity vector harmonics Y^a_{lm}

description: Implement odd-parity (axial) vector spherical harmonics: X^a_{lm} = epsilon^a_b D^b Y_{lm}, where epsilon^a_b is the Levi-Civita tensor on S^2. These are the curl-type vector harmonics. New type OddVectorHarmonic with (l, m), l >= 1. Properties: D_a X^a_{lm} = 0 (divergence-free), epsilon^{ab} D_a X_{b,lm} = l(l+1) Y_{lm}. File: src/harmonics/vector_harmonics.jl (same file as even). Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eq 2.6. Test: verify divergence-free property. ~80 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement odd-parity vector harmonics X^a_{lm}

description: Implement orthogonality integral relations for vector harmonics on S^2. Key relations: integral(Y^a_{lm} Y^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(X^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(Y^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = 0 (even-odd orthogonal). These become simplification rules for products integrated over S^2. File: src/harmonics/orthogonality.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Sec II.C. Test: verify all three orthogonality relations as RewriteRules. ~80 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement vector harmonic orthogonality relations

description: Implement decomposition of a vector field on S^2: V_a = sum_{l,m} [v^{even}_{lm}(t,r) Y^a_{lm} + v^{odd}_{lm}(t,r) X^a_{lm}]. Provide decompose_vector(V_expr, harmonics_config) that rewrites a vector TensorExpr carrying S^2 indices into a sum over (l,m) modes with even/odd radial coefficients. File: src/harmonics/decompose_vector.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Sec II.C. Test: decompose a generic vector V_a and verify even/odd mode separation. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement vector field harmonic decomposition

close_reason: Implemented with MP App A + Gleiser et al. ground truth, 86 tests
description:
Implement systematic angular integration of products of tensor spherical harmonics over S^2. At second order in BH perturbation theory, source terms contain products like Y^{AB}_{l1,m1} * Y_{CD,l2,m2} that must be projected onto a single harmonic mode Y_{l3,m3}. This requires angular integrals of the form:

  I^{l3,m3}_{l1,m1,l2,m2} = integral [T1^{..}_{l1,m1} * T2^{..}_{l2,m2}] * T3^{..}*_{l3,m3} dOmega

where T1, T2, T3 are scalar, vector, or tensor harmonics.

All such integrals reduce to combinations of Gaunt coefficients (triple scalar harmonic integrals). The key reductions are:
1. Scalar x Scalar -> Scalar: already implemented as `harmonic_product` in clebsch_gordan.jl
2. Vector x Scalar -> Vector: integral Y^A_{l1} Y_{l2} X^*_{A,l3} dOmega (reduces to Gaunt + CG coefficients)
3. Tensor x Scalar -> Tensor: integral Y^{AB}_{l1} Y_{l2} Z^*_{AB,l3} dOmega
4. Vector x Vector -> Scalar: integral Y^A_{l1} X_{A,l2} Y*_{l3} dOmega (angular momentum coupling)
5. Tensor x Tensor -> Scalar: integral Z^{AB}_{l1} X_{AB,l2} Y*_{l3} dOmega
6. Cross-parity products: even x odd integrals that couple even and odd sectors

Functions needed:
- `angular_integral(T1, T2, T3)` -> Float64 (generic triple product integral)
- `vector_gaunt(l1, l2, l3, m1, m2, m3, parity1, parity2)` -> Float64
- `tensor_gaunt(l1, l2, l3, m1, m2, m3, type1, type2)` -> Float64

These all reduce to sums of products of Wigner 3j symbols (already available via `wigner3j` in clebsch_gordan.jl) times normalization factors involving l(l+1) from the vector/tensor harmonic norms.

File: src/harmonics/angular_integrals.jl (~180 LOC)
Reference: Martel & Poisson, PRD 71, 104003 (2005), Sec II.D, Eqs 2.18-2.21; Brizuela, Martin-Garcia & Tiglio, PRD 80, 024021 (2009), Appendix A; Gleiser, Nicasio, Price & Pullin, Phys. Rep. 325, 41 (2000), Appendix B.
Test: (1) vector_gaunt reduces to standard Gaunt for divergence-free modes; (2) cross-parity integrals Y^A X^*_A vanish (confirmed by orthogonality.jl); (3) tensor_gaunt at l1=l2=2, l3=0 reproduces the known quadrupole coupling coefficient from Gleiser et al Eq B.12.
title: BH-Pert2: Implement angular integration engine for products of tensor spherical harmonics

description:
DESIGN DECISION: How the full NP formalism connects to existing xideal/ infrastructure.

Current state in src/xideal/:
  - weyl_scalars.jl: weyl_scalars(Weyl, l, n, m, mbar) -> (Psi0,...,Psi4)
    Operates on raw arrays + vectors, no Tetrad struct.
  - petrov_classify.jl: petrov_classify(Weyl, g) or petrov_classify(psi)
    Uses null_tetrad_from_metric internally to build NP tetrad.
  - petrov_invariants.jl: I, J, K, L, N invariants
  - segre.jl: Segre-Plebanski classification of Ricci

Design for NP integration:
1. Add Ricci NP scalars Phi_{ij} (i,j=0,1,2): 9 complex scalars from R_{ab} l^a n^b etc.
   Phi_{00} = (1/2) R_{ab} l^a l^b,  Phi_{22} = (1/2) R_{ab} n^a n^b, etc.
   Plus Lambda = R/24 (Ricci scalar contribution).
   API: ricci_scalars(Ricci::Matrix, l, n, m, mbar) -> NamedTuple
   Or: ricci_scalars(tetrad, Ricci) using Tetrad struct.

2. Overload weyl_scalars to accept Tetrad:
   weyl_scalars(Weyl::Array{4}, tetrad::Tetrad) -> extracts l,n,m,mbar from tetrad, delegates.

3. NP field equations (Stephani et al. Eq 7.21a-r, 18 equations):
   These are the full set of NP equations expressing Riemann components via spin coefficients.
   Implemented as: np_field_equations(tetrad) -> Dict{Symbol, expr} checking all 18 identities.
   This is a VALIDATION tool, not a solver -- verifies consistency of spin coefficients + curvature.

4. Goldberg-Sachs theorem: A vacuum spacetime is algebraically special (Petrov II/D/III/N)
   iff there exists a null geodesic shear-free congruence (kappa=0, sigma=0).
   API: is_geodesic_shear_free(tetrad) -> Bool (checks kappa=sigma=0 for the l vector).
   Links NP spin coefficients to Petrov classification.

File organization:
  - New: src/xideal/np_scalars.jl (Ricci NP scalars, NP equations)
  - Modified: src/xideal/weyl_scalars.jl (add Tetrad overload)
  - New: src/xideal/goldberg_sachs.jl (algebraic speciality check)

References: Newman & Penrose (1962); Chandrasekhar Ch 1 Sec 7-9; Stephani et al. Ch 7; Goldberg & Sachs, Acta Phys. Polon. 22, 13 (1962).
title: Design: NP formalism integration with existing Weyl scalar and Petrov infrastructure

description: Implement linearized perturbation theory for Hassan-Rosen bimetric gravity. Perturb both metrics about proportional backgrounds: g_{ab} = g_bar_{ab} + delta_g_{ab}, f_{ab} = c^2 g_bar_{ab} + delta_f_{ab} where c is the background ratio. The linearized field equations couple delta_g and delta_f through the mass term derived from the Hassan-Rosen interaction potential V(g^{-1}f) = sum_{n=0}^{4} beta_n e_n(sqrt(g^{-1}f)). Express the coupled equations as a matrix system acting on (delta_g, delta_f). ~120 LOC in src/perturbation/bimetric.jl. Reference: Torsello et al, Class. Quant. Grav. 37, 025013 (2020), arXiv:1904.10464, Sec 4; Hassan & Rosen, JHEP 1202, 126 (2012), arXiv:1109.3515, Sec 3. Test: setting beta_0 = beta_1 = beta_3 = beta_4 = 0 (only beta_2 nonzero, i.e., Fierz-Pauli mass term) gives a single massive spin-2 equation for the massive combination, matching Fierz & Pauli, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 173, 211 (1939).
title: Bimetric: Implement linearized Hassan-Rosen perturbation equations for two coupled metrics

description: Implement the spinor Ricci identity (commutator of spin covariant derivatives): [nabla_{AA'}, nabla_{BB'}] phi_C = epsilon_{A'B'} Psi_{ABCD} phi^D + epsilon_{AB} Psi_bar_{A'B'C'D'} phi^{D'_equiv} + ... (curvature terms). Full formula involves Weyl spinor, Ricci spinor, and Lambda. Function: commute_spin_covds(expr; registry) -> expr with commutator replaced by curvature spinors. ~100 LOC in src/spinors/spin_commutator.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eqs 4.9.2-4.9.13. Test: (1) [nabla_{AA'}, nabla_{BB'}] phi_C string-matches P&R Eq 4.9.2, (2) vacuum (Phi=Lambda=0): commutator reduces to Weyl spinor terms only, (3) conformally flat (Psi=0): commutator reduces to Ricci terms.
title: Spinors: Implement spinor commutator identities

description:
Implement automated tensor contraction engine for Feynman diagrams in gravitational EFT.

## Background

A Feynman diagram in the EFT of gravity consists of:
- Internal lines: graviton propagators D^{abcd}(q_i)
- Vertices: n-graviton vertices V^(n) and matter-graviton couplings
- External lines: on-shell graviton polarizations or matter sources

The contraction engine connects these building blocks by:
1. Identifying internal vs external index groups at each vertex
2. Contracting propagator indices D^{abcd} with vertex index slots
3. Imposing momentum conservation at each vertex
4. Summing over internal momenta (for loop diagrams, deferred to TGR-xex)
5. Producing the resulting tensor structure in external indices + momenta

Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Sec 4-5; Porto 1104.2712, Sec 3.

## Design: FeynmanDiagram type

```julia
# A single line (propagator) connecting two vertices
struct DiagramLine
    propagator::TensorExpr        # e.g., graviton_propagator(...)
    momentum::Symbol              # loop or external momentum label
    vertex_a::Int                 # index of first vertex
    vertex_b::Int                 # index of second vertex
    slot_a::Int                   # which index group at vertex_a
    slot_b::Int                   # which index group at vertex_b
end

# A vertex in the diagram
struct DiagramVertex
    vertex_expr::TensorExpr       # the vertex tensor expression
    n_slots::Int                  # number of index groups (legs)
    momenta::Vector{Symbol}       # momentum at each leg
    is_matter::Bool               # matter vertex vs pure graviton
end

# The complete diagram
struct FeynmanDiagram
    vertices::Vector{DiagramVertex}
    lines::Vector{DiagramLine}
    external_legs::Vector{@NamedTuple{vertex::Int, slot::Int, momentum::Symbol}}
    loop_momenta::Vector{Symbol}  # integration variables
end
```

## Integration with existing infrastructure

Must build on:
- `contract_metrics` from `src/algebra/contraction.jl` — the core index contraction engine
- `all_contractions` from `src/algebra/all_contractions.jl` — exhaustive contraction enumeration
- `simplify` pipeline from `src/algebra/simplify.jl` — fixed-point simplification
- `canonicalize` from `src/algebra/canonicalize.jl` — Butler-Portugal for index canonicalization
- `contract_momenta` from `src/action/kernel_extraction.jl` — k_a k^a -> k^2
- `ensure_no_dummy_clash` from `src/ast/indices.jl` — dummy index management

## Function signatures

```julia
# Build a diagram from vertices and connectivity
function build_diagram(vertices::Vector{DiagramVertex},
                       edges::Vector{Tuple{Int,Int,Int,Int}};
                       external_momenta::Vector{Symbol}=Symbol[]) -> FeynmanDiagram

# Contract all internal lines in a diagram
function contract_diagram(diagram::FeynmanDiagram;
                          registry::TensorRegistry=current_registry(),
                          simplify_intermediate::Bool=true) -> TensorExpr

# Contract a single propagator with two vertices
function contract_line(prop::TensorExpr, v1::TensorExpr, v2::TensorExpr,
                       idx_group_1::Vector{TIndex}, idx_group_2::Vector{TIndex};
                       registry=current_registry()) -> TensorExpr

# Impose momentum conservation at each vertex
function impose_momentum_conservation!(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> FeynmanDiagram

# Identify loop momenta from topology
function find_loop_momenta(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> Vector{Symbol}

# Symmetry factor of the diagram
function symmetry_factor(diagram::FeynmanDiagram) -> Rational{Int}
```

## Ground truth (verification tests)

1. **Tree-level graviton exchange**: Two matter vertices connected by one propagator (single internal line, no loops). Contraction must produce:
   M_tree = (kappa^2 / 2) T1^{ab} D_{abcd}(k) T2^{cd}
   = (kappa^2 / 4k^2) [2 T1^{ab} T2_{ab} - T1^a_a T2^b_b]
   Fourier transform gives Newton potential V = -G m1 m2 / r.
   Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156, Eq 30.

2. **Tree-level 2-to-2 graviton scattering**: s + t + u channel diagrams via 3-graviton vertex. Sum over channels must be gauge-invariant (k_a M^{abcd} = 0). Reference: DeWitt vertex Feynman rules.

3. **Triangle diagram topology**: 3 vertices, 3 internal lines, 1 loop momentum. Verify correct momentum routing: q flows in loop, k1, k2 external, with conservation at each vertex.

4. **Symmetry factors**: Self-energy diagram (bubble with 2 propagators) has symmetry factor 1/2. Tadpole has 1/1. Verify against Goldberger & Rothstein counting.

## Implementation notes

- The key challenge is dummy index management: each contraction introduces new dummy pairs. Use `ensure_no_dummy_clash` and `fresh_index` from the AST layer.
- Momentum routing: for L loops and E external legs, there are L independent loop momenta. Use momentum conservation to eliminate (V-1) momenta (V = number of vertices).
- For tree diagrams (L=0), the result is a rational function of external momenta.
- For loop diagrams (L>=1), the result is a MomentumIntegral object (see TGR-xex).
- ~120 LOC in src/feynman/contraction.jl
