close_reason: Implemented with Lee-Wald (1990) Eq 3.2 ground truth
description:
Implement the symplectic current (d-1)-form omega^mu[delta_1 phi, delta_2 phi].

## Physics (Iyer-Wald 1994, Eq 2.7; Lee-Wald 1990, Eq 2.3)

The symplectic current is the antisymmetrized second variation of the Lagrangian:

  omega^mu(phi; delta_1 phi, delta_2 phi) = delta_1 Theta^mu(phi, delta_2 phi)
                                            - delta_2 Theta^mu(phi, delta_1 phi)

Key properties:
- omega is bilinear and antisymmetric in (delta_1 phi, delta_2 phi)
- On-shell (when both variations satisfy linearized EOM): nabla_mu omega^mu = 0
- The symplectic form Omega = integral_Sigma omega^mu dSigma_mu is independent of
  the Cauchy surface Sigma (when on-shell)
- Omega defines the presymplectic structure on the covariant phase space

For EH gravity (Wald 1990, Eq 3.5):
  omega^mu = (1/16piG) P^{mu alpha beta gamma delta epsilon}
             (delta_2 g_{beta gamma} nabla_alpha delta_1 g_{delta epsilon}
              - delta_1 g_{beta gamma} nabla_alpha delta_2 g_{delta epsilon})

where P^{mu alpha beta gamma delta epsilon} = g^{mu epsilon} g^{alpha beta} g^{gamma delta}
- (1/2) g^{mu beta} g^{alpha epsilon} g^{gamma delta} - (1/2) g^{mu alpha} g^{beta gamma} g^{delta epsilon}
- (1/2) g^{beta gamma} g^{mu delta} g^{alpha epsilon} + (1/2) g^{beta gamma} g^{mu epsilon} g^{alpha delta}.

## Implementation (~80 LOC in src/phase_space/symplectic_current.jl)

### Data structures

```julia
struct SymplecticCurrent
    expr::TensorExpr        # omega^mu with one free upper index
    delta1_field::Symbol    # first variation
    delta2_field::Symbol    # second variation
    potential::SymplecticPotential
end
```

### Functions

```julia
symplectic_current(Theta::SymplecticPotential,
                   delta1::Symbol, delta2::Symbol) -> SymplecticCurrent
```

Algorithm:
1. Compute delta_1(Theta[delta_2]) by substituting delta_2 for the field variation
   in Theta, then varying the background fields with respect to delta_1
2. Compute delta_2(Theta[delta_1]) similarly
3. Return omega = step1 - step2

The variation delta_i(Theta) is computed using the existing perturbation infrastructure:
treat Theta as a functional of the background fields, and compute its first-order
perturbation with delta_i as the perturbation parameter.

```julia
# Verify on-shell conservation
verify_omega_conservation(omega::SymplecticCurrent; registry) -> Bool
```

## Ground truth

- Iyer & Wald (1994), PRD 50, 846, Eq 2.7
- Lee & Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 725, Eq 2.3
- Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378, Eq 3.5

## Acceptance criteria

1. omega[delta_1, delta_2] = -omega[delta_2, delta_1] (antisymmetry)
2. omega is bilinear: omega[alpha*delta_1, delta_2] = alpha * omega[delta_1, delta_2]
3. For EH: omega matches the known P-tensor formula above
4. nabla_mu omega^mu simplifies to zero when both variations satisfy linearized Einstein
5. ~8 tests
title: xCPS: Implement symplectic current ω

description:
Implement the Noether current (d-1)-form J^mu[xi] for diffeomorphisms.

## Physics (Iyer-Wald 1994, Eq 2.10-2.12; Wald 1990, Eq 2.6)

For a diffeomorphism generated by vector field xi, the Noether current is:

  J^mu = Theta^mu(phi, Lie_xi phi) - xi^mu L

where Lie_xi phi is the Lie derivative of all dynamical fields along xi.

Key identity (Iyer-Wald Eq 2.12):

  J^mu = E_phi * Lie_xi phi + nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}    (*)

This means J^mu = dQ on-shell (when E = 0). The on-shell conservation nabla_mu J^mu = 0
follows from diffeomorphism invariance of L.

For a Killing vector (Lie_xi g = 0), the EH Noether current simplifies to:

  J^mu = 2 G^{mu nu} xi_nu + 2 nabla_nu nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}

The first term vanishes on-shell (Einstein equations), leaving J = dQ.

For EH gravity specifically:
  J^mu = (1/16piG) [2 G^{mu nu} xi_nu + nabla_nu (nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu)]

## Implementation (~100 LOC in src/phase_space/noether_current.jl)

### Data structures

```julia
struct NoetherCurrent
    expr::TensorExpr        # J^mu with one free upper index
    xi::Symbol              # the vector field generating the diffeomorphism
    lagrangian::LagrangianDensity
    potential::SymplecticPotential
end
```

### Functions

```julia
noether_current(L::LagrangianDensity, field::Symbol, xi::Symbol) -> NoetherCurrent
```

Algorithm:
1. Compute Lie_xi(field) using existing lie_derivative infrastructure
2. Evaluate Theta^mu(phi, Lie_xi phi) by substituting Lie_xi phi for delta_phi in Theta
3. Compute xi^mu * L (contraction of xi with the Lagrangian volume form)
4. Return J^mu = Theta^mu(Lie_xi phi) - xi^mu L

```julia
# Convenience: known J for EH + Killing vector
noether_current_eh(metric::Symbol, xi::Symbol, covd::Symbol) -> TensorExpr
```

```julia
# Verify on-shell conservation
verify_noether_conservation(J::NoetherCurrent; registry) -> Bool
```

### Reuse from existing code

- lie_derivative(v, expr) (src/gr/lie.jl) -- Lie derivative of tensor expressions
- define_killing! (src/gr/killing.jl) -- for testing with Killing vectors
- interior_product(v, alpha) (src/exterior/operations.jl) -- xi contracted with L

## Ground truth

- Iyer & Wald (1994), PRD 50, 846, Eqs 2.10-2.12
- Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378, Eq 2.6
- Lee & Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 725, Sec III

## Acceptance criteria

1. For EH + Killing xi: J^mu = 2 G^{mu nu} xi_nu + 2 nabla_nu nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}
2. On-shell (G=0): J^mu reduces to pure divergence nabla_nu(nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu)
3. nabla_mu J^mu = 0 verified symbolically (off-shell identity for diffeo-covariant L)
4. For scalar field with Killing xi: J^mu = T^{mu nu} xi_nu (stress-energy contracted with xi)
5. ~8 tests

close_reason: Implemented with Iyer-Wald Eqs 3.5, 4.1 — first law + Wald entropy
description:
Implement the Noether charge (d-2)-form Q^{mu nu}[xi].

## Physics (Iyer-Wald 1994, Eqs 2.12, 3.1-3.2, 3.6)

The Noether charge Q^{mu nu} is defined by the decomposition:

  J^mu = E * Lie_xi phi + nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}

where J is the Noether current and E are the equations of motion. On-shell (E=0),
J^mu = nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}, so Q is the potential for the conserved current.

Q^{mu nu} is antisymmetric: Q^{mu nu} = -Q^{nu mu} (it is a (d-2)-form).

For the EH Lagrangian L = (1/16piG) R (Iyer-Wald Eq 3.6):

  Q^{mu nu} = -(1/16piG) nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}
             = -(1/16piG) (1/2)(nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu)

This is (up to normalization) the Komar integrand. Integrating over a (d-2)-sphere:

  H_xi = integral_{partial Sigma} Q^{mu nu} dS_{mu nu}

gives the Komar mass (for timelike Killing xi) or Komar angular momentum (for rotational xi).

For general diffeomorphism-covariant Lagrangians L(g, R, nabla R, ...),
Iyer-Wald Eq 3.2 gives:

  Q^{mu nu} = -E_R^{mu nu alpha beta} nabla_alpha xi_beta
              + 2 xi_beta nabla_alpha E_R^{mu nu alpha beta} + ...

where E_R^{mu nu alpha beta} = partial L / partial R_{mu nu alpha beta}.

## Implementation (~120 LOC in src/phase_space/noether_charge.jl)

### Data structures

```julia
struct NoetherCharge
    expr::TensorExpr        # Q^{mu nu} with two free antisymmetric upper indices
    xi::Symbol              # the diffeomorphism generator
    lagrangian::LagrangianDensity
    current::NoetherCurrent
end
```

### Functions

```julia
noether_charge(J::NoetherCurrent) -> NoetherCharge
```

Algorithm (potential extraction from divergence):
1. Given J^mu = E * Lie_xi phi + nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}, and knowing E and J
2. Compute J^mu - E * Lie_xi phi = nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}
3. Use the divergence potential extraction (TGR-s50.7/TGR-s50.8) to find Q^{mu nu}

```julia
# Direct construction for known Lagrangians
noether_charge_eh(metric::Symbol, xi::Symbol, covd::Symbol) -> TensorExpr
```

Returns -(1/16piG) nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]} directly.

```julia
# For general L(g, Riemann):
noether_charge_from_riemann_deriv(L::LagrangianDensity, xi::Symbol) -> TensorExpr
```

Uses the Iyer-Wald formula Q = -E_R nabla xi + 2 xi nabla E_R where
E_R = dL/dR_{abcd}.

```julia
# Komar integral
komar_integral(Q::NoetherCharge) -> TensorExpr
```

Returns the integrand Q^{mu nu} dS_{mu nu} for integration over (d-2)-surface.

## Ground truth

- Iyer & Wald (1994), PRD 50, 846, Eqs 2.12, 3.1-3.2, 3.6
- Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378, Sec 3
- Komar (1959), Phys. Rev. 113, 934

## Acceptance criteria

1. For EH: Q^{mu nu} = -(1/16piG) nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}
2. nabla_nu Q^{mu nu} = J^mu on-shell (round-trip consistency)
3. Q is antisymmetric: Q^{mu nu} = -Q^{nu mu}
4. For Gauss-Bonnet L = R + alpha G: Q has additional terms from dL/dR_{abcd}
5. ~10 tests
title: xCPS: Implement Noether charge Q

description:
Implement divergence detection: determine if a vector density T^mu can be written as nabla_nu S^{mu nu}.

## Physics

A key step in the covariant phase space formalism is extracting the Noether charge Q^{mu nu}
from the identity J^mu = E * Lie_xi phi + nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}. This requires recognizing that
J^mu - E * Lie_xi phi is a total divergence, and finding the antisymmetric potential.

More generally, given a (d-1)-form J, we need to determine if J = dQ for some (d-2)-form Q
(i.e., J is exact). In tensor language: is T^mu = nabla_nu S^{mu nu} for some antisymmetric S?

This is related to the algebraic Poincare lemma: on a contractible space, closed forms are exact.
In practice, for tensor expressions built from curvature and covariant derivatives, we can use
algebraic methods rather than solving PDEs.

## Implementation (~100 LOC in src/phase_space/divergence_detect.jl)

### Functions

```julia
is_divergence(expr::TensorExpr, div_index::TIndex; registry) -> Bool
```

Determines if expr can be written as nabla_{div_index} S^{...} for some S antisymmetric
in div_index and the free index of expr.

Algorithm:
1. Expand all derivatives via expand_derivatives
2. Check if every term in the expanded sum has the form nabla_a(...) where a = div_index
3. For terms with nested derivatives, use IBP identities to attempt to write as divergence
4. If all terms can be written as divergences, return true

```julia
extract_divergence_terms(expr::TensorExpr, div_index::TIndex; registry) -> (divergence_part, remainder)
```

Split expr into a part that is a divergence and a remainder.

```julia
is_exact_form(expr::TensorExpr, degree::Int; registry) -> Bool
```

For a p-form, check if it is exact (i.e., expr = d(something)).

### Key identities used

1. nabla_a nabla_b V^c - nabla_b nabla_a V^c = R^c_{dab} V^d (Ricci identity)
   -> nabla_a(nabla_b V^c) is NOT simply a divergence due to Riemann terms
2. nabla_[a nabla_b] xi^c = (1/2) R^c_{dab} xi^d (for any vector)
3. nabla_a(nabla^[a xi^b]) is a total divergence by construction

### Edge cases

- Exact expressions like nabla_a(T^{ab}) are trivially divergences
- Expressions involving Lie_xi phi for Killing xi simplify via Killing equation
- Terms like R^{mu nu} xi_nu are NOT divergences in general

## Ground truth

- Margalef-Bentabol & Villasenor (2021), PRD 103, 025011, Sec III (CPS algorithm)
- Anderson (1992), "The variational bicomplex" (mathematical foundations)

## Acceptance criteria

1. is_divergence(nabla_nu(nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu), down(:nu)) = true
2. is_divergence(G^{mu nu} xi_nu, some_index) = false (Einstein tensor term is not a divergence)
3. extract_divergence_terms correctly separates J^mu_{EH} into G*xi + div(Q) parts
4. Handles products of divergences (Leibniz rule)
5. ~8 tests
title: xCPS: Implement divergence detection utility

close_reason: Implemented with Iyer-Wald Eq 3.3, Komar 2-form verified
description:
Implement potential extraction: given nabla_nu T^{mu nu ...} = S^{mu ...}, find T.

## Physics

This is the inverse problem to taking a divergence. Given a vector density J^mu that is
known to be a total divergence (from is_divergence), find the antisymmetric tensor
Q^{mu nu} such that J^mu = nabla_nu Q^{mu nu}.

This is NOT unique: Q + curl(anything) also works. The Iyer-Wald formalism fixes the
ambiguity by using a specific algorithm based on the structure of the Lagrangian.

For diffeomorphism-covariant Lagrangians L(g, R, nabla R, ...), the Noether charge
has a universal form (Iyer-Wald Eq 3.2):

  Q^{ab} = W^{abcd} nabla_c xi_d + X^{abc} xi_c + nabla_c Z^{abc}

where:
  W^{abcd} = -partial L / partial R_{abcd}  (Euler-Lagrange derivative w.r.t. Riemann)
  X^{abc} depends on derivatives of partial L / partial (nabla...R)
  Z^{abc} handles higher-derivative ambiguities

For EH: W^{abcd} = -(1/32piG)(g^{ac}g^{bd} - g^{ad}g^{bc}), giving
  Q^{ab} = -(1/16piG) nabla^{[a} xi^{b]}

## Implementation (~100 LOC in src/phase_space/potential_extract.jl)

### Functions

```julia
extract_potential(expr::TensorExpr, div_index::TIndex; registry) -> Union{TensorExpr, Nothing}
```

Given expr = nabla_{div_index} Q^{...}, find Q.

Algorithm for gravity Lagrangians (Iyer-Wald method):
1. Compute E_R^{abcd} = dL/dR_{abcd} (variation of L w.r.t. Riemann tensor)
2. Q^{ab} = -E_R^{abcd} nabla_c xi_d (leading term)
3. Add correction terms from higher-derivative contributions
4. Verify round-trip: nabla_b Q^{ab} should equal the input

```julia
# Compute dL/dR_{abcd} for a Lagrangian
riemann_derivative(L::LagrangianDensity) -> TensorExpr
```

For L = f(R): dL/dR_{abcd} = f_prime(R) * g^{a[c}g^{d]b} / 2
For L = R_{ab}R^{ab}: dL/dR_{abcd} = g^{a[c}R^{d]b} + g^{b[d}R^{c]a}
For L = R_{abcd}R^{abcd}: dL/dR_{abcd} = 2 R^{abcd}

```julia
# Direct Iyer-Wald formula for Q from Lagrangian
noether_charge_iw(L::LagrangianDensity, xi::Symbol) -> TensorExpr
```

Implements the full Iyer-Wald Eq 3.2 directly from the Lagrangian structure.

### Reuse from existing code

- variational_derivative (for computing dL/dR)
- contract_curvature (src/algebra/contraction.jl) -- simplify curvature products
- canonicalize (src/algebra/canonicalize.jl) -- canonical form for comparison

## Ground truth

- Iyer & Wald (1994), PRD 50, 846, Eqs 3.1-3.5
- Jacobson, Kang & Myers (1994), PRD 49, 6587 (higher-derivative corrections)

## Acceptance criteria

1. extract_potential applied to nabla_b(nabla^a xi^b - nabla^b xi^a) returns nabla^[a xi^b]
2. Round-trip: nabla_b(extract_potential(J, b)) simplifies to J for EH
3. riemann_derivative(L_EH) = (1/32piG)(g^{ac}g^{bd} - g^{ad}g^{bc})
4. For L = alpha R^2: Q^{ab} includes 2 alpha R nabla^{[a} xi^{b]} term
5. For L = beta R_{cd}R^{cd}: Q^{ab} includes terms with R^{a}_{c} nabla^{[b} xi^{c]}
6. ~10 tests
title: xCPS: Implement potential extraction (antiderivative of divergence)

description:
Validate the full CPS pipeline for Einstein-Hilbert gravity: L -> Theta -> J -> Q = Komar.

## Physics

This is the end-to-end validation of the covariant phase space pipeline for the simplest
gravitational case: the Einstein-Hilbert Lagrangian L = (1/16piG) R.

The expected results at each stage are (all well-known, e.g., Wald 1990 Sec 3):

1. EOM: E^{mu nu} = (1/16piG) G^{mu nu} (Einstein tensor)

2. Symplectic potential (Iyer-Wald Eq 3.3):
   Theta^mu = (1/16piG) g^{alpha beta}(nabla^mu delta_g_{alpha beta} - nabla_alpha delta_g^{mu}_{beta})

3. Noether current for Killing vector xi (substitute Lie_xi g = 0):
   J^mu = (1/16piG) [2 G^{mu nu} xi_nu + nabla_nu(nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu)]

   Note: Lie_xi g_{ab} = nabla_a xi_b + nabla_b xi_a = 0 for Killing vectors.
   So Theta^mu(Lie_xi g) picks up terms from nabla_c(nabla_a xi_b + nabla_b xi_a) = 0.
   The nontrivial contribution comes from the general formula, not from Lie_xi g itself.

4. Noether charge:
   Q^{mu nu} = -(1/16piG) nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}
             = -(1/32piG) (nabla^mu xi^nu - nabla^nu xi^mu)

5. Komar integral: for a timelike Killing field xi = partial_t at infinity,
   M = -(1/8piG) integral_{S^2} nabla^mu xi^nu dS_{mu nu}
   This is twice the Noether charge integral (the factor of 2 is the famous Komar factor).

## Implementation (~80 LOC in test/test_phase_space_eh.jl)

### Test structure

```julia
@testset "CPS Pipeline: Einstein-Hilbert" begin
    # Setup
    reg = TensorRegistry()
    @manifold M4 dim=4 metric=g
    @covd D on=M4 metric=g
    define_curvature_tensors!(reg, :M4, :g)
    define_killing!(reg, :xi; manifold=:M4, metric=:g, covd=:D)

    L_EH = LagrangianDensity(Tensor(:RicScalar, TIndex[]), [:g], :g, :D, 4)

    # Test 1: EOM extraction
    E, Theta = eom_extract(L_EH, :g)
    @test simplify(E - Tensor(:Ein, [up(:a), up(:b)]); registry=reg) == ZERO

    # Test 2: Symplectic potential matches known formula
    Theta_known = theta_eh(:g, :delta_g, :D)
    @test simplify(Theta.expr - Theta_known; registry=reg) == ZERO

    # Test 3: Noether current
    J = noether_current(L_EH, :g, :xi)
    # On-shell (G=0): J should be pure divergence
    # ...

    # Test 4: Noether charge = Komar integrand
    Q = noether_charge(J)
    Q_komar = noether_charge_eh(:g, :xi, :D)
    @test simplify(Q.expr - Q_komar; registry=reg) == ZERO

    # Test 5: Round-trip J = nabla_nu Q^{mu nu} on-shell
    # ...
end
```

## Ground truth

- Wald (1990), J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378, Sec 3 (complete EH calculation)
- Komar (1959), Phys. Rev. 113, 934 (Komar integral)
- Iyer & Wald (1994), PRD 50, 846, Sec III (explicit formulas)

## Acceptance criteria

1. Full pipeline L -> E -> Theta -> J -> Q runs without error for EH
2. E matches Einstein tensor G^{mu nu}
3. Theta matches known EH symplectic potential
4. Q matches Komar integrand: -(1/16piG) nabla^{[mu} xi^{nu]}
5. On-shell round-trip: nabla_nu Q^{mu nu} = J^mu when G = 0
6. All simplifications converge within 100 iterations
7. ~12 tests
title: xCPS Validation: EH Noether charge = Komar integral

Independent metric g_{ab} and connection Gamma^a_{bc} (NOT Levi-Civita). Torsion T^a_{bc}, non-metricity Q_{abc}, distortion decomposition, metric-affine Riemann tensor, irreducible decomposition via Brauer algebra (11 pieces in d=4), Poincare gauge theory action. New directory: src/metric_affine/. Ground truth: Hehl, McCrea, Mielke, Ne'eman, Phys. Rep. 258, 1 (1995). ~1000 LOC total across 10 sub-issues.

description: Design how to represent independent metric g_{ab} and connection Gamma^a_{bc} (NOT Levi-Civita) in TensorGR.jl. Key challenge: existing covd infrastructure assumes metric compatibility. Decomposition: Gamma = Gamma_LC + N where Gamma_LC is Levi-Civita and N is distortion. Torsion: T^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{[bc]} (antisymmetric part). Non-metricity: Q_{abc} = -nabla_a g_{bc} (failure of metric compatibility). Design decisions: (1) how to register a general connection (new covd type vs flag), (2) how torsion/non-metricity interact with existing curvature pipeline, (3) storage of irreducible decomposition data. Deliverable: design document. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2. ~0 LOC (design only).
design:
# Metric-Affine Gravity Framework Design for TensorGR.jl

## 1. Overview

This design specifies how to extend TensorGR.jl to support metric-affine gravity (MAG), where the connection Gamma^a_{bc} is independent of the metric g_{ab}. The general connection has 64 independent components in d=4 (vs 40 for Levi-Civita), and is decomposed as:

    Gamma^a_{bc} = {^a_{bc}} + N^a_{bc}

where {^a_{bc}} is the Levi-Civita part and N^a_{bc} is the distortion tensor. The distortion further decomposes as N = K + L (contorsion + disformation).

Reference: Hehl, McCrea, Mielke, Ne'eman, Phys. Rep. 258, 1-171 (1995), hereafter HMMN95.

## 2. New Source Directory

    src/metric_affine/
        connection.jl      # AffineConnectionProperties, define_independent_connection!
        torsion.jl         # torsion_tensor, irreducible decomposition
        nonmetricity.jl    # nonmetricity_tensor, Weyl vector, irreducible decomposition
        distortion.jl      # contorsion, disformation, decompose_connection
        curvature.jl       # ma_riemann, ma_ricci, ma_ricci_scalar
        brauer.jl          # 11-piece irreducible decomposition of R^a_{bcd}
        pgt_action.jl      # Poincare gauge theory Lagrangian
        special_cases.jl   # Einstein-Cartan, teleparallel, symmetric teleparallel limits

## 3. Core Data Structure: AffineConnectionProperties

### 3.1 New struct (in connection.jl)

    struct AffineConnectionProperties
        name::Symbol                    # connection name, e.g. :Gamma
        manifold::Symbol                # e.g. :M4
        metric::Symbol                  # e.g. :g (independent, but needed for decomposition)
        lc_christoffel::Symbol          # name of the Levi-Civita part, e.g. :Gamma_LC
        distortion::Symbol              # name of distortion tensor N^a_{bc}
        torsion::Symbol                 # name of torsion tensor T^a_{bc}
        nonmetricity::Symbol            # name of non-metricity tensor Q_{abc}
        contorsion::Symbol              # name of contorsion tensor K^a_{bc}
        disformation::Symbol            # name of disformation tensor L^a_{bc}
        has_torsion::Bool               # true if torsion is allowed (T != 0)
        has_nonmetricity::Bool          # true if non-metricity is allowed (Q != 0)
        covd_name::Symbol               # name of the associated covariant derivative
    end

### 3.2 Registry integration

We do NOT add a new field to TensorRegistry. Instead, AffineConnectionProperties is stored in the tensor registry's options dict, just like CovDProperties:

    reg.tensors[:Gamma_aff] => TensorProperties(
        name=:Gamma_aff, manifold=:M4, rank=(0,0),
        options=Dict(:is_affine_connection => true,
                     :affine_props => AffineConnectionProperties(...))
    )

A new boolean field is_affine_connection is added to TensorProperties (hot-path flag, following the pattern of is_covd and is_christoffel). This avoids modifying TensorRegistry while still supporting fast lookup.

### 3.3 Relationship to existing CovDProperties

The existing CovDProperties struct has torsion_free::Bool and metric_compatible::Bool fields, but these are only used as flags; the actual torsion tensor is registered independently. The MAG framework extends this by:

1. AffineConnectionProperties wraps a richer set of tensors.
2. define_independent_connection! internally calls define_covd! to set up the covariant derivative, passing torsion_free and metric_compatible flags from the MAG configuration.
3. The CovD created by define_independent_connection! is a THIN WRAPPER: it delegates to the existing covd_to_christoffel infrastructure but uses the general connection symbol (no lower-index symmetry unless torsion_free=true).

This means: all existing simplify pipeline, commute_covds, sort_covds, change_covd infrastructure continues to work with the MAG connection, because it only cares about the CovD name and Christoffel symbol name.

## 4. API: define_independent_connection!

### 4.1 Signature

    define_independent_connection!(reg::TensorRegistry, name::Symbol;
        manifold::Symbol,
        metric::Symbol,
        torsion::Bool=true,
        nonmetricity::Bool=true,
        covd_name::Union{Symbol,Nothing}=nothing)

### 4.2 What it registers (step by step)

1. **General connection tensor Gamma^a_{bc}** (rank (1,2), NO symmetry in lower indices):
   - Name: `name` (e.g. :Gamma)
   - Symmetries: [] (empty - not symmetric in bc in general)
   - Options: :is_affine_connection => true

2. **Levi-Civita Christoffel** (rank (1,2), Symmetric(2,3)):
   - Name: Symbol(name, :_LC) (e.g. :Gamma_LC)
   - Standard Levi-Civita Christoffel, registered via the existing define_covd! mechanism
   - Options: :is_christoffel => true

3. **Distortion tensor N^a_{bc}** (rank (1,2), no symmetry):
   - Name: Symbol(:N_, name) (e.g. :N_Gamma)
   - Definition: N^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{bc} - {^a_{bc}}
   - No assumed symmetry

4. **If torsion=true:**
   a. **Torsion tensor T^a_{bc}** (rank (1,2), AntiSymmetric(2,3)):
      - Name: Symbol(:T_, name) (e.g. :T_Gamma)
      - Definition: T^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma^a_{cb}
      - Symmetry: AntiSymmetric(2,3)

   b. **Contorsion tensor K^a_{bc}** (rank (1,2), no index symmetry BUT K_{abc} = -K_{bac}):
      - Name: Symbol(:K_, name)
      - K^a_{bc} = (1/2)(T^a_{bc} + T_b^a_c + T_c^a_b)
      - Symmetry on K_{(lowered)}: AntiSymmetric on first two lowered indices

   c. **Torsion irreducible pieces** (registered as separate tensors):
      - :Tvec_<name> (torsion vector, rank (0,1)): T_a = T^b_{ba}
      - :Taxial_<name> (axial vector, rank (1,0)): S^a = (1/6) eps^{abcd} T_{bcd}
      - :Ttensor_<name> (tensor part, rank (1,2)): q^a_{bc} (traceless, no axial part)

5. **If nonmetricity=true:**
   a. **Non-metricity tensor Q_{abc}** (rank (0,3), Symmetric(2,3)):
      - Name: Symbol(:Q_, name)
      - Definition: Q_{abc} = -nabla_a g_{bc} (using LC connection internally)
      - Symmetry: Symmetric(2,3) (from symmetry of g_{bc})

   b. **Disformation tensor L^a_{bc}** (rank (1,2), Symmetric(2,3)):
      - Name: Symbol(:L_, name)
      - L^a_{bc} = (1/2)(Q^a_{bc} - Q_b^a_c - Q_c^a_b)
      - Symmetry: Symmetric(2,3) (L_{abc} = L_{acb})

   c. **Non-metricity irreducible pieces** (registered as separate tensors):
      - :Qweyl_<name> (Weyl vector, rank (0,1)): Q_a = g^{bc} Q_{abc}
      - :Qhat_<name> (second trace, rank (0,1)): Q-hat_a = Q_{ab}^b
      - :Qsegmental_<name> (segmental/Weyl part, rank (0,3)): (1/d) g_{bc} Q_a
      - :Qtensor_<name> (traceless tensor part, rank (0,3))

6. **Covariant derivative** (via existing define_covd!):
   - Name: covd_name or Symbol(:nabla_, name)
   - torsion_free = !torsion
   - metric_compatible = !nonmetricity
   - The CovD's Christoffel symbol is set to `name` (the full connection)

7. **Rewrite rules:**
   - Non-metricity rule: nabla_a g_{bc} = -Q_{abc} (replaces the metric-compatibility rule)
   - Distortion decomposition: N = K + L
   - Connection decomposition: Gamma = Gamma_LC + N

8. **AffineConnectionProperties** stored in registry via the tensor options dict.

### 4.3 Naming convention

All derived tensors use a suffix from the connection name to avoid clashes when multiple independent connections are defined on the same manifold:

    Connection :Gamma  =>  :Gamma_LC, :N_Gamma, :T_Gamma, :K_Gamma, :Q_Gamma, :L_Gamma

## 5. Key Functions

### 5.1 Torsion (torsion.jl)

    # Build torsion from connection:  T^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma^a_{cb}
    torsion_tensor(conn_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Torsion vector (trace):  T_a = T^b_{ba}
    torsion_vector(torsion_name::Symbol, a::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Axial torsion (pseudo-trace):  S^a = (1/6) eps^{abcd} T_{bcd}
    torsion_axial(torsion_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, epsilon::Symbol; dim::Int=4) -> TensorExpr

    # Tensor part (traceless, axial-free remainder)
    torsion_tensor_part(torsion_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                        dim::Int=4) -> TensorExpr

    # Full irreducible decomposition: T = (1/3)(T_b delta^a_c - T_c delta^a_b)
    #   + (1/3) eps^a_{bcd} S^d + q^a_{bc}
    torsion_irreducible(torsion_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                        dim::Int=4) -> TensorExpr

Implementation strategy: these are pure expression builders returning TensorExpr ASTs. They compose from the registered tensor names. The irreducible decomposition is only valid in the dimension registered for the manifold; the function reads the dimension from the registry.

### 5.2 Non-metricity (nonmetricity.jl)

    # Build non-metricity from connection and metric:
    # Q_{abc} = partial_a g_{bc} - Gamma^d_{ab} g_{dc} - Gamma^d_{ac} g_{db}
    # (equivalently, Q_{abc} = -nabla_a(Gamma) g_{bc})
    nonmetricity_tensor(conn_name::Symbol, metric::Symbol,
                        a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Weyl vector:  Q_a = g^{bc} Q_{abc}
    weyl_vector(Q_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, metric::Symbol) -> TensorExpr

    # Second trace:  tilde_Q_c = Q_{ab}^{b}  (via g^{ab})
    nonmetricity_second_trace(Q_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, metric::Symbol) -> TensorExpr

    # Irreducible decomposition (HMMN95 Eq. 2.4.3):
    # Q_{abc} = (1/d) g_{bc} Q_a
    #         + (2/(d^2 - 4)) [g_{a(b} Lambda_{c)} - (1/d) g_{bc} Lambda_a]
    #         + Omega_{abc}
    # where Lambda_a = tilde_Q_a - (1/d) Q_a
    nonmetricity_irreducible(Q_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                              metric::Symbol=:g, dim::Int=4) -> TensorExpr

### 5.3 Distortion (distortion.jl)

    # Contorsion from torsion (HMMN95 Eq. 2.8):
    # K^a_{bc} = (1/2)(T^a_{bc} + T_b^a_c + T_c^a_b)
    # Note: T_b^a_c = g^{ad} g_{be} T^e_{dc}
    contorsion_tensor(torsion_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                      metric::Symbol=:g) -> TensorExpr

    # Disformation from non-metricity (HMMN95 Eq. 2.8):
    # L^a_{bc} = (1/2)(Q^a_{bc} - Q_b^a_c - Q_c^a_b)
    disformation_tensor(Q_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                        metric::Symbol=:g) -> TensorExpr

    # Full decomposition: Gamma = LC + K + L
    decompose_connection(conn_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex;
                         metric::Symbol=:g) -> TensorExpr

### 5.4 Curvature (curvature.jl)

    # Riemann tensor for general connection:
    # R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = d_c Gamma^a_{bd} - d_d Gamma^a_{bc}
    #                   + Gamma^a_{ce} Gamma^e_{bd} - Gamma^a_{de} Gamma^e_{bc}
    ma_riemann_expr(conn_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex, c::TIndex, d::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Decomposition into LC curvature + distortion contributions:
    # R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = R^a_{bcd}(LC) + nabla_c(LC) N^a_{bd} - nabla_d(LC) N^a_{bc}
    #                  + N^a_{ce} N^e_{bd} - N^a_{de} N^e_{bc}
    ma_riemann_decomposed(conn_name::Symbol, a, b, c, d) -> TensorExpr

    # Ricci tensor (asymmetric in general):
    # R_{ab}(Gamma) = R^c_{acb}(Gamma)
    # Note: NOT symmetric when torsion or non-metricity are present
    ma_ricci_expr(conn_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Homothetic curvature (trace of Riemann on first pair):
    # F_{ab} = R^c_{cab}(Gamma)
    # Vanishes iff connection preserves volume form; related to Weyl vector
    ma_homothetic_curvature(conn_name::Symbol, a::TIndex, b::TIndex) -> TensorExpr

    # Ricci scalar:
    # R(Gamma) = g^{ab} R_{ab}(Gamma)
    ma_ricci_scalar_expr(conn_name::Symbol, metric::Symbol) -> TensorExpr

### 5.5 Curvature tensor registration

The MA Riemann tensor must be registered with REDUCED symmetries compared to the Riemannian case. In MAG:
- R^a_{b[cd]} holds (antisymmetric in last pair) - ALWAYS
- R_{[ab]cd} does NOT hold in general (only with metric compatibility)
- R_{abcd} = R_{cdab} does NOT hold in general

Registration:
    register_tensor!(reg, TensorProperties(
        name = :Riem_<conn>,
        manifold = manifold,
        rank = (0, 4),   # or (1, 3) depending on convention
        symmetries = [AntiSymmetric(3, 4)],  # ONLY last-pair antisymmetry
        options = Dict(:is_ma_riemann => true, :connection => conn_name)
    ))

For the Ricci tensor:
    register_tensor!(reg, TensorProperties(
        name = :Ric_<conn>,
        manifold = manifold,
        rank = (0, 2),
        symmetries = [],  # NOT symmetric in general
        options = Dict(:is_ma_ricci => true, :connection => conn_name)
    ))

## 6. Integration with Existing Infrastructure

### 6.1 CovD engine (covd.jl)

The existing covd_to_christoffel function works unmodified: it expands nabla_a T^b = partial_a T^b + Gamma^b_{ac} T^c using whatever Christoffel symbol is registered for the CovD. For MAG, the CovD's Christoffel is the full connection Gamma (not Gamma_LC), so the expansion automatically uses the general connection.

Key insight: the covd_to_christoffel mechanism is connection-agnostic. It does not assume Symmetric(2,3). The symmetry is encoded in the tensor registration, and canonicalize handles it.

### 6.2 change_covd (covd.jl)

The existing change_covd(expr, from, to) inserts difference tensors (Gamma_1 - Gamma_2). For MAG, this naturally handles:
- Changing from a general connection's CovD to the LC CovD: difference = N (distortion)
- Changing between two general connections: difference = N_1 - N_2

### 6.3 commute_covds (sort_covds.jl)

The existing _commutator_term generates Riemann terms: [nabla_a, nabla_b] V^c = R^c_{dab} V^d. For MAG:
- The commutator becomes: [nabla_a, nabla_b] V^c = R^c_{dab} V^c + T^d_{ab} nabla_d V^c
- The TORSION TERM is new: when torsion is present, the commutator includes a T * nabla term.

This requires EXTENDING _commutator_term to check if the CovD has torsion, and if so, add the torsion contribution. This is the main modification to existing code.

Proposed change to sort_covds.jl:

    function _commutator_term(a, b, tensor, covd, reg)
        # ... existing Riemann terms ...
        result = <existing Riemann computation>

        # Add torsion term if connection has torsion
        covd_props = get_covd(reg, covd)
        if !covd_props.torsion_free
            torsion_name = Symbol(:T, covd)
            # [nabla_a, nabla_b] V^c += T^d_{ab} nabla_d V^c
            dummy = fresh_index(used)
            torsion = Tensor(torsion_name, [up(dummy), a, b])
            deriv_term = TDeriv(down(dummy), tensor, covd)
            result = result + torsion * deriv_term
        end
        return result
    end

This is the ONLY required change to existing source code in src/. All other MAG code goes into the new src/metric_affine/ directory.

### 6.4 Metric compatibility rule

The existing define_covd! registers a rule: nabla_a g_{bc} = 0 when metric_compatible=true.
For MAG with non-metricity, we instead register: nabla_a g_{bc} = -Q_{abc}.

This is handled by define_independent_connection!, which either:
- (a) Calls define_covd! with metric_compatible=false (skipping the zero rule), then registers its own non-metricity rule, OR
- (b) Calls define_covd! with metric_compatible=true to get the LC CovD, and separately registers the general connection's CovD.

Option (a) is cleaner: one CovD per connection, with the appropriate rules.

### 6.5 Simplify pipeline

The simplify pipeline (expand_products -> contract_metrics -> contract_curvature -> canonicalize -> collect_terms -> apply_rules) does NOT need modification. The MAG curvature tensors have different symmetries (only AntiSymmetric(3,4)), so canonicalize will use the xperm machinery with the correct (reduced) symmetry group. The contract_curvature step will need extension to handle :Riem_<conn> -> :Ric_<conn> contractions, but this is done by registering additional rules, not modifying the pipeline.

### 6.6 Perturbation engine

The perturbation engine (expand.jl) explicitly constructs delta_christoffel using the Levi-Civita formula. For MAG perturbation theory (Palatini variation), the connection is varied independently. This requires:
- A new MetricAffinePerurbation struct (future work, not part of this epic)
- delta_connection is independent of delta_metric (no formula, just a new perturbation tensor)
- delta_torsion and delta_nonmetricity follow from delta_connection

This is a natural extension but is beyond the scope of the current epic (10 issues).

## 7. Special Cases

### 7.1 Einstein-Cartan (Q=0, T != 0)

    define_independent_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g,
                                   torsion=true, nonmetricity=false)

Effects:
- Q_{abc} is registered and set_vanishing!
- Disformation L = 0
- N = K (distortion = contorsion only)
- Gamma = LC + K
- Ricci tensor is NOT symmetric: Ric_{ab} != Ric_{ba}
- Metric compatibility holds: nabla_a g_{bc} = 0

### 7.2 Teleparallel (R(Gamma) = 0, T != 0)

    define_independent_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g,
                                   torsion=true, nonmetricity=false)
    # Then set the full curvature to zero:
    set_vanishing!(reg, :Riem_Gamma)

This represents the Weitzenbock connection. The gravitational dynamics is encoded entirely in torsion (f(T) gravity).

### 7.3 Symmetric teleparallel (R = 0, T = 0, Q != 0)

    define_independent_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g,
                                   torsion=false, nonmetricity=true)
    set_vanishing!(reg, :Riem_Gamma)

Gravitational dynamics encoded in non-metricity (f(Q) gravity).

### 7.4 GR recovery (T = 0, Q = 0)

    define_independent_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g,
                                   torsion=false, nonmetricity=false)

This should be equivalent to define_covd! with torsion_free=true, metric_compatible=true. The distortion, contorsion, and disformation are all set_vanishing!, and Gamma = Gamma_LC. A validation test (TGR-swh.9) must verify that all curvature expressions agree with the standard GR pipeline.

## 8. Brauer Algebra Decomposition (11 pieces, d=4)

The full Riemann tensor R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) in MAG has only the antisymmetry R^a_{b[cd]}. In d=4, this gives n*(n^2-1)/2 * n = 4*15/2 * 4 = ... Actually: R^a_{bcd} with a,b in {1..4} and [cd] antisymmetric gives 4*4*6 = 96 independent components.

Under GL(4,R) these decompose into 11 irreducible pieces, split into:
- Pair-antisymmetric part (R_{[ab]cd}): like the Riemannian sector
  - W1: Weyl-like (traceless, pair-symmetric part of the antisymmetric sector)
  - Ric1: Ricci-like trace
  - R1: Scalar curvature part
  - Z1, X1: additional pieces from the antisymmetric sector
  - R2: pseudo-scalar

- Pair-symmetric part (R_{(ab)cd}): purely non-Riemannian
  - W2: Traceless symmetric part
  - Ric2: Trace part
  - R3: Scalar part
  - Z2, X2: additional pieces

The implementation strategy:
1. Register projectors as rewrite rules or expression builders
2. Each projector is a linear combination of index symmetrizers/antisymmetrizers and trace operations
3. Follow Helpin & Volkov arXiv:2407.18019 for the explicit projector formulas

## 9. Poincare Gauge Theory Action

The most general PGT Lagrangian quadratic in curvature and torsion:

    L_PGT = -(1/2kappa) R + a_0 Lambda
            + a_1 T^(1)_{abc} T^(1)abc + a_2 T^(2)_{abc} T^(2)abc + a_3 T^(3)_{abc} T^(3)abc
            + b_1 R^(1)_{abcd} R^(1)abcd + ... + b_6 R^(6)_{abcd} R^(6)abcd

Implemented as:

    struct PGTParams
        kappa::Rational{Int}     # gravitational coupling
        Lambda::Rational{Int}    # cosmological constant
        a::NTuple{3, Symbol}     # torsion coupling constants (symbolic)
        b::NTuple{6, Symbol}     # curvature coupling constants (symbolic)
    end

    pgt_lagrangian(params::PGTParams; connection::Symbol, metric::Symbol) -> TensorExpr

The field equations are obtained by:
1. Variation w.r.t. metric -> Einstein-like equation (symmetric, 10 components)
2. Variation w.r.t. connection -> Cartan-like equation (64 components)

These use the existing variational_derivative infrastructure from src/perturbation/variation.jl, extended to vary w.r.t. connection.

## 10. Testing Strategy

### Test file: test/test_metric_affine.jl

1. **Registration tests**: define_independent_connection! creates all expected tensors with correct symmetries
2. **GR limit** (TGR-swh.9): T=0, Q=0 => all MAG quantities reduce to GR
3. **Einstein-Cartan** (TGR-swh.10): Q=0 => metric compatibility, Gamma = LC + K
4. **Algebraic identities**:
   - T^a_{bc} = -T^a_{cb} (antisymmetry)
   - Q_{abc} = Q_{acb} (symmetry from metric symmetry)
   - K_{abc} = -K_{bac} (contorsion antisymmetry)
   - L_{abc} = L_{acb} (disformation symmetry)
   - N = K + L (distortion decomposition)
   - Gamma = LC + K + L (connection decomposition)
5. **Irreducible decomposition completeness**:
   - Sum of torsion irreducible pieces = T (for given dimension)
   - Sum of non-metricity irreducible pieces = Q
   - Sum of 11 Brauer pieces = R(Gamma)
6. **Torsion contributions to commutator**:
   - [nabla_a, nabla_b] V^c = R^c_{dab} V^d + T^d_{ab} nabla_d V^c

## 11. Estimated Scope

- connection.jl: ~100 LOC (registration, properties struct)
- torsion.jl: ~100 LOC (tensor + 3 irreducible pieces + reconstruction)
- nonmetricity.jl: ~100 LOC (tensor + 4 irreducible pieces + reconstruction)
- distortion.jl: ~80 LOC (contorsion, disformation, decomposition)
- curvature.jl: ~120 LOC (MA Riemann, Ricci, scalar, homothetic; decomposition)
- brauer.jl: ~120 LOC (11 projectors)
- pgt_action.jl: ~100 LOC (Lagrangian, field equations)
- special_cases.jl: ~50 LOC (convenience constructors for EC, teleparallel, etc.)
- sort_covds.jl modification: ~15 LOC (torsion term in commutator)
- test/test_metric_affine.jl: ~200 LOC

Total: ~985 LOC (within the ~1000 LOC budget)

## 12. Non-goals (explicitly out of scope)

- Palatini variation of the connection (requires extending the perturbation engine)
- Spin connection / vierbein formalism (partially covered by src/tetrads/)
- Projective invariance / Weyl rescaling in MAG
- Higher-dimensional Brauer decomposition (d != 4)
- Propagating torsion modes (particle spectrum analysis for PGT)
- Non-metricity in component computation pipeline (src/components/)
title: Research: Design metric-affine framework for TensorGR

description: Verify Einstein-Cartan theory: metric compatible (Q=0) with torsion. Tests: (1) Non-metricity vanishes: Q_{abc} = nabla_a g_{bc} = 0, (2) Distortion = contortion only: N^a_{bc} = K^a_{bc}, (3) Connection: Gamma^a_{bc} = Gamma_LC^a_{bc} + K^a_{bc}, (4) Field equations: G_{ab} = 8 pi G T_{ab} (Einstein) and T^a_{bc} + delta^a_b T^d_{dc} - delta^a_c T^d_{db} = 8 pi G S^a_{bc} (Cartan, where S is spin tensor), (5) Torsion is algebraically determined by spin (no propagating torsion). Verify that in vacuum (S=0), torsion vanishes and GR is recovered. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 4; Trautman, arXiv:gr-qc/0606062. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Einstein-Cartan theory (Q=0, T!=0)

description: Implement general affine connection Gamma^a_{bc} without metric compatibility or torsion-free assumptions. API: define_affine_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g) that registers a connection tensor with 3 indices (1 up, 2 down) and NO assumed symmetry in lower indices (unlike Levi-Civita which has Gamma^a_{(bc)}). The connection defines a covariant derivative: nabla_a V^b = partial_a V^b + Gamma^b_{ac} V^c. Must also register Levi-Civita part Gamma_LC and distortion N = Gamma - Gamma_LC as derived tensors. New file: src/metric_affine/connection.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement general affine connection

description: Implement torsion tensor T^a_{bc} = 2 Gamma^a_{[bc]} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma^a_{cb} (antisymmetric in lower indices). Irreducible decomposition (d=4): T^a_{bc} = (1/3)(T_b delta^a_c - T_c delta^a_b) + (1/3)epsilon^a_{bcd} S^d + q^a_{bc} where T_b = T^a_{ba} is the torsion vector trace, S^a = (1/6)epsilon^{abcd}T_{bcd} is the axial (pseudo-trace) vector, and q^a_{bc} is the tensor part (traceless, no axial part). Implement: (1) torsion_tensor(connection) -> TensorExpr, (2) torsion_vector(T), torsion_axial(T), torsion_tensor_part(T). New file: src/metric_affine/torsion.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.3. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement torsion tensor with irreducible decomposition

description: Implement non-metricity tensor Q_{abc} = -nabla_a g_{bc} (symmetric in bc). Measures failure of metric compatibility. Irreducible decomposition (d=4): Q_{abc} = (1/4)g_{bc}Q_a + (1/d)g_{a(b}tilde_Q_{c)} + (2/(d-1)(d+2))(g_{a(b}Lambda_{c)} - g_{bc}Lambda_a) + Omega_{abc} where Q_a = g^{bc}Q_{abc} is the Weyl vector (trace), tilde_Q_a is the second trace, Lambda_a is mixed, and Omega_{abc} is the traceless tensor part. Implement: (1) nonmetricity_tensor(connection, metric) -> TensorExpr, (2) weyl_vector(Q), nonmetricity_traceless(Q). New file: src/metric_affine/nonmetricity.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.4. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement non-metricity tensor with irreducible decomposition

description: Implement distortion tensor N^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma_LC^a_{bc} decomposed as N^a_{bc} = K^a_{bc} + L^a_{bc} where K^a_{bc} is contortion (from torsion) and L^a_{bc} is disformation (from non-metricity). Contortion: K^a_{bc} = (1/2)(T^a_{bc} + T_{b}^{a}_{c} + T_{c}^{a}_{b}) with K_{abc} = -K_{bac}. Disformation: L^a_{bc} = (1/2)(Q^a_{bc} - Q_{b}^{a}_{c} - Q_{c}^{a}_{b}) with L_{abc} = L_{acb}. Key identity: N = K + L (complete decomposition). Implement: contortion(T), disformation(Q), distortion(connection). New file: src/metric_affine/distortion.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Eq 2.8; Schouten 'Ricci Calculus'. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement distortion tensor decomposition

description: Implement Riemann tensor R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) for general affine connection Gamma. Definition: R^a_{bcd} = partial_c Gamma^a_{bd} - partial_d Gamma^a_{bc} + Gamma^a_{ce}Gamma^e_{bd} - Gamma^a_{de}Gamma^e_{bc}. Decompose into Riemannian part + torsion/non-metricity contributions: R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = R^a_{bcd}(LC) + nabla_c N^a_{bd} - nabla_d N^a_{bc} + N^a_{ce}N^e_{bd} - N^a_{de}N^e_{bc}. Key difference from Riemannian case: R_{abcd} != -R_{abdc} still holds, but R_{abcd} != R_{cdab} in general (only with metric compatibility). Implement: ma_riemann(connection), ma_ricci(connection), ma_ricci_scalar(connection). New file: src/metric_affine/curvature.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 3. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement metric-affine Riemann tensor

description: Implement irreducible decomposition of the metric-affine Riemann tensor R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) into 11 irreducible pieces in d=4, following the Brauer algebra approach of xBrauer. In metric-affine gravity, R_{abcd} has fewer symmetries than Riemannian R_{abcd}: only R_{ab[cd]} (antisymmetric in last pair). This gives 6*16 = 96 independent components (vs 20 for Riemannian). The 11 pieces correspond to irreducible representations of GL(4,R): 6 from the antisymmetric part (like Weyl, Ricci, scalar) and 5 from the non-symmetric part. Implement: brauer_decompose(R_ma) -> Dict{Symbol, TensorExpr} mapping piece names to projections. New file: src/metric_affine/brauer.jl. Ground truth: Helpin & Volkov, arXiv:2407.18019; McCrea, Class. Quant. Grav. 9, 553 (1992). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement irreducible decomposition via Brauer algebra (11 pieces)

description: Implement the most general Poincare gauge theory (PGT) action quadratic in curvature and torsion: L = -(1/2kappa)R + a_0 Lambda + Sigma_{i=1}^{3} a_i T^(i)_{abc} T^{(i)abc} + Sigma_{j=1}^{6} b_j R^(j)_{abcd} R^{(j)abcd} where T^(i) are the 3 irreducible torsion pieces and R^(j) are the 6 irreducible curvature pieces (from the parity-even sector). This gives 10 free coupling constants. Implement: pgt_lagrangian(params; connection=:Gamma, metric=:g) -> TensorExpr. Derive field equations by variation w.r.t. metric (Einstein-like) and connection (Cartan-like). New file: src/metric_affine/pgt_action.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 5; Blagojevic 'Gravitation and Gauge Symmetries' Ch 5. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Poincare gauge theory action

description: Verify that when torsion T=0 and non-metricity Q=0, the metric-affine framework reduces to standard GR. Tests: (1) Setting T^a_{bc}=0 and Q_{abc}=0 forces Gamma = Gamma_LC (Levi-Civita), (2) Distortion N=0, contortion K=0, disformation L=0, (3) R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = R^a_{bcd}(LC) (standard Riemannian curvature), (4) PGT field equations reduce to Einstein equations G_{ab} + Lambda g_{ab} = 0, (5) All irreducible pieces of Brauer decomposition match standard Weyl/Ricci decomposition. STRING MATCH against existing TensorGR curvature results. New test file: test/test_metric_affine.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.1. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Levi-Civita limit (T=0, Q=0 recovers GR)

description: Generalize the Horndeski Lagrangian from single scalar phi to N scalar fields phi^I (I=1..N) with multi-field kinetic matrix X^{IJ} = -1/2 nabla_a phi^I nabla^a phi^J. The Lagrangian becomes G_2(phi^I, X^{IJ}), G_3(phi^I, X^{IJ}) Box phi^I, etc. with internal field-space indices I,J contracted with a field-space metric G_{IJ}(phi). Must extend the existing Horndeski infrastructure to handle field-space index contractions alongside spacetime indices. ~100 LOC in src/scalar_tensor/multi_scalar.jl. Reference: Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 6; Ohashi et al, JCAP 1512 (2015) 009, arXiv:1507.04344. Test: N=1 reduction correctly recovers single-field Horndeski with G_2(phi, X), matching Kobayashi Sec 2.
title: Multi-scalar Horndeski: Generalize to N scalar fields with multi-field kinetic matrix

description:
Implement the assembly of radial source terms S^{(2)}_{lm}(r) for the second-order Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli equations. After angular decomposition and mode coupling, the second-order Einstein equations reduce to radial ODEs with source terms that are bilinear in first-order radial functions.

For each target mode (l,m), the source has the form:

  S^{(2)}_{lm}(r) = sum_{l1,m1,l2,m2} C^{lm}_{l1 m1, l2 m2} * F(r; psi^{(1)}_{l1}, psi^{(1)}_{l2})

where F(r; ...) is a differential expression involving the first-order master functions and their r-derivatives, multiplied by Schwarzschild background functions f = 1-2M/r and powers of r.

Infrastructure needed:
- `SecondOrderSource` struct: holds (l,m), parity, and a vector of source contributions from each (l1,l2) pair
- `SourceContribution` struct: holds (l1,m1,l2,m2), coupling coefficient C, and a symbolic radial expression F(r)
- `assemble_source(mp, target_l, target_m, target_parity, lmax; coupling_table)` -> `SecondOrderSource`
- The radial expressions F(r) must be assembled from:
  (a) Products of first-order radial functions (H_0, K, h_0, etc.)
  (b) Their r-derivatives (d/dr, d^2/dr^2)
  (c) Background factors: f(r) = 1-2M/r, 1/r^n
- Use existing `expand_perturbation(expr, mp, 2)` to get the abstract second-order expression, then project onto harmonics

The even-parity source involves products of even x even AND odd x odd first-order modes (both contribute to even l+l1+l2 parity). The odd-parity source involves even x odd products.

File: src/perturbation/bh_source_assembly.jl (~200 LOC)
Reference: Brizuela, Martin-Garcia & Tiglio, PRD 80, 024021 (2009), Sec 3, Eqs 3.1-3.8; Gleiser, Nicasio, Price & Pullin, Phys. Rep. 325, 41 (2000), Sec 5.
Test: (1) Source vanishes when first-order perturbation is zero. (2) For l=0 (monopole), only l1=l2 terms contribute (m1=-m2 forced by m=0). (3) Source term count at lmax=2 matches Brizuela et al Table II.
title: BH-Pert2: Implement radial source term assembly from mode-coupled second-order Einstein equations

description: Validation suite verifying Riemann invariant simplification against published identities. Tests: Gauss-Bonnet, degree-2 independence (3 in d=4), degree-3 independence (8 in d=4), Weyl completeness. References: Lovelock (1971); Fulling et al, Class. Quantum Grav. 9 (1992) 1151; Harvey, J. Math. Phys. 36 (1995) 356.
title: Invar Epic 6: Validation (Published Identity Tests)

description: Implement test verifying the Gauss-Bonnet identity: E_4 = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 4R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2 is a total derivative in d=4. Using riemann_simplify with dim=4, the expression E_4 should simplify to zero (modulo total derivatives) or be recognized as the Euler density. This is the most fundamental DDI at degree 2. Test implementation: (1) construct E_4 as TensorExpr, (2) call riemann_simplify(E_4; dim=4), (3) verify result. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. Ground truth: Lovelock, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 12 (1971) 385, Eq. (3.3); also Fulling et al (1992). String match: the simplified form should be zero or euler_density(:M4). ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Gauss-Bonnet identity test in d=4

description: Implement test verifying exactly 3 independent quadratic Riemann invariants in d=4. The 3 independent invariants are: R^2, R_{ab}R^{ab}, and R_{abcd}R^{abcd} (Kretschner scalar). Test: (1) enumerate all distinct degree-2 Riemann monomials (there are 6 before cyclic/DDI reduction), (2) apply riemann_simplify to each, (3) verify they all reduce to linear combinations of the 3 independent invariants, (4) verify the 3 independent ones are genuinely independent (no relation among them). Ground truth: Fulling et al, Class. Quantum Grav. 9 (1992) 1151, Table 1. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Degree-2 independence test (3 invariants in d=4)

description: Implement test verifying exactly 8 independent cubic Riemann invariants in d=4. The independent set includes: R^3, R*R_{ab}R^{ab}, R*R_{abcd}R^{abcd}, R_{a}^{b}R_{b}^{c}R_{c}^{a}, R_{ab}R_{cd}R^{acbd}, R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ef}R_{ef}^{ab}, R_{a}^{bcd}R_{bce}^{f}R_{df}^{ae} (or equivalent bases), and one more. Test: (1) enumerate all distinct degree-3 monomials, (2) apply riemann_simplify to each, (3) verify all reduce to linear combinations of 8 independent ones, (4) verify no relation among the 8. Ground truth: Fulling et al, Class. Quantum Grav. 9 (1992) 1151, Table 2. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Degree-3 independence test (8 invariants in d=4)

description: Implement test verifying the Weyl tensor completeness relation: C_{abcd}C^{abcd} = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 2R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2/3 in d=4 (where d=4 is used for the 1/3 coefficient; in general d it is 2/((d-1)(d-2))). This is a standard identity relating the Weyl scalar squared to Kretschner, Ricci squared, and Ricci scalar squared. Test: (1) construct LHS - RHS, (2) call riemann_simplify, (3) verify result is zero. Also test via Weyl decomposition: weyl_to_riemann then simplify. Ground truth: standard GR identity, also Fulling et al (1992). Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. String match: simplified(LHS - RHS) == TScalar(0). ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Weyl completeness identity test in d=4

description:
Design how to represent tensor n-point functions (propagators, vertices) symbolically in TensorGR.jl for the EFT of post-Newtonian gravity (Goldberger & Rothstein hep-th/0409156; Porto 1104.2712).

## Research findings

### Decision 1: Introduce new FeynmanDiagram type hierarchy (not extend KineticKernel)

KineticKernel is designed for bilinear decomposition of quadratic actions (2-point functions only). The n-point infrastructure needs:
- TensorVertex: n-point tensor structure with grouped indices (one pair per leg) and per-leg momenta
- TensorPropagator: 2-index-group object with momentum and gauge parameter
- FeynmanDiagram: graph of vertices connected by propagators, with topology, loop momenta, symmetry factors
- DiagramAmplitude: result of diagram contraction (external indices + tensor expression + loop integrals)

Rationale: the index grouping structure (pairs of Lorentz indices per graviton leg) has no analog in KineticKernel, which tracks individual bilinear terms. The graph topology (vertices, edges, loops) is fundamentally different from the flat list of bilinear terms.

### Decision 2: Momentum routing — all-incoming convention

At each n-point vertex, all momenta are incoming: k_1 + k_2 + ... + k_n = 0. For internal lines (propagators), the momentum is determined by momentum conservation at the vertices. Loop momenta are the L = E - V + 1 independent integration variables (E = edges, V = vertices). This matches the convention in Goldberger & Rothstein and is compatible with the existing to_fourier infrastructure.

### Decision 3: Index contraction via existing contract_metrics engine

The core contraction operation (propagator D^{abcd} contracted with vertex index slots) maps directly to the existing contract_metrics engine in src/algebra/contraction.jl. The key addition is:
- A "connect" operation that identifies index groups between vertices and propagators, renames indices to create dummy pairs, then calls contract_metrics
- ensure_no_dummy_clash from src/ast/indices.jl is critical for combining multiple vertices/propagators without index collisions
- fresh_index generates unique dummy names for each contraction

### Decision 4: Interface with Fourier/momentum infrastructure

- Vertices are generated via expand_perturbation (order n) followed by to_fourier — reusing the existing perturbation engine
- Momentum factors k_a in the vertex expression are Tensor(:k, [down(:a)]) — the same representation used by to_fourier
- contract_momenta (k_a k^a -> k^2) from src/action/kernel_extraction.jl handles momentum contractions
- For loop integrals: introduce MomentumIntegral type that wraps the tensor numerator and propagator denominators

### Decision 5: Gauge fixing as separate layer

The gauge-fixing action and Faddeev-Popov ghosts are separated from the pure GR vertex rules:
- gauge_fixing_action produces the gauge-fixing Lagrangian term
- ghost_propagator and ghost-graviton vertices are separate TensorPropagator/TensorVertex objects
- The gauge parameter xi flows through the propagator expression
- This allows easy switching between gauges (de Donder, Landau, background field)

## Decomposition into implementation issues

7 issues created with dependency chain:

Layer 0 (this issue - design): TGR-ub6
Layer 1 (foundations):
  TGR-ec6 — Feynman diagram type hierarchy and builder API (~120 LOC, src/feynman/types.jl)
  TGR-4ic — Gauge-fixing action and Faddeev-Popov ghost sector (~80 LOC, src/feynman/gauge_fixing.jl)
Layer 2 (physics):
  TGR-vzx — Graviton propagator in harmonic gauge (~80 LOC, src/feynman/propagator.jl)
  TGR-qks — 3-point and 4-point graviton vertices (~150 LOC, src/feynman/vertices.jl)
  TGR-9vn — Matter-graviton coupling vertices (~100 LOC, src/feynman/matter_vertices.jl)
Layer 3 (automation):
  TGR-d42 — Tensor contraction engine for diagrams (~120 LOC, src/feynman/contraction.jl)
Layer 4 (loops):
  TGR-xex — Loop integral representation with dim-reg (~100 LOC, src/feynman/loop_integrals.jl)
Downstream (not part of this design issue):
  TGR-ar7 — PN potential extraction via Fourier transform
  TGR-v8u — Validation: reproduce 1PN Einstein-Infeld-Hoffmann potential

Total: ~650 LOC in new src/feynman/ directory, plus ~200 LOC tests.

## Key references

- Goldberger & Rothstein, PRD 73 (2006) 104029, hep-th/0409156 — EFT of NRGR
- Porto, Phys. Rept. 633 (2016) 1-104, 1104.2712 — EFTofPNG review
- Sannan, PRD 34 (1986) 1749 — explicit 3-graviton vertex
- DeWitt, Phys. Rev. 162 (1967) 1195 — gravity Feynman rules
- Passarino & Veltman, Nucl. Phys. B 160 (1979) 151 — PV reduction
- Levi & Steinhoff, JHEP 06 (2015) 059, 1510.06752 — spin-gravity coupling
title: Research: Design EFT Feynman diagram infrastructure for gravitational n-point functions

Create examples/28_boundaries_ghy.jl demonstrating GHY boundary term for EH action on manifold with boundary.
title: Add tests and example for submanifolds and boundaries

description: Implement the spin covariant derivative nabla_{AA'} acting on spinor fields: nabla_{AA'} phi_B = sigma^a_{AA'} nabla_a phi_B. Must handle: (1) Leibniz rule on spinor products, (2) metric compatibility: nabla_{AA'} epsilon_{BC} = 0, (3) commutation with complex conjugation. The spin covd carries one undotted + one dotted index. Integrate with existing TDeriv infrastructure. Function: spin_covd(expr, undotted_idx, dotted_idx; registry). ~100 LOC in src/spinors/spin_covd.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eq 4.4.1. Test: (1) nabla_{AA'} epsilon_{BC} = 0, (2) Leibniz: nabla_{AA'}(phi_B psi_C) = (nabla_{AA'} phi_B) psi_C + phi_B (nabla_{AA'} psi_C), (3) relation to tensor covd via soldering form.
title: Spinors: Implement spin covariant derivative nabla_{AA'}

Higher-level than current simplify(). Depends on Epic: DDIs. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4. 3 sub-issues, ~100 LOC each.

description: Implement comprehensive curvature simplification combining all available tools: canonicalize + contract_metrics + contract_curvature + Bianchi identity rules + DDIs (from Epic TGR-xlu) + collect_terms + user rules. Higher-level than current simplify(). Key differences from simplify(): (1) automatically applies DDIs when dimension is known, (2) uses Bianchi identities more aggressively, (3) attempts multiple simplification strategies and picks shortest result, (4) supports curvature basis choice (Riemann vs Weyl decomposition). API: full_simplify(expr; registry=reg, basis=:riemann, use_ddis=true) -> TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/full_simplify.jl.

Algorithm: (1) identify terms with covariant derivatives, (2) use integration by parts (existing ibp.jl) to move derivatives onto fewer factors, (3) recognize divergence patterns nabla_a(...^a...), (4) collect divergence terms. This is useful for conservation laws and boundary term identification. API: sort_covds_to_div(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4.2. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.

description: Enhance covariant derivative sorting to convert to Box operator form more aggressively than current. Current box() in src/gr/box.jl creates Box = g^{ab}nabla_a nabla_b, but sort_covds does not attempt to produce this form. Enhancement: (1) detect g^{ab}nabla_a nabla_b patterns after commuting derivatives, (2) replace with Box operator, (3) handle multiple Box applications (Box^2, etc.), (4) recognize grad_squared patterns nabla_a phi nabla^a phi. API: sort_covds_to_box(expr, covd_name; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 4.3. ~100 LOC in src/gr/sort_covds.jl.
